Name: Aarakocra Aeromancer
Actions:
Multiattack. The aarakocra makes two Wind Staff attacks, and it can use Spellcasting to cast Gust of Wind.

Spellcasting. The aarakocra casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13): At will: Elementalism, Gust of Wind, Mage Hand, Message; 1/day: Lightning Bolt

Wind Staff. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Bludgeoning damage plus 11 (2d10) Lightning damage.
Reactions: Feather Fall (1/Day). The aarakocra casts Feather Fall in response to that spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Aarakocra aeromancers are elemental spellcasters who have mastered the power of the air. They are often found in mountainous regions or on the Elemental Plane of Air, where they serve as guardians of ancient secrets and protectors of their kin. Aeromancers use their control over the wind to strike from a distance and manipulate the battlefield, making them formidable opponents for adventurers who dare to challenge them.

Name: Aarakocra Skirmisher
Actions:
Talons. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Slashing damage, or 9 (3d4 + 2) Slashing damage if the aarakocra moved 30+ feet straight toward the target immediately before the hit.

Wind Javelin. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage plus 2 (1d4) Thunder damage. Hit or Miss: The javelin magically returns to the aarakocra's hand immediately after a ranged attack.
Description: Aarakocra skirmishers are agile and swift warriors who excel in hit-and-run tactics. They are often found in mountainous regions or on the Elemental Plane of Air, where they use their flying abilities to outmaneuver their foes. Skirmishers rely on their speed and precision to strike quickly and retreat before their enemies can retaliate, making them a challenging adversary for adventurers who encounter them.

Name: Aberrant Cultist
Actions:
Multiattack. The cultist makes two Tentacle Lash attacks. It can replace any attack with a use of Mind Rot.

Spellcasting. The cultist casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15): At will: Detect Thoughts, Minor Illusion

Tentacle Lash. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) Slashing damage plus 14 (4d6) Psychic damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 14) from one of two tentacles, and it has the Restrained condition until the grapple ends.

Mind Rot. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 15, one creature the cultist can see within 90 feet. Failure: 27 (6d8) Psychic damage, and the target has the Poisoned condition until the start of the cultist's next turn. Success: Half damage only.
Reactions: Counterspell (2/Day). The cultist casts Counterspell in response to that spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Aberrant cultists are devoted followers of otherworldly entities, often seeking to bring about the return of their dark masters. They are found in various locations, from hidden cultist lairs to urban centers where they operate in secret. These cultists use their knowledge of forbidden magic and their connection to the Far Realm to manipulate and control others, making them dangerous adversaries for adventurers who cross their path.

Name: Aberrant Spirit
Languages: Deep Speech, understands the languages you know
CR: None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)
Traits:
Regeneration (Slaad Only). The spirit regains 5 Hit Points at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 Hit Point.

Whispering Aura (Psion Devourer Only). At the start of each of the spirit's turns, the spirit emits psionic energy if it doesn't have the Incapacitated condition. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC equals your spell save DC, each creature (other than you) within 5 feet of the spirit. Failure: 2d6 Psychic damage.
Actions:
Multiattack. The spirit makes a number of attacks equal to half this spell's level (round down).

Claw (Slaad Only). Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d10 + 3 + the spell's level Slashing damage, and the target can't regain Hit Points until the start of the spirit's next turn.

Eye Ray (oculus Only). Ranged Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, range 150 ft. Hit: 1d8 + 3 + the spell's level Psychic damage.

Psychic Slam (Psion Devourer Only). Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d8 + 3 + the spell's level Psychic damage.
Description: Aberrant spirits are manifestations of otherworldly energies, often summoned or created through powerful magic. They can take various forms, from the monstrous slaad to the eerie psion devourer, each with its own unique abilities and traits. These spirits are typically found in areas where the veil between planes is thin, such as the Far Realm or locations touched by eldritch forces. They serve as both guardians and agents of their creators, making them unpredictable and dangerous foes for adventurers who encounter them.

Name: Nethaloth, the Abyssal Oracle
Traits:
Amphibious. Nethaloth can breathe air and water.

Eldritch Rebirth. If destroyed, Nethaloth reforms a new body in 5d10 days in a distant abyssal rift or Far Realm pocket.

Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If Nethaloth fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Corruptive Miasma. While underwater, Nethaloth exudes a psychic-tainted mucus. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 14, each creature in a 5-foot Emanation at the end of Nethaloth's turn. Failure: The target is cursed. While cursed, its skin becomes translucent and slimy, it can breathe air and water, and it can't regain Hit Points unless underwater.

A cursed creature outside water takes 6 (1d12) Acid damage every 10 minutes unless its skin is moistened.

Mind-Piercing Telepathy. Any creature that communicates telepathically with Nethaloth reveals its deepest desire to it.
Actions:
Multiattack. Nethaloth makes two Tentacle attacks and uses either Memory Drain or Mind Subjugation if available.

Tentacle. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 15 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is Large or smaller, it becomes Grappled (escape DC 14) by one of four tentacles.

Memory Drain. Intelligence Saving Throw: DC 16, one creature within 30 feet that is Charmed or Grappled. Failure: 10 (3d6) Psychic damage. Success: Half damage. If a Humanoid is reduced to 0 HP by this action, Nethaloth absorbs its memories.

Mind Subjugation (2/Day). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 16, one creature Nethaloth can see within 30 feet. Failure: The target is Charmed until Nethaloth dies or leaves the plane. While Charmed, the target obeys Nethaloth and remains under its influence within 60 feet. They can communicate telepathically across any distance.

The target repeats the save whenever it takes damage or after 24 hours spent at least 1 mile away from Nethaloth.
Legendary Actions:
Lash. Nethaloth makes one Tentacle attack.

Psychic Leech. If Nethaloth has a creature Charmed or Grappled, it uses Memory Drain and regains 5 (1d10) Hit Points.
Regional Effects:
The region around Nethaloth's lair is warped by its presence:

Corrupted Waters. Water sources within 1 mile become tainted. Creatures drinking it must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution save or be Poisoned for 1 hour.

Astral Echo. While in its lair, Nethaloth can cast Project Image (DC 16) without components. The illusion’s range is 1 mile, and Nethaloth can use telepathy as if present in the illusion’s space.

These effects end immediately if Nethaloth dies or relocates its lair.
Description: Nethaloth, the Abyssal Oracle, is a powerful aberration that dwells in the depths of the Underdark or underwater realms. It is a being of immense intelligence and cunning, often sought after for its knowledge of forbidden secrets and its ability to manipulate minds. Nethaloth's presence warps the surrounding environment, creating a dangerous and eerie atmosphere for those who venture near its lair. Adventurers who encounter Nethaloth must be prepared to face its psychic powers and the corrupting influence it exerts on the world around it.

Name: Abominable Yeti
Traits:
Fear of Fire. When the yeti suffers Fire damage, its focus falters, giving it Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until its next turn ends.

Avalanche-Born. This creature carries the raw power of blizzards within its body. Frost clings to its fur like crystal shards, and the air around it chills as if a storm were gathering.
Actions:
Multiattack. The yeti uses Chilling Gaze and makes two attacks of its choice: Claw or Ice Throw.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +11, reach 5 ft. Hit: 14 (2d6 + 7) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Cold damage.

Ice Throw. Ranged Attack Roll: +11, range 60/240 ft. Hit: 12 (2d4 + 7) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) Cold damage.

Chilling Gaze. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 18, one creature the yeti can see within 30 feet. Failure: 21 (6d6) Cold damage, and the target becomes Paralyzed until the start of the yeti’s next turn unless it is immune to Cold. Success: The creature resists the effect and cannot be affected again by this yeti’s gaze for 1 hour.

Cold Breath (Recharge 6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 18, each creature in a 30-foot Cone. Failure: 45 (10d8) Cold damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: These towering predators roam frozen peaks and desolate tundras, thriving where few other creatures can survive. Their immense strength and icy breath make them terrifying foes, and their relentless pursuit of prey has earned them a fearsome reputation among travelers who brave the cold wilderness.

Name: Adult Black Dragon
Senses: Blindsight 60 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 21
Traits:
Amphibious. The dragon can breathe air and water.

Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes three Rend attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting to cast Melf's Acid Arrow (level 3 version).

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +11, reach 10 ft. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 6) Slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) Acid damage.

Acid Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 18, each creature in a 60-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 54 (12d8) Acid damage. Success: Half damage.

Spellcasting. The dragon casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17, +9 to hit with spell attacks): At will: Detect Magic, Fear, Melf's Acid Arrow (level 3 version); 1/day each: Speak with Dead, Vitriolic Sphere
Legendary Actions:
Cloud of Insects. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 17, one creature the dragon can see within 120 feet. Failure: 22 (4d10) Poison damage, and the target has Disadvantage on saving throws to maintain Concentration until the end of its next turn. Failure or Success: The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Frightful Presence. The dragon uses Spellcasting to cast Fear. The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Pounce. The dragon can move up to half its Speed, and it makes one Rend attack.
Regional Effects:
The region containing an adult or ancient black dragon's lair is warped by its presence, creating the following effects:

Acrid Haze. Odorous and stifling fog covers the area within 1 mile of the lair, rendering that area Lightly Obscured. Travel for creatures other than the dragon and its allies takes twice the usual time in that area.

Foul Water. Water sources within 1 mile of the lair are supernaturally fouled. A creature that drinks such water must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or have the Poisoned condition for 1 hour.

If the dragon dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.
Description: Adult black dragons are fearsome predators that dwell in swamps and marshes. They are known for their acidic breath and their cunning nature, often using the terrain to their advantage when hunting. These dragons are territorial and aggressive, making them a significant threat to any adventurers who venture into their domain. Adult black dragons are also known for their hoards of treasure, which they fiercely guard against intruders.

Name: Adult Blue Dragon
Senses: Blindsight 60 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 22
Traits: Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes three Rend attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting to cast Shatter.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +12, reach 10 ft. Hit: 16 (2d8 + 7) Slashing damage plus 5 (1d10) Lightning damage.

Lightning Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 19, each creature in a 90-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 60 (11d10) Lightning damage. Success: Half damage.

Spellcasting. The dragon casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 18): At will: Detect Magic, Invisibility, Mage Hand, Shatter; 1/day each: Scrying, Sending
Legendary Actions:
Cloaked Flight. The dragon uses Spellcasting to cast Invisibility on itself, and it can fly up to half its Fly Speed. The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Sonic Boom. The dragon uses Spellcasting to cast Shatter. The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Tail Swipe. The dragon makes one Rend attack.
Regional Effects:
The region containing an adult or ancient blue dragon's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:

Sinkholes. Sinkholes form more frequently in the area within 1 mile of the lair. Whenever a creature in that area other than the dragon and its allies finishes a Long Rest, roll 1d20. On a 1, a sinkhole opens beneath the creature, and the creature must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or fall 2d4 * 10 feet into the sinkhole.

Spiteful Storms. Dust devils and thunderstorms rage within 1 mile of the lair. The area is Lightly Obscured.

If the dragon dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.
Description: Adult blue dragons are majestic and powerful creatures that inhabit coastal regions and deserts. They are known for their lightning breath and their ability to burrow through the earth. These dragons are territorial and often hoard treasures in their lairs, which can be found in caves or underground chambers. Adult blue dragons are cunning and strategic, using their environment to their advantage when hunting or defending their territory. Adventurers who encounter an adult blue dragon should be prepared for a challenging battle against this formidable foe.

Name: Adult Brass Dragon
Senses: Blindsight 60 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 21
Traits: Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes three Rend attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of (A) Sleep Breath or (B) Spellcasting to cast Scorching Ray.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +11, reach 10 ft. Hit: 17 (2d10 + 6) Slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) Fire damage.

Fire Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 18, each creature in a 60-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 45 (10d8) Fire damage. Success: Half damage.

Sleep Breath. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 18, each creature in a 60-foot Cone. Failure: The target has the Incapacitated condition until the end of its next turn, at which point it repeats the save. Second Failure: The target has the Unconscious condition for 10 minutes. This effect ends for the target if it takes damage or a creature within 5 feet of it takes an action to wake it.

Spellcasting. The dragon casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 16): At will: Detect Magic, Minor Illusion, Scorching Ray, Shapechange (Beast or Humanoid form only, no Temporary Hit Points gained from the spell, and no Concentration or Temporary Hit Points required to maintain the spell), Speak with Animals; 1/day each: Detect Thoughts, Control Weather
Legendary Actions:
Blazing Light. The dragon uses Spellcasting to cast Scorching Ray.

Pounce. The dragon moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Rend attack.

Scorching Sands. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 16, one creature the dragon can see within 120 feet. Failure: 27 (6d8) Fire damage, and the target's Speed is halved until the end of its next turn. Failure or Success: The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.
Regional Effects:
The area containing an adult or ancient brass dragon's lair is altered by its presence, creating the following effects:

Mirages. While in its lair, the dragon can cast Major Image, requiring no Material components and using the same spellcasting ability as its Spellcasting action. When casting the spell this way, the spell's range is 1 mile, and the dragon doesn't need to see the spot where the illusion appears.

Refreshing Water. Water within 1 mile of the lair is magically refreshing. A creature that drinks such water gains 2d4 Temporary Hit Points, and the dragon is immediately aware of the creature's presence.

If the dragon dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.
Description: Adult brass dragons are majestic and benevolent creatures that inhabit deserts and arid regions. They are known for their fiery breath and their ability to create illusions. These dragons are often found in caves or underground chambers, where they hoard treasures and protect them from intruders. Adult brass dragons are friendly and curious, often engaging in conversation with adventurers who show respect and kindness. They are also known for their love of storytelling and their desire to share knowledge with those they deem worthy.

Name: Adult Bronze Dragon
Senses: Blindsight 60 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 22
Traits:
Amphibious. The dragon can breathe air and water.

Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes three Rend attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of (A) Repulsion Breath or (B) Spellcasting to cast Guiding Bolt (level 2 version).

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +12, reach 10 ft. Hit: 16 (2d8 + 7) Slashing damage plus 5 (1d10) Lightning damage.

Lightning Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 19, each creature in a 90-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 55 (10d10) Lightning damage. Success: Half damage.

Repulsion Breath. Strength Saving Throw: DC 19, each creature in a 30-foot Cone. Failure: The target is pushed up to 60 feet straight away from the dragon and has the Prone condition.

Spellcasting. The dragon casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17, +10 to hit with spell attacks): At will: Detect Magic, Guiding Bolt (level 2 version), Shapechange (Beast or Humanoid form only, no Temporary Hit Points gained from the spell, and no Concentration or Temporary Hit Points required to maintain the spell), Speak with Animals, Thaumaturgy; 1/day each: Detect Thoughts, Water Breathing
Legendary Actions:
Guiding Light. The dragon uses Spellcasting to cast Guiding Bolt (level 2 version).

Pounce. The dragon moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Rend attack.

Thunderclap. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 17, each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point the dragon can see within 90 feet. Failure: 10 (3d6) Thunder damage, and the target has the Deafened condition until the end of its next turn.
Regional Effects:
The region containing an adult or ancient bronze dragon's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:

Buoying Currents. Creatures within 1 mile of the lair that lack a Swim Speed ignore the extra cost of movement while swimming.

Sun and Storms. While in its lair, the dragon can cast Control Weather, requiring no Material components and using the same spellcasting ability as its Spellcasting action. When casting the spell this way, the dragon can control the weather within 1 mile of its lair, regardless if the dragon is inside or outside.

If the dragon dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.
Description: Adult bronze dragons are noble and majestic creatures that inhabit coastal regions and islands. They are known for their lightning breath and their ability to control the weather. These dragons are often found in caves or underground chambers, where they hoard treasures and protect them from intruders. Adult bronze dragons are friendly and protective, often coming to the aid of those in need. They are also known for their love of storytelling and their desire to share knowledge with those they deem worthy.

Name: Adult Copper Dragon
Senses: Blindsight 60 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 22
Traits: Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes three Rend attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of (A) Slowing Breath or (B) Spellcasting to cast Mind Spike (level 4 version).

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +11, reach 10 ft. Hit: 17 (2d10 + 6) Slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) Acid damage.

Acid Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 18, each creature in an 60-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 54 (12d8) Acid damage. Success: Half damage.

Slowing Breath. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 18, each creature in a 60-foot Cone. Failure: The target can't take Reactions; its Speed is halved; and it can take either an action or a Bonus Action on its turn, not both. This effect lasts until the end of its next turn.

Spellcasting. The dragon casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17): At will: Detect Magic, Mind Spike (level 4 version), Minor Illusion, Shapechange (Beast or Humanoid form only, no Temporary Hit Points gained from the spell, and no Concentration or Temporary Hit Points required to maintain the spell); 1/day each: Greater Restoration, Major Image
Legendary Actions:
Giggling Magic. Charisma Saving Throw: DC 17, one creature the dragon can see within 90 feet. Failure: 24 (7d6) Psychic damage. Until the end of its next turn, the target rolls 1d6 whenever it makes an ability check or attack roll and subtracts the number rolled from the D20 Test. Failure or Success: The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Mind Jolt. The dragon uses Spellcasting to cast Mind Spike (level 4 version). The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Pounce. The dragon moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Rend attack.
Regional Effects:
The region containing an adult or ancient copper dragon's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:

Chatty Critters. Tiny Beasts magically gain the ability to speak and understand Draconic while within 6 miles of the lair.

Giggle Fits. Whenever a creature other than the dragon and its allies is within 1 mile of the lair and rolls a 1 on a D20 Test, it must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or have the Incapacitated condition until the end of its next turn, as it is wracked with laughter.

If the dragon dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.
Description: Adult copper dragons are playful and mischievous creatures that inhabit hills and rocky terrain. They are known for their acidic breath and their love of pranks. These dragons are often found in caves or underground chambers, where they hoard treasures and protect them from intruders. Adult copper dragons are friendly and curious, often engaging in conversation with adventurers who show respect and kindness. They are also known for their love of storytelling and their desire to share knowledge with those they deem worthy.

Name: Adult Gold Dragon
Senses: Blindsight 60 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 24
Traits:
Amphibious. The dragon can breathe air and water.

Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes three Rend attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of (A) Spellcasting to cast Guiding Bolt (level 2 version) or (B) Weakening Breath.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +14, reach 10 ft. Hit: 17 (2d8 + 8) Slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) Fire damage.

Fire Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 21, each creature in a 60-foot Cone. Failure: 66 (12d10) Fire damage. Success: Half damage.

Spellcasting. The dragon casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 21, +13 to hit with spell attacks): At will: Detect Magic, Guiding Bolt (level 2 version), Shapechange (Beast or Humanoid form only, no Temporary Hit Points gained from the spell, and no Concentration or Temporary Hit Points required to maintain the spell); 1/day each: Flame Strike, Zone of Truth

Weakening Breath. Strength Saving Throw: DC 21, each creature that isn't currently affected by this breath in a 60-foot Cone. Failure: The target has Disadvantage on Strength-based D20 Tests and subtracts 3 (1d6) from its damage rolls. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.
Legendary Actions:
Banish. Charisma Saving Throw: DC 21, one creature the dragon can see within 120 feet. Failure: 10 (3d6) Force damage, and the target has the Incapacitated condition and is transported to a harmless demiplane until the start of the dragon's next turn, at which point it reappears in an unoccupied space of the dragon's choice within 120 feet of the dragon. Failure or Success: The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Guiding Light. The dragon uses Spellcasting to cast Guiding Bolt (level 2 version).

Pounce. The dragon moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Rend attack.
Regional Effects:
The region containing an adult or ancient gold dragon's lair is altered by its presence, creating the following effects:

Dream Messenger. While in its lair, the dragon can cast Dream, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability. When casting the spell this way, the dragon can target any creature within 6 miles.

Foretelling Fog. The area within 1 mile of the lair is Lightly Obscured by opalescent fog. While in that area, creatures can't be surprised, as the fog swirls into shapes that warn of danger.

If the dragon dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.
Description: Adult gold dragons are majestic and noble creatures that inhabit forests and grasslands. They are known for their fiery breath and their ability to shapechange. These dragons are often found in caves or underground chambers, where they hoard treasures and protect them from intruders. Adult gold dragons are benevolent and protective, often coming to the aid of those in need. They are also known for their love of storytelling and their desire to share knowledge with those they deem worthy.

Name: Adult Green Dragon
Senses: Blindsight 60 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 22
Traits:
Amphibious. The dragon can breathe air and water.

Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes three Rend attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting to cast Mind Spike (level 3 version).

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +11, reach 10 ft. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Poison damage.

Poison Breath (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 18, each creature in a 60-foot Cone. Failure: 56 (16d6) Poison damage. Success: Half damage.

Spellcasting. The dragon casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17): At will: Detect Magic, Mind Spike (level 3 version); 1/day: Geas
Legendary Actions:
Mind Invasion. The dragon uses Spellcasting to cast Mind Spike (level 3 version).

Noxious Miasma. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 17, each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point the dragon can see within 90 feet. Failure: 7 (2d6) Poison damage, and the target takes a -2 penalty to AC until the end of its next turn. Failure or Success: The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Pounce. The dragon moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Rend attack.
Regional Effects:
The region containing an adult or ancient green dragon's lair is warped by its presence, creating the following effects:

Beast Spies. Tiny Beasts magically gain the ability to understand Draconic and can communicate telepathically with the dragon while within 1 mile of the lair.

Poisonous Thicket. Ordinary plants growing within 1 mile of the lair poison the air around them. Whenever a creature other than the dragon or its allies finishes a Long Rest in that area, it must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or have the Poisoned condition for 1 hour.

If the dragon dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.
Description: Adult green dragons are cunning and territorial creatures that inhabit forests. They are known for their poisonous breath and their ability to communicate with plants and animals. These dragons are often found in caves or underground chambers, where they hoard treasures and protect them from intruders. Adult green dragons are manipulative and deceitful, often using their charm and intelligence to achieve their goals. They are also known for their love of storytelling and their desire to share knowledge with those they deem worthy.

Name: Adult Red Dragon
Senses: Blindsight 60 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 23
Traits: Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes three Rend attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting to cast Scorching Ray.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +14, reach 10 ft. Hit: 13 (1d10 + 8) Slashing damage plus 5 (2d4) Fire damage.

Fire Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 21, each creature in a 60-foot Cone. Failure: 59 (17d6) Fire damage. Success: Half damage.

Spellcasting. The dragon casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 20, +12 to hit with spell attacks): At will: Command (level 2 version), Detect Magic, Scorching Ray; 1/day: Fireball
Legendary Actions:
Commanding Presence. The dragon uses Spellcasting to cast Command (level 2 version). The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Fiery Rays. The dragon uses Spellcasting to cast Scorching Ray. The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Pounce. The dragon moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Rend attack.
Regional Effects:

These dragons are often found in caves or underground chambers, where they hoard treasures and protect them from intruders. Ancient red dragons are malevolent and territorial, often attacking anyone who enters their domain without permission. They are also known for their love of storytelling and their desire to share knowledge with those they deem worthy.

Name: Ancient Silver Dragon
Senses: Blindsight 60 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 26
Traits: Legendary Resistance (4/Day, or 5/Day in Lair). If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes three Rend attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of (A) Paralyzing Breath or (B) Spellcasting to cast Ice Knife (level 2 version).

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +17, reach 15 ft. Hit: 19 (2d8 + 10) Slashing damage plus 9 (2d8) Cold damage.

Cold Breath (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 24, each creature in a 90-foot Cone. Failure: 67 (15d8) Cold damage. Success: Half damage.

Paralyzing Breath. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 24, each creature in a 90-foot Cone. First Failure: The target has the Incapacitated condition until the end of its next turn, when it repeats the save. Second Failure: The target has the Paralyzed condition, and it repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.

Spellcasting. The dragon casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 23, +15 to hit with spell attacks): At will: Detect Magic, Hold Monster, Ice Knife (level 2 version), Shapechange (Beast or Humanoid form only, no Temporary Hit Points gained from the spell, and no Concentration or Temporary Hit Points required to maintain the spell); 1/day each: Control Weather, Ice Storm (level 7 version), Teleport, Zone of Truth
Legendary Actions:
Chill. The dragon uses Spellcasting to cast Hold Monster. The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Cold Gale. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 23, each creature in a 60-foot-long, 10-foot-wide Line. Failure: 14 (4d6) Cold damage, and the target is pushed up to 30 feet straight away from the dragon. Success: Half damage only. Failure or Success: The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Pounce. The dragon moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Rend attack.
Regional Effects:
The region containing an adult or ancient silver dragon's lair is changed by its magic, creating the following effects:

Gentle Gusts. Winds buoy creatures that fall within 1 mile of the lair. Such creatures descend at a rate of 60 feet per round and take no damage from falling.

Sun and Storms. While in its lair, the dragon can cast Control Weather, requiring no Material components and using the same spellcasting ability as its Spellcasting action.

If the dragon dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.
Description: Ancient silver dragons are noble and protective creatures that inhabit mountains and urban areas. They are known for their cold breath and their ability to fly. These dragons are often found in caves or underground chambers, where they hoard treasures and protect them from intruders. Ancient silver dragons are benevolent and protective, often coming to the aid of those in need. They are also known for their love of storytelling and their desire to share knowledge with those they deem worthy.

Name: Ancient White Dragon
Speed: 40 ft., Burrow 40 ft., Fly 80 ft., Swim 40 ft.
Senses: Blindsight 60 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 23
Traits:
Ice Walk. The dragon can move across and climb icy surfaces without needing to make an ability check. Additionally, Difficult Terrain composed of ice or snow doesn't cost it extra movement.

Legendary Resistance (4/Day, or 5/Day in Lair). If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes three Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +14, reach 15 ft. Hit: 17 (2d8 + 8) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Cold damage.

Cold Breath (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 22, each creature in a 90-foot Cone. Failure: 63 (14d8) Cold damage. Success: Half damage.
Legendary Actions:
Freezing Burst. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 20, each creature in a 30-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet. Failure: 14 (4d6) Cold damage, and the target's Speed is 0 until the end of the target's next turn. Failure or Success: The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Frightful Presence. The dragon casts Fear, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 18). The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Pounce. The dragon moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Rend attack.
Regional Effects:
The region containing an adult or ancient white dragon's lair is affected by its presence, creating the following effects:

Frigid Cold. The area within 1 mile of the lair is an area of extreme cold. Any water in that area is frigid water. See the Dungeon Master's Guide for rules on extreme cold and frigid water.

Glacial Gloom. The area within 1 mile of the lair is Lightly Obscured by chilly fog. Whenever a creature other than the dragon or one of its allies finishes a Long Rest in that area, that creature must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or have its Speed reduced by 10 feet for 1 hour.

If the dragon dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.
Description: Ancient white dragons are vicious and predatory creatures that inhabit arctic regions. They are known for their cold breath and their ability to move across icy surfaces. These dragons are often found in caves or underground chambers, where they hoard treasures and protect them from intruders. Ancient white dragons are malevolent and territorial, often attacking anyone who enters their domain without permission. They are also known for their love of storytelling and their desire to share knowledge with those they deem worthy.

Name: Animal Lord
Traits:
Animal Lordship. An animal lord represents a Forager, Hunter, or Sage (DM's choice), which determines certain traits in this stat block.

Legendary Resistance (4/Day). If the animal lord fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Lordly Presence. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 20, any enemy that starts its turn in a 30-foot Emanation originating from the animal lord. Failure: The target suffers one of the following effects:

Captivated (Forager Only). The target has the Charmed condition until the end of its next turn. While Charmed, the target has the Incapacitated condition.

Fearful (Hunter Only). The target has the Frightened condition until the end of its next turn.

Mired (Sage Only). The target takes 10 (3d6) Psychic damage, and the target is magically bewildered until the end of its next turn. While bewildered, the target subtracts 1d4 from its saving throws.

Magic Resistance. The animal lord has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The animal lord makes two attacks, using Rend or Radiant Ray in any combination, and uses Animal Spirit.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +13, reach 5 ft. Hit: 14 (2d6 + 7) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Force damage.

Radiant Ray. Ranged Attack Roll: +12, range 120 ft. Hit: 20 (4d6 + 6) Radiant damage.

Animal Spirit. The animal lord conjures an animal spirit that strikes at a creature and then disappears. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 20, one creature the animal lord can see within 120 feet. Failure: 28 (4d10 + 6) Radiant damage. Success: Half damage. Failure or Success: One of the following effects occurs:

Fortify (Forager Only). The animal lord gains 20 Temporary Hit Points.

Marked as Prey (Hunter Only). The animal lord has Advantage on attack rolls against the target until the start of the animal lord's next turn.

Pesky Swarm (Sage Only). The target has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the end of its next turn.

Spellcasting. The animal lord casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 20): At will: Animal Friendship, Animal Messenger, Speak with Animals; 2/day each: Awaken, Greater Restoration; 1/day each: Animal Shapes, Sunburst
Bonus Actions: Shape-Shift. The animal lord shape-shifts into a Huge or smaller version of the animal it represents or a Medium or Small Humanoid, or it returns to its true form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.
Legendary Actions:
Feral Strike. The animal lord moves up to its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks, and it makes one Rend attack.

Radiant Strike. The animal lord makes one Radiant Ray attack.
Description: Animal lords are powerful celestial beings that embody the essence of nature and the animal kingdom. They are often associated with specific types of animals, such as predators, prey, or wise creatures. Animal lords are known for their ability to communicate with and command animals, as well as their mastery of nature magic. They are often found in the Beastlands, a plane of existence that is home to many different types of animals and natural environments. Animal lords are revered by many creatures in the Beastlands and are often sought out for guidance and protection.

Name: Animated Armor
Condition Immunities: charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned
Actions:
Multiattack. The armor makes two Slam attacks.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Animated armor is a construct that can be animated to serve as a guardian or protector. It is often found in urban environments and is known for its durability and strength.

Name: Animated Broom
Condition Immunities: charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned
Traits: Flyby. The broom doesn't provoke an Opportunity Attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.
Actions: Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Animated brooms are small constructs that can be animated to serve as helpers or servants. They are often found in urban environments and are known for their agility and speed.

Name: Animated Flying Sword
Condition Immunities: charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned
Actions: Slash. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Slashing damage.
Description: Animated flying swords are small constructs that can be animated to serve as guardians or protectors. They are often found in urban environments and are known for their agility and sharpness.

Name: Animated Object
Size: Tiny, Small, Medium, Large, or Huge
CR: None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)
Actions: Slam. Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: Force damage equal to 1d4 + 3, 2d6 + 3 + your spellcasting ability modifier (Large), or 2d12 + 3 + your spellcasting ability modifier (Huge).
Description: Animated objects are inanimate objects that have been magically animated to serve a purpose. They can be of various sizes and types, and they are often used as guardians or servants. Animated objects are known for their strength and durability, and they can be found in a variety of environments.

Name: Animated Rug of Smothering
Condition Immunities: charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned
Actions:
Smother. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, the rug can give it the Grappled condition (escape DC 13) instead of dealing damage. Until the grapple ends, the target has the Blinded and Restrained conditions, is suffocating, and takes 10 (2d6 + 3) Bludgeoning damage at the start of each of its turns. The rug can smother only one creature at a time.

While grappling the target, the rug can't take this action, the rug halves the damage it takes (round down), and the target takes the same amount of damage.
Description: Animated rugs of smothering are large constructs that can be animated to serve as guardians or protectors. They are often found in urban environments and are known for their ability to smother and suffocate their targets.

Name: Burrowmaw Devourer
Senses: Darkvision 60 ft., Tremorsense 60 ft., passive Perception 11
Traits: Tunneler. The burrowmaw devourer can burrow through solid rock at half its Burrow Speed and leaves a 10-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.
Actions:
Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5 (with Advantage if the target is Grappled by the burrowmaw devourer), reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) Acid damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 13).

Acid Spray (Recharge 6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 30-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 14 (4d6) Acid damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Burrowmaw devourers are massive insectoid predators that tunnel beneath the earth, erupting from below to seize prey in their crushing mandibles. Their bodies secrete volatile acids used both for feeding and for carving tunnels through soil and stone. Though mindless, they are relentless hunters, guided by vibrations in the ground and an instinctive drive to ambush anything that wanders above their subterranean lairs.

Name: Ankylosaurus
Actions:
Multiattack. The ankylosaurus makes two Tail attacks.

Tail. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.
Description: Ankylosauruses are large, heavily armored dinosaurs that use their powerful tails to defend themselves from predators. They are often found in grasslands, where they graze on vegetation and use their armor to protect themselves from threats.

Name: Ape
Actions:
Multiattack. The ape makes two Fist attacks.

Fist. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Bludgeoning damage.

Rock (Recharge 6). Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 25/50 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Apes are intelligent and agile creatures that inhabit forests. They are known for their climbing abilities and their use of tools. Apes are social animals that live in groups and communicate with each other through a variety of vocalizations and gestures.

Name: Arcanshade Vizier
Traits:
Fiendish Restoration. If the Arcanshade Vizier is slain anywhere other than Gehenna, its form collapses into foul sludge, and its essence reforms instantly in Gehenna with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The Arcanshade Vizier has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Soul Tome. The Arcanshade Vizier wields a cursed grimoire etched with infernal glyphs. While it carries the tome, it can use its Banishing Claw action.

The tome has AC 17; HP 35; and Immunity to Necrotic, Poison, and Psychic damage. It fully regenerates at the end of each turn. If reduced to 0 Hit Points or if the Vizier dies, the tome disintegrates into ash. The Vizier can conjure a replacement after a Short or Long Rest.
Actions:
Multiattack. The Arcanshade Vizier makes three Fiendish Burst attacks, and it may replace one of them with a Banishing Claw attack.

Fiendish Burst. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 31 (4d12 + 5) Necrotic damage.

Banishing Claw (Requires Soul Tome). Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d4 + 5) Slashing damage plus 19 (3d12) Psychic damage. If the target is a creature, it must make a Charisma Saving Throw (DC 17). Failure: The creature is drawn into a pocket dimension within the Soul Tome and becomes Incapacitated. At the end of each of its turns, it repeats the save, escaping on a success and reappearing in its original space or the nearest free space.

If the creature fails three of these saves while trapped, it becomes bound to the tome and cannot escape unless the tome is destroyed.

Spellcasting. The Arcanshade Vizier casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17): At will: Alter Self, Detect Magic, Identify, Mage Hand, Prestidigitation; 1/day each: Contact Other Plane, Detect Thoughts, Dimension Door, Mind Blank
Bonus Actions: Teleport. The Arcanshade Vizier teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.
Reactions: Counterspell. The Arcanshade Vizier casts Counterspell when triggered, using the same spellcasting ability as its Spellcasting trait.
Description: Arcanshade Viziers are cunning fiends who treat knowledge as both a commodity and a weapon. Their Soul Tomes serve as arcane repositories and prisons, capable of capturing the essence of those who oppose them. Driven by ambition and an insatiable hunger for secrets, they act as advisors, manipulators, and arcane brokers throughout the Lower Planes, weaving long-term schemes that often outlast mortal civilizations.

Name: Arch-hag
Traits:
Coven Magic. When the arch-hag is within 30 feet of at least two other hags, she can cast one of the following spells without Material components, using the spell’s normal casting time and Intelligence as her spellcasting ability (spell save DC 19): Augury, Find Familiar, Identify, Locate Object, Scrying, or Unseen Servant. She must complete a Long Rest before she can cast that spell again using this trait.

Legendary Resistance (4/Day, or 5/Day in Lair). If the arch-hag fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

Magic Resistance. The arch-hag has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Spiteful Escape. When the arch-hag is reduced to 0 Hit Points, she dies only if she is within 30 feet of her anathema (a concept or object chosen by the DM as the thing she despises most). Otherwise, she instead drops to 1 Hit Point and instantly teleports to a harmless demiplane. She cannot return to the plane she left for 2d6 days. When she vanishes, each creature within 60 feet of where she stood becomes cursed. Until the curse ends, the creature has Disadvantage on ability checks and saving throws, and the arch-hag always knows its location anywhere in the multiverse.
Actions:
Multiattack. The arch-hag makes two Spectral Claw attacks and uses Crackling Wave.

Spectral Claw. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +14, reach 10 ft. or range 60 ft. Hit: 17 (3d6 + 7) Force damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is knocked Prone.

Crackling Wave. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 22, each creature in a 60-foot Cone. Failure: 32 (5d12) Lightning damage. Success: Half damage. Regardless of the save, the target is cursed until the end of the arch-hag’s next turn and cannot take Reactions while cursed.

Spellcasting. The arch-hag casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 22): At will: Detect Thoughts, Dimension Door, Dispel Magic, Hypnotic Pattern; 2/day each: Mass Suggestion, Modify Memory, Plane Shift
Bonus Actions: Witch Strike. Each creature cursed by the arch-hag and within 60 feet of her takes 14 (4d6) Lightning damage.
Reactions: Tongue Twister. The arch-hag casts Counterspell when triggered, using the same spellcasting ability as her Spellcasting trait. If the target fails its saving throw, it becomes cursed until the end of its next turn. While cursed, it cannot cast spells with Verbal components, and anything it says comes out as the opposite of what it intends.
Legendary Actions:
Hag's Swipe. The arch-hag makes one Spectral Claw attack.

Malicious Magic. The arch-hag uses Spellcasting to cast Dimension Door or Hypnotic Pattern. She cannot use this action again until the start of her next turn.
Regional Effects:
The land surrounding an arch-hag’s lair warps under her influence, producing the following effects:

Lapsus Linguae. Creatures other than the arch-hag and her allies within 1 mile of the lair subtract 1d10 from any ability check they make when taking the Influence action.

Meddlesome Magic. When a creature other than the arch-hag or her allies finishes a Long Rest within 1 mile of the lair, the first time it casts a spell using a spell slot afterward, it also casts Confusion centered on itself. The spell uses the creature’s spellcasting ability and does not require Concentration.

If the arch-hag is slain or relocates her lair, these effects end immediately.
Description: Arch-hags are ancient fey tyrants who command covens and twist magic to suit their whims. Their silver tongues and overwhelming presence make them master manipulators, while their lairs become centers of supernatural mischief and corruption. Wherever an arch-hag settles, schemes unfold, alliances crumble, and mortals find themselves entangled in plots that can span generations.

Name: Archelon
Traits: Amphibious. The archelon can breathe air and water.
Actions:
Multiattack. The archelon makes two Bite attacks.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 14 (3d6 + 4) Piercing damage.
Description: Archelons are giant sea turtles that inhabit underwater environments. They are known for their massive size and their powerful bites. Archelons are often found in coastal areas and can be a formidable threat to sailors and other creatures that venture into their territory.

Name: Archmage
Traits: Magic Resistance. The archmage has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The archmage makes four Arcane Burst attacks.

Arcane Burst. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. or range 150 ft. Hit: 27 (4d10 + 5) Force damage.

Spellcasting. The archmage casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17): At will: Detect Magic, Detect Thoughts, Disguise Self, Invisibility, Light, Mage Armor (included in AC), Mage Hand, Prestidigitation; 2/day each: Fly, Lightning Bolt (level 7 version); 1/day each: Cone of Cold (level 9 version), Mind Blank (cast before combat), Scrying, Teleport
Bonus Actions: Misty Step (3/Day). The mage casts Misty Step, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Reactions: Protective Magic (3/Day). The archmage casts Counterspell or Shield in response to the spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Archmages are powerful spellcasters who have mastered the arcane arts. They are known for their intelligence, their magical prowess, and their ability to manipulate the forces of magic. Archmages can be found in various environments, where they often serve as advisors, researchers, or leaders in magical communities.

Name: Archpriest
Actions:
Multiattack. The archpriest makes three Radiant Burst attacks.

Radiant Burst. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. Hit: 27 (4d10 + 5) Radiant damage.

Holy Word (Recharge 4-6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 17, each enemy in a 20-foot Emanation originating from the archpriest. Failure: 21 (6d6) Radiant damage, and the target has the Stunned condition until the end of the archpriest's next turn. Success: Half damage only.

Spellcasting. The archpriest casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17): At will: Light, Thaumaturgy; 1/day each: Flame Strike (level 6 version), Greater Restoration, Raise Dead, Zone of Truth
Bonus Actions: Divine Aid (3/Day). The priest casts Bless, Dispel Magic, Healing Word, or Lesser Restoration, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Archpriests are powerful clerics who have devoted their lives to the service of their deity. They are known for their wisdom, their healing abilities, and their ability to channel divine power. Archpriests can be found in various environments, where they often serve as leaders, advisors, or healers in religious communities.

Name: Assassin
Traits: Evasion. If the assassin is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, the assassin instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the save and only half damage if it fails. It can't use this trait if it has the Incapacitated condition.
Actions:
Multiattack. The assassin makes three attacks, using Shortsword or Light Crossbow in any combination.

Shortsword. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) Piercing damage plus 17 (5d6) Poison damage, and the target has the Poisoned condition until the start of the assassin's next turn.

Light Crossbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +7, range 80/320 ft. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) Piercing damage plus 21 (6d6) Poison damage.
Bonus Actions: Cunning Action. The assassin takes the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.
Description: Assassins are skilled killers who operate in the shadows. They are known for their stealth, their precision, and their ability to take down targets quickly.

Name: Avatar of Death
HP: Half the HP maximum of its summoner
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, unconscious
Languages: All languages known to its summoner
Traits: Incorporeal Movement. The avatar can move through other creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain. It takes 5 (1d10) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.
Actions:
Multiattack. The avatar makes a number of Reaping Scythe attacks equal to half the summoner's Proficiency Bonus (rounded up).

Reaping Scythe. Melee Attack Roll: Automatic hit, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) Necrotic damage.
Description: The Avatar of Death is a powerful undead entity that serves as a manifestation of death itself. It is often summoned by powerful necromancers or other dark spellcasters to do their bidding. The avatar is known for its ability to move through solid objects and its deadly Reaping Scythe attack, which can strike multiple targets in a single turn.

Name: Awakened Shrub
Actions: Rake. Melee Attack Roll: +1, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Slashing damage.
Description: Awakened shrubs are small, sentient plants that have been magically animated. They are often found in forests, where they can blend in with the surrounding vegetation. Awakened shrubs are known for their ability to defend themselves with their Rake attack, which allows them to strike at nearby creatures with their branches.

Name: Awakened Tree
Actions: Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Awakened trees are large, sentient plants that have been magically animated. They are often found in forests, where they can blend in with the surrounding vegetation. Awakened trees are known for their powerful Slam attack, which allows them to strike at nearby creatures with their massive branches.

Name: Axe Beak
Actions: Beak. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Slashing damage.
Description: Axe beaks are large, flightless birds that inhabit various environments, including arctic regions, grasslands, and hills. They are known for their powerful beaks, which they use to defend themselves and to hunt for food. Axe beaks are often found in packs and can be a formidable threat to smaller creatures.

Name: Azer Pyromancer
Traits:
Fire Aura. At the end of each of the azer's turns, each creature of the azer's choice in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the azer takes 11 (2d10) Fire damage unless the azer has the Incapacitated condition.

Illumination. The azer sheds Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet.
Actions:
Multiattack. The azer makes two Flame Burst attacks.

Flame Burst. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) Fire damage.

Spellcasting. The azer casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15): At will: Elementalism, Mage Hand; 1/day: Fireball
Reactions: Hellish Rebuke (2/Day). The azer casts Hellish Rebuke in response to that spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Azer pyromancers are fiery elemental beings that serve as guardians of the Elemental Plane of Fire. They are known for their mastery of fire magic and their ability to create a fiery aura that damages nearby creatures. Azer pyromancers are often found in mountainous regions and can be a formidable threat to those who venture into their territory.

Name: Azer Sentinel
Traits:
Fire Aura. At the end of each of the azer's turns, each creature of the azer's choice in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the azer takes 5 (1d10) Fire damage unless the azer has the Incapacitated condition.

Illumination. The azer sheds Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet.
Actions: Burning Hammer. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) Bludgeoning damage plus 3 (1d6) Fire damage.
Description: Azer sentinels are fiery elemental beings that serve as guardians of the Elemental Plane of Fire. They are known for their strength and their ability to create a fiery aura that damages nearby creatures. Azer sentinels are often found in mountainous regions and can be a formidable threat to those who venture into their territory.

Name: Baboon
Traits: Pack Tactics. The baboon has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the baboon's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +1, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 (1d4 - 1) Piercing damage.
Description: Baboons are intelligent primates that live in groups and are known for their social behavior and adaptability. They are often found in forested areas and can be a nuisance to travelers.

Name: Badger
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Piercing damage.
Description: Badgers are small, burrowing mammals that are known for their tenacity and their ability to defend themselves with powerful bites. They are often found in forested areas and can be a nuisance to travelers.

Name: Balor
Traits:
Death Throes. When the balor is slain, its body detonates in a violent burst of energy. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 20, each creature in a 30-foot Emanation centered on the balor. Failure: 31 (9d6) Fire damage plus 31 (9d6) Force damage. Success: Half damage. Regardless of the save, if the balor dies outside the Abyss, its essence reforms instantly somewhere within the Abyss with full Hit Points.

Fire Aura. At the end of each of the balor’s turns, every creature within 5 feet of it takes 13 (3d8) Fire damage.

Legendary Resistance (3/Day). If the balor fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Magic Resistance. The balor has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The balor makes one Flame Whip attack and one Lightning Blade attack.

Flame Whip. Melee Attack Roll: +14, reach 30 ft. Hit: 18 (3d6 + 8) Force damage plus 17 (5d6) Fire damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, the balor drags it up to 25 feet straight toward itself, and the target is knocked Prone.

Lightning Blade. Melee Attack Roll: +14, reach 10 ft. Hit: 21 (3d8 + 8) Force damage plus 22 (4d10) Lightning damage. The target cannot take Reactions until the start of the balor’s next turn.
Bonus Actions: Teleport. The balor teleports itself or a willing demon within 10 feet up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.
Description: Balors are towering demon warlords whose presence ignites the battlefield with fire and raw destructive power. Serving as commanders of Abyssal legions, they wield infernal weapons and overwhelming might, crushing foes through sheer force and unrelenting fury. Their mastery of flame and lightning, combined with their resistance to magic, makes them among the most feared entities in the Abyss.

Name: Bandit
Actions:
Scimitar. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d6 + 1) Slashing damage.

Light Crossbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +3, range 80/320 ft. Hit: 5 (1d8 + 1) Piercing damage.
Description: Bandits are common criminals who operate in various environments. They are known for their opportunistic nature and their willingness to engage in theft, robbery, and other illicit activities. Bandits can be found in groups and can pose a threat to travelers and small settlements.

Name: Bandit Captain
Actions:
Multiattack. The bandit makes two attacks, using Scimitar and Pistol in any combination.

Scimitar. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Slashing damage.

Pistol. Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 30/90 ft. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) Piercing damage.
Reactions: Parry. Trigger: The bandit is hit by a melee attack roll while holding a weapon. Response: The bandit adds 2 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.
Description: Bandit captains are the leaders of bandit groups. They are known for their combat skills, their leadership abilities, and their willingness to engage in theft, robbery, and other illicit activities. Bandit captains can be found in various environments and can pose a significant threat to travelers and small settlements.

Name: Bandit Crime Lord
Traits: Evasion. If the bandit is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, the bandit instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the save and only half damage if it fails. It can't use this trait if it has the Incapacitated condition.
Actions:
Multiattack. The bandit makes three attacks, using Scimitar or Pistol in any combination.

Scimitar. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Slashing damage plus 14 (4d6) Poison damage.

Pistol. Ranged Attack Roll: +9, range 30/90 ft. Hit: 10 (1d10 + 5) Piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) Poison damage.
Bonus Actions: Deadly Aim. The bandit gives itself Advantage on the next attack roll it makes during the current turn. If that attack hits, the target takes an extra 28 (8d6) Poison damage.
Description: Bandit crime lords are the most powerful and influential members of bandit groups. They are known for their combat skills, their leadership abilities, and their willingness to engage in theft, robbery, and other illicit activities. Bandit crime lords can be found in various environments and can pose a significant threat to travelers and small settlements.

Name: Bandit Deceiver
Actions:
Multiattack. The bandit makes three Dagger attacks.

Dagger. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) Piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) Poison damage.

Blinding Flash (Recharge 4-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 14, each creature in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point the bandit can see within 120 feet. Failure: 13 (3d6 + 3) Radiant damage, and the target has the Blinded condition until the start of the bandit's next turn. Success: Half damage only.

Spellcasting. The bandit casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14): At will: Disguise Self, Mage Hand, Minor Illusion; 1/day each: Hold Person (level 4 version), Mage Armor (included in AC), Major Image
Description: Bandit deceivers are cunning and manipulative members of bandit groups. They are known for their combat skills, their ability to deceive and manipulate others, and their willingness to engage in theft, robbery, and other illicit activities. Bandit deceivers can be found in various environments and can pose a significant threat to travelers and small settlements.

Name: Banshee
Damage Resistances: acid, bludgeoning, fire, lightning, piercing, slashing, thunder
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained
Traits:
Detect Life. The banshee can magically sense the presence and direction of living creatures within 1 mile, excluding Constructs and Undead.

Incorporeal Movement. The banshee can pass through creatures and objects as though they were Difficult Terrain. If it ends its turn inside an object, it takes 5 (1d10) Force damage.
Actions:
Multiattack. The banshee makes two Corrupting Touch attacks and uses Horrify.

Corrupting Touch. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Necrotic damage.

Horrify. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 13, one creature within 60 feet that can see the banshee. Failure: The target becomes Frightened until the start of the banshee’s next turn. Success: The target becomes immune to this banshee’s Horrify for 24 hours.

Deathly Wail (1/Day). The banshee unleashes a chilling scream, provided it is not in sunlight. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 13, each creature within 30 feet that can hear it and is not a Construct or Undead. Failure: If the creature has 25 Hit Points or fewer, it drops to 0 Hit Points. Otherwise, it takes 10 (3d6) Psychic damage.
Description: Banshees are tormented spirits bound to the places of their demise. Their ghostly forms drift effortlessly through walls and barriers, and their anguished cries can freeze the hearts of the living. These spectral beings linger in haunted ruins and cursed lands, where their presence is a deadly omen for any who dare to intrude.

Name: Barbed Devil
Senses: Darkvision 120 ft. (unimpeded by magical Darkness), passive Perception 18
Traits:
Barbed Hide. At the start of each of its turns, the devil deals 5 (1d10) Piercing damage to any creature it is grappling or any creature grappling it.

Diabolical Restoration. If the devil is slain outside the Nine Hells, its form dissolves into acrid smoke, and its essence reforms instantly somewhere in the Nine Hells with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The devil has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The devil makes one Claws attack and one Tail attack, or it makes two Hurl Flame attacks.

Claws. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it becomes Grappled (escape DC 13) by both claws.

Tail. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. Hit: 14 (2d10 + 3) Slashing damage.

Hurl Flame. Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 150 ft. Hit: 17 (5d6) Fire damage. If the target is a flammable object not being worn or carried, it ignites.
Description: Barbed devils serve as brutal enforcers within the infernal hierarchy, their bodies covered in razor-sharp spines that punish any who dare to grapple them. Their resilience to magic and their cruel efficiency make them favored agents of higher-ranking devils. Found throughout the Nine Hells, they carry out orders with ruthless precision, often acting as guards, tormentors, or battlefield shock troops.

Name: Barlgurak
Senses: Blindsight 30 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 15
Traits: Demonic Restoration. If the Barlgurak is slain outside the Abyss, its body melts into foul ichor, and its essence reforms instantly somewhere in the Abyss with full Hit Points.
Actions:
Multiattack. The Barlgurak makes one Tormenting Bite attack and two Thrash attacks.

Tormenting Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Piercing damage plus 13 (2d12) Psychic damage.

Spellcasting. The Barlgurak casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Wisdom as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13):; 2/day each: Disguise Self, Invisibility (self only); 1/day each: Entangle, Phantasmal Killer (level 6 version)

Thrash. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is knocked Prone.
Bonus Actions: Leap. The Barlgurak leaps up to 40 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.
Description: Barlguraks are brutal, ape-like demons that stalk the twisted landscapes of the Abyss. Their immense strength, savage instincts, and ability to scale nearly any surface make them relentless hunters. Though not subtle, they are cunning enough to use illusions and ambush tactics, tearing into prey with ferocity and serving as shock troops in demonic conflicts.

Name: Basilisk
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Poison damage.
Bonus Actions: Petrifying Gaze (Recharge 4-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 30-foot Cone. If the basilisk sees its reflection within the Cone, the basilisk must make this save. First Failure: The target has the Restrained condition and repeats the save at the end of its next turn if it is still Restrained, ending the effect on itself on a success. Second Failure: The target has the Petrified condition instead of the Restrained condition.
Description: Basilisks are reptilian monstrosities that are known for their deadly gaze, which can turn creatures to stone. They are often found in mountainous regions and can be a significant threat to adventurers who venture into their territory.

Name: Bat
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Piercing damage.
Description: Bats are small, flying mammals that are known for their echolocation abilities and their nocturnal habits. They are often found in caves, forests, and urban areas, and can be a nuisance to travelers and small settlements.

Name: Barbaru
Senses: Darkvision 120 ft. (unimpeded by magical Darkness), passive Perception 10
Traits: Magic Resistance. The devil has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The devil makes one Beard attack and one Infernal Glaive attack.

Beard. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Piercing damage, and the target becomes Poisoned until the start of the devil’s next turn. While affected by this poison, the target cannot regain Hit Points.

Infernal Glaive. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 10 ft. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) Slashing damage. If the target is a creature and does not already have an infernal wound, it must make a Constitution Saving Throw (DC 12). Failure: The target gains an infernal wound. While wounded, it loses 5 (1d10) Hit Points at the start of each of its turns. The wound closes after 1 minute, after the target receives healing from a spell, or after the target or a creature within 5 feet uses an action to treat the wound and succeeds on a DC 12 Wisdom (Medicine) check.
Description: Barbarus serve as disciplined soldiers within the infernal legions. Their barbed facial tendrils drip with venom that halts healing, making them dangerous foes in prolonged battles. Loyal to the hierarchy of the Nine Hells, they enforce the commands of their superiors with ruthless efficiency and relish opportunities to prove their worth on the battlefield.

Name: Beast of the Land
HP: 5 plus five times your Ranger level (the beast has a number of Hit Dice [d8s] equal to your Ranger level)
CR: None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)
Traits: Primal Bond. Add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check or saving throw the beast makes.
Actions: Beast's Strike. Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d8 + 2 plus your Wisdom modifier Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage (your choice when you summon the beast). If the beast moved at least 20 feet straight toward the target before the hit, the target takes an extra 1d6 damage of the same type, and the target has the Prone condition if it is a Large or smaller creature.
Description: Beasts of the land are loyal animal companions that rangers can summon to aid them in their adventures. They are known for their versatility and their ability to adapt to various environments. Beasts of the land can be found in a wide range of habitats and can provide valuable assistance to rangers in combat and exploration.

Name: Beast of the Sea
HP: 5 plus five times your Ranger level (the beast has a number of Hit Dice [d8s] equal to your Ranger level)
CR: None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)
Traits:
Amphibious. The beast can breathe air and water.

Primal Bond. Add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check or saving throw the beast makes.
Actions: Beast's Strike. Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d6 + 2 plus your Wisdom modifier Bludgeoning or Piercing damage (your choice when you summon the beast), and the target has the Grappled condition (escape DC equals your spell save DC).
Description: Beasts of the sea are loyal aquatic companions that rangers can summon to aid them in their adventures. They are known for their ability to thrive in aquatic environments and their usefulness in underwater exploration and combat. Beasts of the sea can be found in oceans, lakes, and other bodies of water, providing valuable assistance to rangers in aquatic settings.

Name: Beast of the Sky
HP: 4 plus four times your Ranger level (the beast has a number of Hit Dice [d6s] equal to your Ranger level)
CR: None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)
Traits:
Flyby. The beast doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

Primal Bond. Add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check or saving throw the beast makes.
Actions: Beast's Strike. Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d4 + 3 plus your Wisdom modifier Slashing damage.
Description: Beasts of the sky are loyal flying companions that rangers can summon to aid them in their adventures. They are known for their ability to soar through the air and their usefulness in aerial exploration and combat. Beasts of the sky can be found in various environments, providing valuable assistance to rangers in situations where flight is advantageous.

Name: Beithir
Actions:
Multiattack. The Beithir makes one Bite attack and uses Constrict.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 10 ft. Hit: 19 (2d12 + 6) Piercing damage plus 11 (2d10) Lightning damage.

Constrict. Strength Saving Throw: DC 18, one Large or smaller creature the Beithir can see within 5 feet. Failure: 28 (5d8 + 6) Bludgeoning damage. The target becomes Grappled (escape DC 16) and is Restrained until the grapple ends.

Lightning Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 16, each creature in a 90-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 66 (12d10) Lightning damage. Success: Half damage.
Bonus Actions:
Swallow. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 18, one Large or smaller creature Grappled by the Beithir (it can have only one swallowed creature at a time). Failure: The creature is swallowed and no longer Grappled. While swallowed, it is Blinded and Restrained, has Total Cover from effects outside the Beithir, and takes 21 (6d6) Acid damage at the start of each of the Beithir’s turns.

If the Beithir takes 30 or more damage in a single turn from the swallowed creature, it must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or regurgitate the creature, which falls Prone in a space within 10 feet of the Beithir. If the Beithir dies, the swallowed creature is freed and can crawl out using 15 feet of movement, exiting Prone.
Description: Beithirs are massive, lightning-charged serpentine predators that stalk the depths of the Underdark. Their bodies crackle with electrical energy, and they strike with terrifying speed, constricting and swallowing prey whole. These elusive monstrosities lurk in cavernous tunnels and vertical shafts, making them deadly ambushers for any who wander too deep beneath the earth.

Name: Oculus Tyrant
Traits: Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the oculus tyrant fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The oculus tyrant uses Arcane Beams three times.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) Piercing damage.

Arcane Beams. The oculus tyrant unleashes one of the following magical beams at a target it can see within 120 feet (roll 1d10; reroll if the tyrant has already used that beam this turn):1) Beguiling Beam.
