Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 16. Failure: 13 (3d8) Psychic damage, and the target is Charmed for 1 hour or until it takes damage. Success: Half damage only.2) Paralytic Beam. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 16. Failure: The target is Paralyzed and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.3) Terror Beam. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 16. Failure: 14 (4d6) Psychic damage, and the target is Frightened until the end of its next turn. Success: Half damage only.4) Crippling Beam. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 16. Failure: 18 (4d8) Necrotic damage. Until the end of its next turn, the target's Speed is halved, it can't take Reactions, and it can take either an Action or a Bonus Action, not both. Success: Half damage only.5) Withering Beam. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 16. Failure: 13 (3d8) Poison damage, and the target is Poisoned until the end of its next turn. While Poisoned, it can't regain Hit Points. Success: Half damage only.6) Gravitic Beam. Strength Saving Throw: DC 16 (automatic success if Gargantuan). Failure: The tyrant moves the target up to 30 feet in any direction. The target is Restrained until the start of the tyrant's next turn or until the tyrant becomes Incapacitated. The tyrant can also manipulate objects with this beam.7) Slumber Beam. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 16 (automatic success for Constructs or Undead). Failure: The target is Unconscious for 1 minute. The condition ends if the target takes damage or if a creature within 5 feet uses an action to wake it.8) Petrifying Beam. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 16. First Failure: The target is Restrained and repeats the save at the end of its next turn. Second Failure: The target becomes Petrified.9) Dissolution Beam. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 16. Failure: 36 (8d8) Force damage. Nonmagical objects or magical force constructs in a 10-foot cube disintegrate into dust. Success: Half damage. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points, it disintegrates.10) Reaping Beam. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 16. Failure: 55 (10d10) Necrotic damage. Success: Half damage. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points, it dies instantly.
Bonus Actions: Nullfield Gaze. The tyrant's central eye emits a suppressive wave in a 150-foot Cone. Until the start of its next turn, that area functions as an Antimagic Field and also suppresses the tyrant's own Arcane Beams.
Legendary Actions:
Rend. The tyrant makes two Bite attacks.

Menacing Glare. The tyrant uses Arcane Beams.
Regional Effects:
The region around an oculus tyrant's lair is warped by its presence, creating the following effects:

Paranoic Influence. Creatures within 1 mile feel constantly observed. Any creature (excluding the tyrant and its allies) that finishes a Short Rest within this area must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or gain no benefit from the rest.

Reality Flicker. Subtle distortions ripple through the terrain; any creature (excluding the tyrant) within 1 mile that rolls a 1 on a D20 Test gains the Prone condition.

If the oculus tyrant dies or relocates its lair, these effects end immediately.
Description: Oculus tyrants are powerful aberrations that dwell in the Underdark. They are known for their devastating magical beams, their ability to resist magic, and their legendary resistance. Oculus tyrants are often found in the Underdark, where they can pose a significant threat to adventurers who venture into their territory.

Name: Oculus Husk
Languages: Understands Deep Speech and Undercommon but can't speak
Traits: Undead Fortitude. If damage reduces the husk to 0 Hit Points, it makes a Constitution saving throw (DC 5 plus the damage taken) unless the damage is Radiant or from a Critical Hit. On a successful save, the husk drops to 1 Hit Point instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The husk uses Decay Beams twice.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 16 (4d6 + 2) Piercing damage.

Decay Beams. The husk fires one of the following unstable beams at a target it can see within 120 feet (roll 1d4; reroll if the husk has already used that beam this turn):1) Paralysis Beam. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 14. Failure: The target is Paralyzed and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.2) Dread Beam. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 14. Failure: 13 (3d8) Psychic damage, and the target is Frightened until the end of its next turn.3) Rotting Beam. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 14. Failure: 10 (3d6) Necrotic damage, and the target is Poisoned until the end of its next turn. While Poisoned, it can't regain Hit Points. Success: Half damage only.4) Dissolution Beam. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 14. Failure: 27 (5d10) Force damage. If the target is a nonmagical object or a creation of magical force, a 10-foot cube of it disintegrates into dust. Success: Half damage. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points, it disintegrates into dust.
Description: Oculus husks are undead creatures that are created when oculus tyrants die. They are known for their unstable magical beams, their ability to resist death, and their haunting presence. Oculus husks are often found in the Underdark and the Shadowfell, where they can pose a significant threat to adventurers who venture into their territory.

Name: Berserker
Traits: Bloodied Frenzy. While Bloodied, the berserker has Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws.
Actions: Greataxe. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (1d12 + 3) Slashing damage.
Description: Berserkers are fierce warriors who are known for their rage in combat. They are often found in various environments, where they can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them. Berserkers are driven by their bloodlust and can be difficult to reason with, making them dangerous opponents in battle.

Name: Berserker Commander
Traits: Bloodied Frenzy. While Bloodied, the berserker has Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws.
Actions:
Multiattack. The berserker makes three attacks, using Greataxe or Javelin in any combination.

Greataxe. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (1d12 + 4) Slashing damage, plus 10 (3d6) Thunder damage to the target or another creature within 5 feet of the target.

Javelin. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft. Hit: 18 (4d6 + 4) Piercing damage, and the target's Speed decreases by 5 feet until the start of the berserker's next turn.
Bonus Actions: Frenzied Rush. Each ally within 30 feet of the berserker can take a Reaction to move up to half the ally's Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks. The berserker can also move up to half its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.
Description: Berserker commanders are powerful leaders who command berserker warriors in battle. They are known for their strategic prowess, their ability to inspire their allies, and their devastating attacks. Berserker commanders can be found in various environments, where they can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them.

Name: Bestial Spirit
HP: 20 (Air only) or 30 (Land and Water only) + 5 for each spell level above 2
Speed: 30 ft., Climb 30 ft. (Land only), Fly 60 ft. (Air only), Swim 30 ft. (Water only)
CR: None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)
Traits:
Flyby (Air Only). The spirit doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

Pack Tactics (Land and Water Only). The spirit has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the spirit's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

Water Breathing (Water Only). The spirit can breathe only underwater.
Actions:
Multiattack. The spirit makes a number of Rend attacks equal to half this spell's level (round down).

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d8 + 4 + the spell's level Piercing damage.
Description: Bestial spirits are ethereal manifestations of animalistic energy that can be summoned by spellcasters to aid them in combat. They are known for their versatility and their ability to adapt to various environments. Bestial spirits can take on different forms depending on the type of terrain they are summoned in, providing valuable assistance to spellcasters in a wide range of situations.

Name: Black Bear
Actions:
Multiattack. The bear makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Slashing damage.
Description: Black bears are large, powerful animals that can be found in forests and other wooded areas. They are known for their strength and their ability to climb trees. Black bears can pose a threat to adventurers who encounter them in the wild, especially if they feel threatened or if they are protecting their young.

Name: Black Dragon Wyrmling
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 14
Traits: Amphibious. The dragon can breathe air and water.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Slashing damage plus 2 (1d4) Acid damage.

Acid Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 11, each creature in a 15-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 22 (5d8) Acid damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Black dragon wyrmlings are young dragons that are known for their acidic breath and their affinity for swampy environments. They can pose a threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Black Pudding
Condition Immunities: charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, prone, restrained
Traits:
Amorphous. The pudding can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.

Corrosive Form. A creature that hits the pudding with a melee attack roll takes 4 (1d8) Acid damage. Nonmagical ammunition is destroyed immediately after hitting the pudding and dealing any damage. Any nonmagical weapon takes a cumulative -1 penalty to attack rolls immediately after dealing damage to the pudding and coming into contact with it. The weapon is destroyed if the penalty reaches -5. The penalty can be removed by casting the Mending spell on the weapon.

In 1 minute, the pudding can eat through 2 feet of nonmagical wood or metal.

Spider Climb. The pudding can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.
Actions: Dissolving Pseudopod. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 10 ft. Hit: 17 (4d6 + 3) Acid damage. Nonmagical armor worn by the target takes a -1 penalty to the AC it offers. The armor is destroyed if the penalty reduces its AC to 10. The penalty can be removed by casting the Mending spell on the armor.
Reactions: Split. Trigger: While the pudding is Large or Medium and has 10+ Hit Points, it becomes Bloodied or is subjected to Lightning or Slashing damage. Response: The pudding splits into two new Black Puddings. Each new pudding is one size smaller than the original pudding and acts on its Initiative. The original pudding's Hit Points are divided evenly between the new puddings (round down).
Description: Black puddings are large, amorphous oozes that dwell in the Underdark. They are known for their corrosive form, their ability to split when damaged, and their resistance to various types of damage. Black puddings can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Blink Dog
Languages: Understands Elvish and Sylvan but can't speak them
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Piercing damage.
Bonus Actions: Teleport (Recharge 4-6). The dog teleports up to 40 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.
Description: Blink dogs are fey canines gifted with the ability to slip between spaces in the blink of an eye. They roam enchanted forests and the Feywild in tightly bonded packs, using their short-range teleportation to outmaneuver threats and protect their territory. Loyal and intelligent, they coordinate their movements with uncanny precision, making them elusive and challenging opponents.

Name: Ooze of Destruction
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, stunned, unconscious
Traits:
Astral Implosion. When the blob is reduced to 0 Hit Points, its mass collapses inward, ejecting any engulfed creatures or objects into the Astral Sea. The blob itself disappears, leaving a thin layer of corrosive slime across everything within 600 feet. After 1d20 years, it reforms on a random world in the Material Plane.

Legendary Resistance (4/Day). If the blob fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Magic Resistance. The blob has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The blob makes two Pseudopod attacks and uses Engulf. It may replace one attack with Restraining Glob.

Pseudopod. Melee Attack Roll: +15, reach 30 ft. Hit: 24 (3d10 + 8) Force damage.

Engulf. The blob moves up to its Speed and can pass through the spaces of Huge or smaller creatures and objects. Strength Saving Throw: DC 23 for each creature or object whose space it enters during this movement. Failure: The target is engulfed. While engulfed, the target has Total Cover from outside effects, moves with the blob, and nonmagical objects are destroyed after 1 minute.While engulfed, a creature takes 21 (6d6) Force damage at the start of each of its turns, is suffocating, is Restrained, and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns. A creature reduced to 0 Hit Points is disintegrated into ash and expelled into the Astral Sea. Success: The creature escapes into the nearest unoccupied space.

Restraining Glob. The blob hurls a mass of adhesive slime at one Large or smaller creature within 600 feet. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 23. Failure: 18 (3d6 + 8) Acid damage, the target is dragged 60 feet straight toward the blob, and becomes Restrained until the end of its next turn, when the glob dissolves. Success: Half damage only.
Legendary Actions:
Decay. The blob deals 14 (4d6) Necrotic damage to each creature engulfed by it. It cannot use this action again until the start of its next turn.

Grasping Glob. The blob uses Restraining Glob. It cannot use this action again until the start of its next turn.

Lashing Goop. The blob makes one Pseudopod attack.
Description: Oozes of Destruction are titanic oozes capable of consuming nearly anything they touch. Their amorphous bodies sweep across landscapes, dissolving creatures and objects with terrifying efficiency. These entities appear in many environments, leaving devastation in their wake, and are dreaded for their ability to engulf, crush, and utterly erase anything unfortunate enough to be caught in their path.

Name: Blood Hawk
Traits: Pack Tactics. The hawk has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the hawk's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions: Beak. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Piercing damage, or 6 (1d8 + 2) Piercing damage if the target is Bloodied.
Environment: Arctic, Coastal, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Mountain
Description: Blood hawks are small, predatory birds that are known for their keen eyesight and their ability to hunt in packs. They can be found in various environments, where they can pose a threat to adventurers who encounter them. Blood hawks are often used as hunting companions by rangers and other wilderness explorers, but they can also be dangerous if they feel threatened or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Blue Dragon Wyrmling
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 14
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) Slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) Lightning damage.

Lightning Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 30-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 21 (6d6) Lightning damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Blue dragon wyrmlings are young dragons that are known for their lightning breath and their affinity for coastal and desert environments. They can pose a threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Azure Mutant
Traits:
Magic Resistance. The azure mutant has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Regeneration. The azure mutant regains 10 Hit Points at the start of each of its turns as long as it has at least 1 Hit Point.
Actions:
Multiattack. The azure mutant makes three Mutagenic Claw attacks.

Mutagenic Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 10 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) Poison damage. If the target is a Humanoid not already cursed by a slaad, it must make a Constitution Saving Throw (DC 15). Failure: The target becomes cursed. A cursed creature cannot regain Hit Points, and its Hit Point maximum is reduced by 10 (3d6) every 24 hours. This reduction does not heal after a Long Rest. If the curse reduces the creature’s Hit Point maximum to 0, the curse ends and the creature instantly mutates into a Crimson Spawn, or into an Emerald Mutant if it can cast spells of 3rd level or higher. Only a Wish spell can undo this transformation.
Description: Azure mutants are warped slaadi altered by volatile mutagenic energies. Their bodies constantly shift and regenerate, granting them remarkable resilience and resistance to elemental harm. Their claws carry a corruptive curse capable of transforming humanoids into new aberrant forms. Found amid the ever-changing chaos of Limbo, these creatures are unpredictable threats to any who cross their path.

Name: Boar
Traits: Bloodied Fury. While Bloodied, the boar has Advantage on attack rolls.
Actions: Gore. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d6 + 1) Piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature and the boar moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 3 (1d6) Piercing damage and has the Prone condition.
Description: Boars are wild animals that can be found in forests, grasslands, and hills. They are known for their aggressive nature and their ability to charge at their targets with great force. Boars can pose a threat to adventurers who encounter them in the wild, especially if they feel threatened or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Bone Devil
Senses: Darkvision 120 ft. (unimpeded by magical Darkness), passive Perception 12
Traits:
Diabolical Restoration. If the devil dies outside the Nine Hells, its body disappears in sulfurous smoke, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Nine Hells.

Magic Resistance. The devil has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The devil makes two Claw attacks and one Infernal Sting attack.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 10 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Slashing damage.

Infernal Sting. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 10 ft. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) Piercing damage plus 18 (4d8) Poison damage, and the target has the Poisoned condition until the start of the devil's next turn. While Poisoned, the target can't regain Hit Points.
Description: Bone devils are fiendish creatures that serve as enforcers and torturers in the Nine Hells. They are known for their diabolical restoration ability, which allows them to revive after death, and their resistance to cold damage. Bone devils can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Bone Naga
Actions:
Multiattack. The naga makes two Bite attacks. It can replace any attack with a use of Serpentine Gaze.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 10 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Necrotic damage.

Serpentine Gaze. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 13, one creature the naga can see within 60 feet. Failure: 13 (3d6 + 3) Psychic damage, and the target has the Charmed condition until the start of the naga's next turn.

Spellcasting. The naga casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13): At will: Mage Hand, Thaumaturgy; 1/day each: Command, Detect Thoughts, Lightning Bolt
Description: Bone nagas are undead creatures that are created from the remains of powerful spellcasters. They are known for their serpentine gaze, which can charm and damage their targets, and their ability to cast spells. Bone nagas can be found in the Underdark, where they can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them.

Name: Brass Dragon Wyrmling
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 14
Actions:
Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d10 + 2) Slashing damage.

Fire Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 11, each creature in a 20-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 14 (4d6) Fire damage. Success: Half damage.

Sleep Breath. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 11, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: The target has the Incapacitated condition until the end of its next turn, at which point it repeats the save. Second Failure: The target has the Unconscious condition for 1 minute. This effect ends for the target if it takes damage or a creature within 5 feet of it takes an action to wake it.
Description: Brass dragon wyrmlings are young dragons that are known for their fire breath and their affinity for desert environments. They can pose a threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Brazen Gorgon
Traits:
Flame Aura. At the end of each of the gorgon's turns, each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the gorgon takes 13 (3d8) Fire damage.

Illumination. The gorgon sheds Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet.
Actions:
Multiattack. The gorgon makes two Gore attacks.

Gore. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) Fire damage.

Smelting Charge (Recharge 5-6). The gorgon moves up to its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks and can move through the spaces of Medium or smaller creatures. Each time the gorgon enters a creature's space for the first time during this move, that target is subjected to the following effect. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 16. Failure: 13 (2d8 + 4) Piercing damage plus 13 (3d8) Fire damage, and the target is pulled into the gorgon's space and has the Grappled condition (escape DC 14); if the gorgon already has a creature Grappled, the target has the Prone condition instead. Until the grapple ends, the target has the Restrained condition. When the gorgon moves, the Grappled target moves with it, costing no extra movement.
Description: Brazen gorgons are large, metallic constructs that are known for their fiery breath and their ability to petrify their targets. They can be found in various environments, where they can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them. Brazen gorgons are often used as guardians or enforcers by powerful individuals or organizations, and they can be formidable opponents in combat.

Name: Bronze Dragon Wyrmling
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 14
Traits: Amphibious. The dragon can breathe air and water.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) Slashing damage.

Lightning Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 40-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 16 (3d10) Lightning damage. Success: Half damage.

Repulsion Breath. Strength Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 30-foot Cone. Failure: The target is pushed up to 30 feet straight away from the dragon and has the Prone condition.
Description: Bronze dragon wyrmlings are young dragons that are known for their lightning breath and their affinity for coastal environments. They can pose a threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Brown Bear
Actions:
Multiattack. The bear makes one Bite attack and one Claw attack.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Piercing damage.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Slashing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.
Description: Brown bears are large, powerful animals that can be found in various environments, including arctic regions, forests, and hills. They are known for their strength and their ability to climb trees. Brown bears can pose a threat to adventurers who encounter them in the wild, especially if they feel threatened or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Bugbear Stalker
Traits: Abduct. The bugbear needn't spend extra movement to move a creature it is grappling.
Actions:
Multiattack. The bugbear makes two Javelin or Morningstar attacks.

Javelin. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +5, reach 10 ft. or range 30/120 ft. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) Piercing damage.

Morningstar. Melee Attack Roll: +5 (with Advantage if the target is Grappled by the bugbear), reach 10 ft. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) Piercing damage.
Bonus Actions: Quick Grapple. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 13, one Medium or smaller creature the bugbear can see within 10 feet. Failure: The target has the Grappled condition (escape DC 13).
Description: Bugbear stalkers are larger and more aggressive than their warrior counterparts. They are known for their ability to grapple and restrain their targets, as well as their proficiency with javelins and morningstars. Bugbear stalkers can be found in various environments, where they can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them.

Name: Bugbear Warrior
Traits: Abduct. The bugbear needn't spend extra movement to move a creature it is grappling.
Actions:
Grab. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 10 ft. Hit: 9 (2d6 + 2) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 12).

Light Hammer. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +4 (with Advantage if the target is Grappled by the bugbear), reach 10 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 9 (3d4 + 2) Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Bugbear warriors are common members of bugbear tribes. They are known for their ability to grapple and restrain their targets, as well as their proficiency with light hammers. Bugbear warriors can be found in various environments, where they can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them.

Name: Terrashark Ravager
Senses: Darkvision 60 ft., Tremorsense 120 ft., passive Perception 16
Actions:
Multiattack. The terrashark ravager makes two Maw attacks.

Maw. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 17 (2d12 + 4) Piercing damage.

Crushing Pounce. The ravager spends 5 feet of movement to leap to a space within 15 feet that contains one or more Large or smaller creatures. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 15, each creature in the ravager's destination space. Failure: 19 (3d12) Bludgeoning damage, and the target is knocked Prone. Success: Half damage, and the target is pushed 5 feet straight away from the ravager.
Bonus Actions: Burrowing Leap. The terrashark ravager jumps up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement, often bursting from the ground in a spray of earth.
Description: Terrashark ravagers are large, aggressive monsters that inhabit grasslands, hills, and mountains. They are known for their crushing pounce ability and their powerful maw attacks.

Name: Terrashark Whelp
Senses: Darkvision 30 ft., Tremorsense 60 ft., passive Perception 14
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 14 (2d10 + 3) Piercing damage.
Bonus Actions: Burrow Leap. The terrashark whelp jumps up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement, often bursting from loose soil or gravel.
Description: Terrashark whelps are smaller, less aggressive versions of terrashark ravagers. They inhabit grasslands, hills, and mountains, where they can pose a threat to adventurers who encounter them. Terrashark whelps are known for their burrowing leap ability and their powerful bite attacks.

Name: Mireling Bog Sage
Traits:
Amphibious. The mireling can breathe air and water.

Speak with Frogs and Toads. The mireling can communicate simple concepts to frogs and toads when it speaks in Mireling.
Actions:
Multiattack. The mireling makes two Bog Staff attacks. It can replace any attack with a use of Spellcasting to cast Ray of Sickness.

Bog Staff. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Bludgeoning damage plus 10 (3d6) Poison damage.

Spellcasting. The mireling casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13, +5 to hit with spell attacks): At will: Dancing Lights, Druidcraft, Ray of Sickness; 1/day each: Speak with Plants, Vitriolic Sphere
Bonus Actions: Leap. The mireling can jump up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.
Description: Mireling bog sages are amphibious fey mystics who dwell in deep marshes. They commune with the flora and fauna of the wetlands, channeling toxic magic through their carved bog staves. Revered among their kin, they act as guides, healers, and wardens of the swamp’s ancient secrets.

Name: Mireling Skirmisher
Traits:
Amphibious. The mireling can breathe air and water.

Speak with Frogs and Toads. The mireling can communicate simple concepts to frogs and toads when it speaks in Mireling.
Actions: Insectile Rapier. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Piercing damage plus 2 (1d4) Poison damage.
Bonus Actions: Leap. The mireling can jump up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.
Description: Mireling skirmishers are agile fey hunters who patrol the marshlands. Their swift leaps and venom-tipped weapons make them dangerous ambushers. They often serve as scouts or guardians for mireling communities, striking quickly before vanishing into reeds and murky waters.

Name: Cambion
Actions:
Multiattack. The cambion makes two attacks, choosing Claw or Fire Ray in any combination.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Fire damage.

Fire Ray. Ranged Attack Roll: +7, range 120 ft. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) Fire damage.

Spellcasting. The cambion casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14):; 2/day each: Alter Self, Command (level 3 version), Detect Magic; 1/day each: Dominate Person (level 8 version), Plane Shift (self only)
Description: Cambions are the half-fiend offspring of mortals and infernal beings, inheriting both supernatural resilience and a talent for manipulation. Their innate magic and elemental resistances make them dangerous adversaries, while their cunning nature drives them to pursue power wherever they roam. Found in many realms, they often act as agents of darker forces or pursue their own ruthless ambitions.

Name: Camel
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Camels are large, domesticated animals that are commonly found in desert environments. They are known for their ability to carry heavy loads and their resistance to harsh conditions. Camels can pose a threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are provoked or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Grave Maw Crawler
Traits: Wall Scuttle. The grave maw crawler can climb difficult surfaces, including ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.
Actions:
Multiattack. The grave maw crawler uses Numbing Tendrils and makes one Bite attack.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (2d4 + 2) Piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) Poison damage.

Numbing Tendrils. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12, one creature the crawler can see within 10 feet. Failure: The target is Poisoned and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically. While Poisoned, the target is also Paralyzed.
Description: Grave maw crawlers are large, insect-like monstrosities that inhabit the Underdark and urban environments. They are known for their ability to climb walls and ceilings, as well as their numbing tendrils that can poison and paralyze their targets. Grave maw crawlers can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Cat
Traits: Jumper. The cat's jump distance is determined using its Dexterity rather than its Strength.
Actions: Scratch. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Slashing damage.
Description: Cats are small, agile animals that can be found in various environments, including deserts, forests, grasslands, and urban areas. They are known for their stealth and their ability to climb. Cats can pose a minor threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are provoked or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Celestial Spirit
Languages: Celestial, understands the languages you know
CR: None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)
Actions:
Multiattack. The spirit makes a number of attacks equal to half this spell's level (round down).

Radiant Bow (Avenger Only). Ranged Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, range 600 ft. Hit: 2d6 + 2 + the spell's level Radiant damage.

Radiant Mace (Defender Only). Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d10 + 3 + the spell's level Radiant damage, and the spirit can choose itself or another creature it can see within 10 feet of the target. The chosen creature gains 1d10 Temporary Hit Points.

Healing Touch (1/Day). The spirit touches another creature. The target regains Hit Points equal to 2d8 + the spell's level.
Description: Celestial spirits are powerful beings that can be summoned by spellcasters to aid them in combat. They are known for their radiant attacks and their ability to heal allies. Celestial spirits can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their summoner.

Name: Centaur Trooper
Actions:
Multiattack. The centaur makes two attacks, using Pike or Longbow in any combination.

Pike. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) Piercing damage.

Longbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 150/600 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Piercing damage.
Bonus Actions: Trampling Charge (Recharge 5-6). The centaur moves up to its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks and can move through the spaces of Medium or smaller creatures. Each creature whose space the centaur enters is targeted once by the following effect. Strength Saving Throw: DC 14. Failure: 7 (1d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage, and the target has the Prone condition.
Description: Centaur troopers are noble fey creatures that inhabit forests, grasslands, and the Feywild. They are known for their proficiency with pikes and longbows, as well as their ability to charge through enemies with their trampling charge. Centaur troopers can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Centaur Warden
Actions:
Multiattack. The centaur makes two attacks, using Forest Staff or Sun Ray in any combination.

Forest Staff. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Bludgeoning damage plus 14 (4d6) Poison damage.

Spellcasting. The centaur casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15): At will: Druidcraft, Speak with Animals

Sun Ray. Ranged Attack Roll: +7, range 90 ft. Hit: 14 (3d6 + 4) Radiant damage, and the target has the Blinded condition until the start of the centaur's next turn.
Bonus Actions: Entangling Trail (Recharge 5-6). The centaur moves up to its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks. Each creature within 5 feet of the centaur as it moves is targeted once by the following effect. Strength Saving Throw: DC 15. Failure: 11 (2d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage, and the target has the Restrained condition until the end of its next turn.
Description: Centaur wardens are powerful fey creatures that inhabit forests, grasslands, and the Feywild. They are known for their proficiency with forest staffs and their ability to cast spells, as well as their entangling trail ability that can restrain enemies. Centaur wardens can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Chain Devil
Senses: Darkvision 120 ft. (unimpeded by magical Darkness), passive Perception 11
Traits:
Diabolical Restoration. If the devil is slain outside the Nine Hells, its form dissolves into acrid smoke, and its essence reforms instantly somewhere in the Nine Hells with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The devil has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The devil makes two Chain attacks and uses Conjure Infernal Chain.

Chain. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Slashing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it becomes Grappled (escape DC 14) by one of the devil’s chains and is Restrained until the grapple ends.

Conjure Infernal Chain. The devil summons a burning chain to ensnare a creature. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 15, one creature within 60 feet. Failure: 9 (2d4 + 4) Fire damage, and the target becomes Restrained until the end of the devil’s next turn, when the chain vanishes. If the target is Large or smaller, the devil pulls it up to 30 feet straight toward itself. Success: The conjured chain dissipates harmlessly.
Reactions: Unnerving Gaze. Trigger: A creature the devil can see begins its turn within 30 feet and can see the devil. Response—Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 15. Failure: The creature becomes Frightened until the end of its turn. Success: The creature becomes immune to this devil’s Unnerving Gaze for 24 hours.
Description: Chain devils are sinister enforcers of the Nine Hells, wielding animated chains as both weapons and instruments of torment. Their mastery over infernal bindings allows them to restrain foes with terrifying efficiency, while their unnatural presence can instill fear in even seasoned warriors. Often stationed as guardians or interrogators, they defend their domains with ruthless precision.

Name: Chasmek
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 15
Traits:
Demonic Restoration. If the Chasmek is slain outside the Abyss, its body liquefies into foul ichor, and its essence reforms instantly somewhere in the Abyss with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The Chasmek has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Spider Climb. The Chasmek can scale vertical surfaces and ceilings without needing to make an ability check.
Actions: Proboscis. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 16 (4d6 + 2) Piercing damage plus 21 (6d6) Necrotic damage. If the target is a creature, its Hit Point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the Necrotic damage dealt.
Bonus Actions: Drone. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 30-foot Emanation centered on the Chasmek (demons automatically succeed). Failure: The creature falls Unconscious and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns. The creature automatically succeeds after 10 minutes, or if it takes damage, or if a creature within 5 feet uses an action to pour Holy Water on it. Success: The creature becomes immune to this Chasmek’s Drone for 24 hours.
Description: Chasmeks are grotesque, insectoid demons that haunt the depths of the Abyss. Their needle-like proboscis drains vitality from their victims, while their droning buzz can lull entire groups into sudden unconsciousness. Often acting as scouts or tormentors, Chasmeks are relentless predators that thrive in the chaotic, hostile layers of the Abyss.

Name: Chimera
Actions:
Multiattack. The chimera makes one Ram attack, one Bite attack, and one Claw attack. It can replace the Claw attack with a use of Fire Breath if available.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Piercing damage, or 18 (4d6 + 4) Piercing damage if the chimera had Advantage on the attack roll.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) Slashing damage.

Ram. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (1d12 + 4) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

Fire Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 15, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: 31 (7d8) Fire damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Chimeras are monstrous creatures that have the body of a lion, the head of a goat, and the tail of a dragon. They are known for their ferocity and their ability to breathe fire. Chimeras can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Abyssal Clawspawn
Languages: Understands Deep Speech but can't speak
Traits:
Amphibious. The clawspawn can breathe air and water.

Arcane Sense. The clawspawn detects magical auras within 120 feet of itself. This functions like Detect Magic but is not itself magical.
Actions:
Multiattack. The clawspawn makes two Crushing Pincer attacks and uses Paralyzing Tendrils.

Crushing Pincer. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is Grappled (escape DC 14) by one of the clawspawn's pincers.

Paralyzing Tendrils. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 13, one creature Grappled by the clawspawn. Failure: The target is Poisoned and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically. While Poisoned, the target is also Paralyzed.
Description: Abyssal clawspawn are aberrant creatures that inhabit coastal areas, swamps, and the Underdark. They are known for their powerful pincers and their paralyzing tendrils that can immobilize their prey. Abyssal clawspawn can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Clay Golem
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned
Traits:
Acid Absorption. Whenever the golem is subjected to Acid damage, it takes no damage and instead regains a number of Hit Points equal to the Acid damage dealt.

Berserk. Whenever the golem starts its turn Bloodied, roll 1d6. On a 6, the golem goes berserk. On each of its turns while berserk, the golem attacks the nearest creature it can see. If no creature is near enough to move to and attack, the golem attacks an object. Once the golem goes berserk, it continues to be berserk until it is destroyed or it is no longer Bloodied.

Immutable Form. The golem can't shape-shift.

Magic Resistance. The golem has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The golem makes two Slam attacks, or it makes three Slam attacks if it used Hasten this turn.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (1d10 + 5) Bludgeoning damage plus 6 (1d12) Acid damage, and the target's Hit Point maximum decreases by an amount equal to the Acid damage taken.
Bonus Actions: Hasten (Recharge 5-6). The golem takes the Dash and Disengage actions.
Description: Clay golems are powerful constructs that are often created to serve as guardians or enforcers. They are known for their ability to absorb acid damage and their berserk state when they become bloodied. Clay golems can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Umbrashroud
Traits: Shadow Sensitivity. While in Bright Light, the umbrashroud has Disadvantage on attack rolls.
Actions:
Multiattack. The umbrashroud makes one Envelop attack and two Tail Swipe attacks.

Envelop. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is Large or smaller, the umbrashroud coils around it. While enveloping a creature, the target is Blinded, and the umbrashroud cannot use Envelop on another target. The umbrashroud takes half damage (rounded down), and the enveloped creature takes the same amount.

The umbrashroud can release the target by spending 5 feet of movement. A creature within 5 feet of the target can force it free with a successful DC 14 Strength (Athletics) check.

Tail Swipe. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) Slashing damage.
Bonus Actions:
Dread Moan. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 13, each creature in a 60-foot Emanation centered on the umbrashroud. Failure: The creature becomes Frightened until the end of the umbrashroud’s next turn. Success: The creature becomes immune to this umbrashroud’s Dread Moan for 24 hours.

Shadow Phantoms (Recharge after a Short or Long Rest). The umbrashroud casts Mirror Image without components, using Wisdom as its spellcasting ability. The spell ends early if the umbrashroud begins or ends its turn in Bright Light.
Description: Umbrashrouds are eerie, shadow-dwelling aberrations that drift through the caverns of the Underdark. They smother prey beneath their shroud-like bodies, sharing damage with their victims while blinding them in darkness. Their haunting moans can send even seasoned adventurers fleeing in terror, and their affinity for shadow magic makes them elusive foes in the subterranean gloom.

Name: Cloud Giant
Actions:
Multiattack. The giant makes two attacks, using Thunderous Mace or Thundercloud in any combination. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting to cast Fog Cloud.

Thunderous Mace. Melee Attack Roll: +12, reach 10 ft. Hit: 21 (3d8 + 8) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) Thunder damage.

Spellcasting. The giant casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15): At will: Detect Magic, Fog Cloud, Light; 1/day each: Control Weather, Gaseous Form, Telekinesis

Thundercloud. Ranged Attack Roll: +12, range 240 ft. Hit: 18 (3d6 + 8) Thunder damage, and the target has the Incapacitated condition until the end of its next turn.
Bonus Actions: Misty Step. The giant casts the Misty Step spell, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Cloud giants are majestic and powerful beings that inhabit mountainous regions. They are known for their ability to fly and their proficiency with thunderous maces. Cloud giants can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Cockatrice
Actions: Petrifying Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 3 (1d4 + 1) Piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must make a Constitution Saving Throw (DC 11). First Failure: The target becomes Restrained. At the end of its next turn, if still Restrained, it repeats the save, ending the effect on a success. Second Failure: The target becomes Petrified for 24 hours instead of being Restrained.
Description: Cockatrices are small, erratic creatures resembling a twisted blend of rooster and reptile. Their bite carries a strange magical affliction capable of turning victims to stone in moments. Though individually weak, their unpredictable nature and flocking behavior make them dangerous nuisances for travelers wandering through open fields or rural wildlands.

Name: Cockatrice Regent
Traits: Flyby. The cockatrice regent does not provoke Opportunity Attacks when it flies out of a creature’s reach.
Actions:
Multiattack. The cockatrice regent makes one Petrifying Bite attack and two Talons attacks.

Petrifying Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must make a Constitution Saving Throw (DC 14). First Failure: The target becomes Restrained. At the end of its next turn, if still Restrained, it repeats the save, ending the effect on a success. Second Failure: The target becomes Petrified instead of Restrained.

Talons. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 18 (4d6 + 4) Slashing damage.
Reactions: Magical Backlash. Trigger: A creature within 120 feet deals damage to the cockatrice regent. Response—Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 14. Failure: The triggering creature takes 13 (3d6 + 3) Force damage.
Description: Cockatrice regents are imposing, oversized variants of common cockatrices, ruling over flocks with brute strength and instinctive ferocity. Their petrifying bite is far more potent, and their swift aerial strikes allow them to harass foes without exposing themselves to counterattacks. Though unintelligent, their territorial nature and sheer physical power make them formidable threats in open plains.

Name: Colossus
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, stunned, unconscious
Languages: Understands Celestial and Common but can't speak
Traits:
Immutable Form. The colossus cannot be transformed or shape-shifted.

Legendary Resistance (4/Day). If the colossus fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Magic Resistance. The colossus has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Siege Monster. The colossus deals double damage to objects and structures.
Actions:
Multiattack. The colossus makes three attacks, choosing Slam or Radiant Ray in any combination.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +18, reach 20 ft. Hit: 32 (4d10 + 10) Bludgeoning damage, and the target is pushed up to 20 feet directly away from the colossus.

Radiant Ray. Ranged Attack Roll: +18, range 300 ft. Hit: 22 (4d10) Radiant damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is knocked Prone.

Divine Beam (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 26, each creature in a 300-foot-long, 10-foot-wide Line. Failure: 65 (10d12) Radiant damage. Success: Half damage. Failure or Success: A creature reduced to 0 Hit Points by this beam is disintegrated into dust, leaving behind any magic items it carried.
Legendary Actions:
Smite. The colossus makes one Radiant Ray attack.

Stomp. The colossus moves up to half its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks and may make one Slam attack at any point during the movement.
Description: Colossi are monumental constructs forged for war, divine service, or the protection of sacred sites. Towering over the battlefield, they unleash devastating physical and radiant attacks capable of leveling armies and fortifications alike. Though mindless, they follow their creators' commands with absolute precision, making them some of the most fearsome guardians ever built.

Name: Commoner
Traits: Training. The commoner has proficiency in one skill of the DM's choice and has Advantage whenever it makes an ability check using that skill.
Actions: Club. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 2 (1d4) Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Commoners are ordinary people who make up the majority of the population in most settings. They can be farmers, merchants, laborers, or any other type of civilian. Commoners are not typically trained for combat and are often defenseless against threats. However, they can still pose a danger if they are armed or if they outnumber their opponents.

Name: Constrictor Snake
Actions:
Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Piercing damage.

Constrict. Strength Saving Throw: DC 12, one Medium or smaller creature the snake can see within 5 feet. Failure: 7 (3d4) Bludgeoning damage, and the target has the Grappled condition (escape DC 12).
Description: Constrictor snakes are large, powerful serpents that can be found in a variety of environments. They are known for their ability to constrict their prey, dealing damage and immobilizing them. Constrictor snakes can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Construct Spirit
CR: None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)
Traits:
Heated Body (Metal Only). A creature that hits the spirit with a melee attack or that starts its turn in a grapple with the spirit takes 1d10 Fire damage.

Stony Lethargy (Stone Only). When a creature starts its turn within 10 feet of the spirit, the spirit can target it with magical energy if the spirit can see it. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC equals your spell save DC, the target. Failure: Until the start of its next turn, the target can't make Opportunity Attacks, and its Speed is halved.
Actions:
Multiattack. The spirit makes a number of Slam attacks equal to half this spell's level (round down).

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d8 + 4 + the spell's level Bludgeoning damage.
Reactions: Berserk Lashing (Clay Only). Trigger: The spirit takes damage from a creature. Response: The spirit makes a Slam attack against that creature if possible, or the spirit moves up to half its Speed toward that creature without provoking Opportunity Attacks.
Description: Construct spirits are magical constructs that can be summoned by spellcasters using the Conjure Construct spell. They are known for their ability to deal damage with their slam attacks and their unique traits that can affect creatures that attack them or start their turn nearby. Construct spirits can be valuable allies for adventurers who have the ability to summon them, but they can also pose a threat to those who encounter them without warning.

Name: Copper Dragon Wyrmling
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 14
Actions:
Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d10 + 2) Slashing damage.

Acid Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 11, each creature in a 20-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 18 (4d8) Acid damage. Success: Half damage.

Slowing Breath. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 11, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: The target can't take Reactions; its Speed is halved; and it can take either an action or a Bonus Action on its turn, not both. This effect lasts until the end of its next turn.
Description: Copper dragon wyrmlings are young dragons that have just hatched from their eggs. They are known for their playful and mischievous nature, as well as their ability to breathe acid. Copper dragon wyrmlings can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Couatl
Traits: Shielded Mind. The couatl’s thoughts cannot be perceived by any means, and it only allows telepathic communication when it chooses to.
Actions:
Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (1d12 + 5) Piercing damage, and the target becomes Poisoned until the end of the couatl’s next turn.

Constrict. Strength Saving Throw: DC 15, one Medium or smaller creature within 5 feet. Failure: 8 (1d6 + 5) Bludgeoning damage, and the target becomes Grappled (escape DC 13) and Restrained until the grapple ends.

Spellcasting. The couatl casts one of the following spells without components, using Wisdom as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15): At will: Detect Evil and Good, Detect Magic, Detect Thoughts, Shapechange (Beast or Humanoid form only; no Temporary Hit Points gained; no Concentration required); 1/day each: Create Food and Water, Dream, Greater Restoration, Scrying, Sleep
Bonus Actions: Divine Aid (2/Day). The couatl casts Bless, Lesser Restoration, or Sanctuary without components, using the same spellcasting ability as its Spellcasting trait.
Description: Couatl sono celestiali serpiformi venerati come messaggeri e guardiani sacri. Dotati di grande saggezza e poteri magici, comunicano telepaticamente e possono assumere forme mortali per guidare o proteggere gli innocenti. Sebbene raramente aggressivi, difendono con determinazione i luoghi sacri e coloro che giurano di proteggere.

Name: Crab
Traits: Amphibious. The crab can breathe air and water.
Actions: Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Crabs are small, aquatic creatures that can be found in coastal and underwater environments. They are known for their ability to breathe both air and water, as well as their sharp claws that they use for defense and hunting. Crabs can pose a minor threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Crawling Claw
Actions: Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 2 Necrotic damage.
Description: Crawling claws are the severed hands of spellcasters that have been reanimated as undead creatures. They are known for their ability to climb surfaces and their necrotic slam attack. Crawling claws can pose a minor threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Crocodile
Traits: Hold Breath. The crocodile can hold its breath for 1 hour.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 12). While Grappled, the target has the Restrained condition.
Description: Crocodiles are large, aquatic reptiles that can be found in coastal, swamp, and urban environments. They are known for their powerful bite and their ability to hold their breath for extended periods of time. Crocodiles can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Cultist
Actions: Ritual Sickle. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 3 (1d4 + 1) Slashing damage plus 1 Necrotic damage.
Description: Cultists are members of secretive and often dangerous organizations that worship dark deities or pursue sinister goals. They are known for their willingness to do whatever it takes to achieve their objectives, including committing acts of violence and betrayal. Cultists can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Cultist Fanatic
Actions:
Pact Blade. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Necrotic damage.

Spellcasting. The cultist casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12, +4 to hit with spell attacks): At will: Light, Thaumaturgy; 2/day: Command; 1/day: Hold Person
Bonus Actions: Spiritual Weapon (2/Day). The cultist casts the Spiritual Weapon spell, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Cultist fanatics are more devoted and dangerous members of cults. They are known for their willingness to use magic and violence to achieve their goals, as well as their ability to inspire others to follow them. Cultist fanatics can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Cultist Hierophant
Actions:
Multiattack. The cultist makes three attacks, using Pact Blade or Radiant Ray in any combination.

Pact Blade. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Slashing damage plus 18 (4d8) Radiant damage.

Radiant Ray. Ranged Attack Roll: +9, range 120 ft. Hit: 31 (4d12 + 5) Radiant damage.

Spellcasting. The cultist casts one of the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17): At will: Thaumaturgy; 1/day each: Jallarzi's Storm of Radiance (level 7 version), Mass Suggestion
Description: Cultist hierophants are the leaders of cults, often serving as high priests or prophet figures. They are known for their powerful magic and their ability to inspire and command their followers. Cultist hierophants can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Cyclops Oracle
Actions:
Multiattack. The cyclops makes three attacks, choosing Radiant Strike or Flash of Light in any combination.

Radiant Strike. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 10 ft. Hit: 22 (3d10 + 6) Radiant damage.

Flash of Light. Ranged Attack Roll: +10, range 120 ft. Hit: 17 (2d10 + 6) Radiant damage, and the target has Disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of the cyclops’s next turn.

Spellcasting. The cyclops casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Wisdom as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 16):; 2/day each: Arcane Eye, Detect Magic, Locate Object; 1/day: Legend Lore
Reactions: Portent (Recharge 4-6). Trigger: The cyclops or an ally it can see makes a D20 Test. Response: The cyclops rolls 1d20 and chooses whether to replace the triggering roll with its own.
Environment: Coastal, Desert, Grassland, Hill, Mountain, Underdark
Description: Cyclops oracles are towering giants gifted with glimpses of fate and ancient knowledge. Their single eye radiates prophetic power, allowing them to unleash searing bursts of light or divine insight. Though often solitary, they guard sacred sites or hidden lore, and their radiant attacks and divinatory magic make them formidable opponents when roused to anger.

Name: Cyclops Sentry
Actions:
Multiattack. The cyclops makes two attacks, choosing Stone Club or Rock in any combination.

Stone Club. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 10 ft. Hit: 16 (3d6 + 6) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it is knocked Prone.

Rock. Ranged Attack Roll: +9, range 30/120 ft. Hit: 22 (3d10 + 6) Bludgeoning damage.
Reactions: Limited Foresight (Recharge 6). Trigger: A creature the cyclops can see makes an attack roll against it. Response: The cyclops imposes Disadvantage on that roll and gains Advantage on attack rolls against the triggering creature until the end of its next turn.
Environment: Coastal, Desert, Grassland, Hill, Mountain, Underdark
Description: Cyclops sentries serve as hulking guardians for giant clans and remote territories. Though not gifted with great insight, their raw strength and instinctive battle sense make them dangerous foes. Their crushing blows can topple even large creatures, and brief flashes of intuition allow them to evade attacks and retaliate with surprising accuracy.

Name: Dao
Traits:
Earth Glide. The dao can move through natural, unworked earth and stone without disturbing the material.

Elemental Restoration. If the dao dies outside the Elemental Plane of Earth, its body crumbles into soil, and it reforms with full Hit Points somewhere on the Plane of Earth after 1d4 days.

Magic Resistance. The dao has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Wishes. The dao has a 30 percent chance of knowing the Wish spell. If it does, it can cast the spell only to fulfill the spoken desire of a non-genie creature. The dao suffers no strain from casting it this way. After casting Wish three times, it cannot do so again for 365 days.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dao makes three Earthen Maul attacks or two Earth Burst attacks.

Earthen Maul. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 5 ft. Hit: 20 (4d6 + 6) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is knocked Prone.

Earth Burst. Ranged Attack Roll: +10, range 120 ft. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) Bludgeoning damage. Hit or Miss: The ground erupts from the target’s space. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 16, each creature in a 10-foot Emanation centered on the target. Failure: 10 (3d6) Thunder damage.

Spellcasting. The dao casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 16): At will: Detect Evil and Good, Detect Magic, Stone Shape; 1/day each: Gaseous Form, Invisibility, Move Earth, Passwall, Plane Shift, Tongues, Wall of Stone
Description: The Dao are earth spirits, formed from living rock and minerals. They can pass through stone as if it were air and unleash violent earth eruptions against their enemies. Proud and powerful, they rule over underground realms and treat strangers with caution, but their magic makes them formidable foes and valuable allies.

Name: Umbracap
Actions:
Crushing Shroud. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Bludgeoning damage, and the umbracap attaches to the target. If the target is Medium or smaller and the umbracap had Advantage on the attack roll, it envelops the target's head, imposing the Blinded condition and causing the target to suffocate while attached.

While attached, the umbracap can attack only the creature it is covering, but it has Advantage on its attack rolls. Its Speed becomes 0, it can't benefit from bonuses to Speed, and it moves with the target.

A creature can use an action to attempt to tear the umbracap free with a successful DC 13 Strength (Athletics) check. The umbracap can detach itself on its turn by using 5 feet of movement.

Umbral Veil (1/Day). Magical darkness fills a 15-foot Emanation originating from the umbracap. The effect lasts while the umbracap maintains Concentration, up to 10 minutes. Darkvision can't penetrate this darkness, and no light can illuminate it.
Description: Umbracaps are small, flying aberrations that are often found in the Underdark. They are known for their ability to attach themselves to creatures and impose the Blinded condition, as well as their ability to create magical darkness. Umbracaps can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Death Cultist
Actions:
Multiattack. The cultist makes three attacks, using Dread Scythe or Deathly Ray in any combination.

Dread Scythe. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) Slashing damage plus 11 (2d10) Necrotic damage, and the target can't regain Hit Points until the end of its next turn.

Deathly Ray. Ranged Attack Roll: +6, range 120 ft. Hit: 22 (4d10) Necrotic damage.

Spellcasting. The cultist casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14): At will: Speak with Dead, Thaumaturgy
Bonus Actions: Spirit Wail (Recharge 5-6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 14, each creature in a 20-foot Emanation originating from the cultist. Failure: 14 (4d6) Psychic damage, and the target has the Frightened condition until the end of its next turn. Success: Half damage only.
Description: Death cultists are members of cults that worship death and seek to bring about the end of life. They are known for their powerful necrotic attacks and their ability to cast spells that can manipulate the dead. Death cultists can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Death Dog
Condition Immunities: blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, stunned, unconscious
Actions:
Multiattack. The death dog makes two Bite attacks.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it is subjected to the following effect. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12. First Failure: The target has the Poisoned condition. While Poisoned, the target's Hit Point maximum doesn't return to normal when finishing a Long Rest, and it repeats the save every 24 hours that elapse, ending the effect on itself on a success. Subsequent Failures: The Poisoned target's Hit Point maximum decreases by 5 (1d10).
Description: Death dogs are monstrous canines that are often associated with death and decay. They are known for their poisonous bite and their ability to resist certain conditions. Death dogs can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Death Knight
Traits:
Legendary Resistance (3/Day). If the death knight fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Magic Resistance. The death knight has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Marshal Undead. Undead of the knight’s choice within 60 feet gain Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws while the knight is not Incapacitated.

Undead Restoration. If destroyed before redeeming its past sins, the death knight reforms a new body in 1d10 days at a site tied to its former life.
Actions:
Multiattack. The death knight makes three Dread Blade attacks.

Dread Blade. Melee Attack Roll: +11, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Slashing damage plus 13 (3d8) Necrotic damage.

Hellfire Orb (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 18, 20-foot-radius Sphere within 120 feet. Failure: 35 (10d6) Fire damage plus 35 (10d6) Necrotic damage. Success: Half damage.

Spellcasting. The death knight casts one of the following spells using Charisma (DC 18): At will: Command, Phantom Steed; 2/day each: Destructive Wave (Necrotic), Dispel Magic
Reactions: Parry. When hit by a melee attack while holding a weapon, the death knight adds 6 to its AC against that attack.
Legendary Actions:
Dread Authority. The death knight casts Command.

Fell Word. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 18, one creature within 120 feet. Failure: 17 (5d6) Necrotic damage and the target’s Hit Point maximum is reduced by the same amount.

Lunge. The death knight moves up to half its Speed and makes one Dread Blade attack.
Description: Death knights are powerful undead warriors that are often associated with dark magic and evil deeds. They are known for their powerful necrotic attacks, their ability to command other undead, and their resistance to certain conditions. Death knights can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Death Knight Aspirant
Traits:
Magic Resistance. The aspirant has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Marshal Undead. Undead of the aspirant’s choice within 60 feet gain Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws while it is not Incapacitated.
Actions:
Multiattack. The aspirant makes three Dread Blade attacks.

Dread Blade. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) Slashing damage plus 10 (3d6) Necrotic damage.

Hellfire Orb (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 15, 20-foot-radius Sphere within 120 feet. Failure: 21 (6d6) Fire damage plus 21 (6d6) Necrotic damage. Success: Half damage.

Spellcasting. The aspirant casts one of the following spells using Charisma (DC 15): At will: Phantom Steed; 1/day each: Destructive Wave (Necrotic), Dispel Magic
Reactions: Parry. When hit by a melee attack while holding a weapon, the aspirant adds 4 to its AC against that attack.
Description: Death knight aspirants are undead warriors who are on the path to becoming full-fledged death knights. They are known for their powerful necrotic attacks, their ability to command other undead, and their resistance to certain conditions. Death knight aspirants can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Voidspawn Mutant
Traits:
Magic Resistance. The voidspawn mutant has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Regeneration. The mutant regains 10 Hit Points at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 Hit Point.

Innate Spellcasting. The mutant's innate spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 15). It can innately cast the following spells, requiring no components: At will: Detect Magic, Mage Hand; 3/day each: Fear, Fireball, Fly, Invisibility; 1/day each: Plane Shift (self only), Finger of Death
Actions:
Multiattack. The voidspawn mutant makes three Claw attacks.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 10 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Slashing damage plus 10 (3d6) Necrotic damage.
Description: Voidspawn mutants are aberrations that have been twisted and mutated by the chaotic energies of the Limbo. They are known for their powerful attacks, their ability to regenerate, and their innate spellcasting abilities. Voidspawn mutants can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Death Oculus Tyrant
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone
Traits: Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the death oculus tyrant fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The death oculus tyrant uses Eye Rays three times.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 feet. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Piercing damage.

Eye Rays. The death oculus tyrant randomly shoots one of the following magical rays at a target it can see within 120 feet of itself (roll 1d10; reroll if the death oculus tyrant has already used that ray during this turn):1) Charm Ray. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 17. Failure: 13 (3d8) Psychic damage, and the target has the Charmed condition for 1 hour or until it takes damage. Success: Half damage only.2) Paralyzing Ray. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 17. Failure: The target has the Paralyzed condition and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.3) Fear Ray. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 17. Failure: 10 (3d6) Psychic damage, and the target has the Frightened condition until the end of its next turn. Success: Half damage only.4) Slowing Ray. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 17. Failure: 18 (4d8) Necrotic damage. Until the end of the target's next turn, the target can't take Reactions; its Speed is halved; and it can take either an action or a Bonus Action on its turn, not both. Success: Half damage only.5) Enervation Ray. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 17. Failure: 16 (3d10) Poison damage, and the target has the Poisoned condition until the end of its next turn. While Poisoned, the target can't regain Hit Points. Success: Half damage only.6) Telekinetic Ray. Strength Saving Throw: DC 17 (the target succeeds automatically if it is Gargantuan). Failure: The death oculus tyrant moves the target up to 30 feet in any direction. The target has the Restrained condition until the start of the death oculus tyrant's next turn or until the death oculus tyrant has the Incapacitated condition. The death oculus tyrant can also exert fine control on objects with this ray, such as manipulating a tool or opening a door or container.7) Sleep Ray. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 17 (the target succeeds automatically if it is a Construct or an Undead). Failure: The target has the Unconscious condition for 1 minute. The condition ends if the target takes damage or a creature within 5 feet of it takes an action to wake it.8) Petrification Ray. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 17. First Failure: The target has the Restrained condition and repeats the save at the end of its next turn if it is still Restrained, ending the effect on itself on a success. Second Failure: The target has the Petrified condition instead of the Restrained condition.9) Disintegration Ray. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 17. Failure: 36 (8d8) Force damage. If the target is a nonmagical object or a creation of magical force, a 10-foot Cube of it disintegrates into dust. Success: Half damage. Failure or Success: If the target is a creature and this damage reduces it to 0 Hit Points, it disintegrates into dust.10) Death Ray. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 17. Failure: 55 (10d10) Necrotic damage. Success: Half damage. Failure or Success: The target dies if the ray reduces it to 0 Hit Points.
Bonus Actions: Negative Energy Cone. The death oculus tyrant's central eye emits an imperceptible, magical wave of negative energy in a 150-foot Cone. Creatures in that area can't regain Hit Points until the start of the death oculus tyrant's next turn. An intact Humanoid corpse there instantly rises as a Zombie under the death oculus tyrant's control and takes its turn immediately after the death oculus tyrant on the same initiative count.
Legendary Actions:
Chomp. The death oculus tyrant makes two Bite attacks.

Glare. The death oculus tyrant uses Eye Rays.
Regional Effects:
The region containing a death oculus tyrant's lair is warped by its presence, creating the following effects:

Negative Energy Suffusion. Whenever a creature within 1 mile of the lair regains Hit Points from a spell, it subtracts 1d10 from the number of Hit Points regained.

Scopophobia. Creatures within 1 mile of the lair feel as if they're being watched. Any creature (excluding the death oculus tyrant and its allies) that finishes a Short Rest while within 1 mile of the lair must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or gain no benefit from that rest.

If the death oculus tyrant dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.
Description: A death oculus tyrant is the undead remnant of a once-living ocular tyrant whose obsession with domination outlived its flesh. Its floating skull radiates malevolent energy, and each spectral eye socket channels a different form of destructive magic. These beings drift through the dark like silent executioners, raising corpses with a glance and warping the land around their lairs with oppressive, watchful dread.

Name: Deer
Traits: Agile. The deer doesn't provoke an Opportunity Attack when it moves out of an enemy's reach.
Actions: Ram. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 2 (1d4) Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Deer are graceful and agile creatures that are often found in forests and grasslands. They are known for their speed and their ability to evade predators. Deer can pose a minor threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Demilich
Condition Immunities: charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, stunned
Traits:
Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the demilich fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Undead Restoration. If the demilich is destroyed, its essence reforms and it regains all its Hit Points in 1d10 days unless a Wish spell is cast on its remains.
Actions:
Multiattack. The demilich makes three Necrotic Burst attacks.

Necrotic Burst. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +11, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 24 (7d6) Necrotic damage.

Howl (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 19, each creature in a 30-foot Emanation centered on the demilich. Failure: 70 (20d6) Psychic damage. Failure or Success: The creature becomes Frightened until the start of the demilich’s next turn.
Legendary Actions:
Energy Drain. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 19, one creature the demilich can see within 120 feet. Failure: The target’s Hit Point maximum is reduced by 14 (4d6). Failure or Success: The demilich can’t use this action again until the start of its next turn.

Grave-Dust Flight. The demilich moves up to its Fly Speed, scattering necrotic dust. Each creature within 5 feet during this movement must make a DC 19 Constitution saving throw. Failure: The creature is Blinded until the end of the demilich’s next turn. Failure or Success: The demilich can’t use this action again until the start of its next turn.

Necrosis. The demilich makes one Necrotic Burst attack.
Regional Effects:
The demilich’s lair warps the surrounding area, producing the following effects:
