Enervating Domain. Any creature other than the demilich or its allies that finishes a Long Rest within 1 mile of the lair must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or have its Hit Point maximum reduced by 1d4. This reduction lasts until the creature completes a Long Rest outside the area.

Travel Ward. Teleportation and planar travel cannot be used to enter or leave the lair.

If the demilich dies or relocates its lair, these effects end immediately.
Description: Demiliches are the sentient remnants of powerful undead spellcasters, reduced to fragments of their former selves yet still imbued with dark magic. Their psychic screams and necrotic attacks can wipe out entire groups, while their immortal essence allows them to reform even after destruction. These ethereal entities pose an extreme threat to anyone who dares disturb their ancient resting places.

Name: Deva
Traits:
Exalted Restoration. If the deva is slain outside Mount Celestia, its form vanishes in a flash of light, and it instantly reforms somewhere on Mount Celestia with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The deva has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The deva makes two Holy Mace attacks.

Holy Mace. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage plus 18 (4d8) Radiant damage.

Spellcasting. The deva casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17): At will: Detect Evil and Good, Shapechange (Beast or Humanoid form only; no Temporary Hit Points gained; no Concentration required); 1/day each: Commune, Raise Dead
Bonus Actions: Divine Aid (2/Day). The deva casts Cure Wounds, Lesser Restoration, or Remove Curse, using the same spellcasting ability as its Spellcasting trait.
Description: Deva are celestial emissaries who embody purity, justice, and compassion. Armed with sacred powers and an unshakable will, they protect mortals and punish evil wherever they find it. Their weapons radiate divine light, and their magic can heal, purify, or bring the innocent back to life. When acting as guardians or messengers of the gods, their presence is a beacon of hope for allies and a terrible warning to the wicked.

Name: Dire Wolf
Traits: Pack Tactics. The wolf has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the wolf's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.
Description: Dire wolves are large and powerful canines that are often found in forests and hills. They are known for their strength, speed, and pack tactics. Dire wolves can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Dire Worg
Traits: Magic Resistance. The dire worg has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dire worg makes three Bite attacks.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 5 ft. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Poison damage. The target becomes Poisoned until the start of the dire worg's next turn and can't regain Hit Points while Poisoned.

Dreadful Howl (Recharge 5-6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 16, each creature within 30 feet that isn't a worg. Failure: 36 (8d8) Psychic damage and the Frightened condition until the start of the dire worg's next turn. Success: Half damage.
Bonus Actions: Warp Step. The dire worg bends the fey-touched terrain and teleports, along with a willing creature within 5 feet, up to 30 feet to a space it can see.
Description: Dire worgs are large and powerful fey creatures that are often found in forests, grasslands, hills, and the Feywild. They are known for their strength, speed, and magical abilities. Dire worgs can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Phantom Prowler
Traits:
Evasion. If the phantom prowler is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage on a success and half damage on a failure. It can't use this trait if it is Incapacitated.

Phantom Mirage. Attack rolls against the phantom prowler have Disadvantage due to its shifting afterimages that obscure its true position. This trait is suppressed while the prowler is Incapacitated.
Actions:
Multiattack. The phantom prowler makes one Rending Swipe attack and one Barbed Tendril attack.

Rending Swipe. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 feet. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) Slashing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is knocked Prone.

Barbed Tendril. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 feet. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Piercing damage.
Description: Phantom prowlers are large and dangerous monstrosities that are often found in forests. They are known for their powerful attacks, their ability to evade damage, and their shifting afterimages that make them difficult to hit. Phantom prowlers can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Djinni
Traits:
Elemental Restoration. If the djinni perishes outside the Elemental Plane of Air, its form disperses into vapor, and it reforms with full Hit Points somewhere on the Plane of Air after 1d4 days.

Magic Resistance. The djinni has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Wishes. The djinni has a 30 percent chance of knowing the Wish spell. If it does, it can cast the spell only to fulfill the spoken desire of a non-genie creature. The djinni suffers no strain from casting Wish this way. After casting it three times, the djinni cannot cast it again for 365 days.
Actions:
Multiattack. The djinni makes three attacks, choosing Storm Blade or Storm Bolt in any combination.

Storm Blade. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Lightning damage.

Spellcasting. The djinni casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17): At will: Detect Evil and Good, Detect Magic; 2/day each: Create Food and Water (may create wine instead of water), Tongues, Wind Walk; 1/day each: Creation, Gaseous Form, Invisibility, Major Image, Plane Shift

Storm Bolt. Ranged Attack Roll: +9, range 120 ft. Hit: 13 (3d8) Thunder damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is knocked Prone.

Create Whirlwind. The djinni summons a whirlwind at a point it can see within 120 feet. The whirlwind fills a 20-foot-radius, 60-foot-high Cylinder and lasts while the djinni maintains Concentration. At the start of each of its turns, the djinni can move the whirlwind up to 20 feet.

Whenever the whirlwind enters a creature’s space or a creature enters it, that creature must make a Strength Saving Throw (DC 17; only once per turn). Failure: The creature becomes Restrained while inside the whirlwind and moves with it. At the start of each of its turns, the creature takes 21 (6d6) Thunder damage. At the end of each of its turns, it repeats the save, ending the effect on a success.
Description: Djinni are spirits of the air, known for their free-spirited and unpredictable nature. They command winds and storms, unleashing lightning and thunder upon their enemies. Some possess the power to grant wishes, a gift as precious as it is dangerous. Although generally benevolent, they fiercely defend their domains and can become formidable foes when provoked.

Name: Doppelganger
Actions:
Multiattack. The doppelganger makes two Slam attacks and uses Unsettling Visage if available.

Read Thoughts. The doppelganger casts Detect Thoughts, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12).

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +6 (with Advantage during the first round of each combat), reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage.

Unsettling Visage (Recharge 6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 15-foot Emanation originating from the doppelganger that can see the doppelganger. Failure: The target has the Frightened condition and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.
Bonus Actions: Shape-Shift. The doppelganger shape-shifts into a Medium or Small Humanoid, or it returns to its true form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.
Description: Doppelgangers are shape-shifting monstrosities that are often found in urban environments and the Underdark. They are known for their ability to mimic the appearance and mannerisms of other creatures, making them excellent infiltrators and spies. Doppelgangers can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Dracolich
Senses: Blindsight 60 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 24
Traits:
Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the dracolich fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Life Suppression. Creatures within 60 feet of the dracolich cannot regain Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The dracolich has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Soul Gem. The dracolich binds its essence to a magical gem. If its body is destroyed while the gem remains on the same plane, the dracolich reforms in 1d20 days with full Hit Points, appearing within 5 feet of the gem.

The gem is a Tiny object with AC 20, 50 Hit Points, and Immunity to Necrotic, Poison, and Psychic damage. It regains all Hit Points at the end of every turn. If reduced to 0 Hit Points, it crumbles to dust. The dracolich can craft a new gem through an 8-hour ritual that consumes a gem worth at least 1,000 GP and an additional 5,000 GP.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dracolich makes three Rend attacks. It may replace one attack with Spellcasting to cast Ray of Sickness (level 2 version).

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +13, reach 10 ft. Hit: 18 (2d10 + 7) Slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) Necrotic damage.

Necrotic Breath (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 20, each creature in a 60-foot Cone. Failure: 52 (8d12) Necrotic damage. Success: Half damage.

Spellcasting. The dracolich casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 19, +11 to hit with spell attacks): At will: Detect Magic, Ray of Sickness (level 2 version); 1/day each: Create Undead (level 8 version), Finger of Death
Legendary Actions:
Pounce. The dracolich moves up to half its Speed and makes one Rend attack.

Sickening Ray. The dracolich casts Ray of Sickness (level 2 version). It cannot use this action again until the start of its next turn.

Terrifying Presence. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 19, each creature in a 30-foot Emanation centered on the dracolich. Failure: 11 (2d10) Psychic damage, and the creature becomes Frightened until the end of the dracolich’s next turn. Failure or Success: The dracolich cannot use this action again until the start of its next turn.
Regional Effects:
A dracolich’s lair corrupts the surrounding region, producing the following effects:

Sapping Mist. A pale fog lightly obscures the area within 1 mile of the lair. Any creature other than the dracolich or its allies that finishes a Long Rest there must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or gain 1 level of Exhaustion.

Soul Drain. Creatures within 1 mile of the lair have Disadvantage on Death Saving Throws.

If the dracolich is destroyed or relocates its lair, these effects end immediately. They resume if the dracolich reforms via its Soul Gem.
Description: Dracoliches are dragons that have embraced undeath to gain eternal power. Their animated bones emit an aura of death that weakens all nearby life forms, while their necrotic attacks can wipe out entire groups. Bound to a gem that holds their soul, they are able to be reborn even after their body is destroyed, making them among the most feared creatures in the world.

Name: Draconic Spirit
Senses: Blindsight 30 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 12
Languages: Draconic, understands the languages you know
CR: None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)
Traits: Shared Resistances. When you summon the spirit, choose one of its Resistances. You have Resistance to the chosen damage type until the spell ends.
Actions:
Multiattack. The spirit makes a number of Rend attacks equal to half the spell's level (round down), and it uses Breath Weapon.

Rend. Melee Attack: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 10 feet. Hit: 1d6 + 4 + the spell's level Piercing damage.

Breath Weapon. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC equals your spell save DC, each creature in a 30-foot Cone. Failure: 2d6 damage of a type this spirit has Resistance to (your choice when you cast the spell). Success: Half damage.
Description: Draconic spirits are powerful manifestations of draconic energy that can be summoned by spellcasters. They are known for their powerful attacks, their ability to grant resistance to certain types of damage, and their connection to the draconic forces of the world. Draconic spirits can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Draft Horse
Actions: Hooves. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d4 + 4) Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Draft horses are large and powerful beasts that are often used for pulling heavy loads. They are known for their strength and endurance. Draft horses can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Dragon Turtle
Traits: Amphibious. The dragon can breathe air and water.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes three Bite attacks. It can replace one attack with a Tail attack.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +13, reach 15 ft. Hit: 23 (3d10 + 7) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Fire damage. Being underwater doesn't grant Resistance to this Fire damage.

Tail. Melee Attack Roll: +13, reach 15 ft. Hit: 18 (2d10 + 7) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

Steam Breath (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 19, each creature in a 60-foot Cone. Failure: 56 (16d6) Fire damage. Success: Half damage. Failure or Success: Being underwater doesn't grant Resistance to this Fire damage.
Description: Dragon turtles are massive and powerful aquatic dragons that are often found in coastal and underwater environments. They are known for their strength, their powerful attacks, and their resistance to fire. Dragon turtles can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Dretch
Languages: Abyssal; telepathy 60 ft. (works only with creatures that understand Abyssal)
Actions:
Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d6 + 1) Slashing damage.

Fetid Cloud (1/Day). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 11, each creature in a 10-foot Emanation centered on the dretch. Failure: The creature becomes Poisoned until the end of its next turn. While Poisoned, the creature can take only an action or a Bonus Action on its turn, not both, and it cannot take Reactions.
Description: Dretches are among the most wretched and weakest demons of the Abyss—stinking, brutal creatures driven solely by rage and instinct. Though not very intelligent, their foul-smelling cloud can hinder even seasoned adventurers, especially when they gather in packs. Their resistance to elemental damage makes them a nuisance to deal with, despite their lack of individual strength.

Name: Arachneid
Traits:
Wall-Skitter. The arachneid can climb difficult surfaces, including ceilings, without needing an ability check.

Light Aversion. While in sunlight, the arachneid has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.

Websense. The arachneid ignores movement restrictions caused by webs and instinctively perceives any creature touching the same web network.
Actions:
Multiattack. The arachneid makes three attacks, using Piercing Limb or Venom Spit in any combination.

Piercing Limb. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Piercing damage.

Venom Spit. Ranged Attack Roll: +6, range 120 ft. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) Poison damage.
Bonus Actions: Weaver’s Shroud (Recharge 5-6). The arachneid manipulates its innate web-magic to cast Darkness, Faerie Fire, or Web without components, using Wisdom (DC 14).
Description: Arachneids are large and dangerous monstrosities that are often found in forests and the Underdark. They are known for their powerful attacks, their ability to climb difficult surfaces, and their resistance to certain types of damage. Arachneids can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Druid
Actions:
Multiattack. The druid makes two attacks, using Vine Staff or Verdant Wisp in any combination.

Vine Staff. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Bludgeoning damage plus 2 (1d4) Poison damage.

Spellcasting. The druid casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13): At will: Druidcraft, Speak with Animals; 2/day each: Entangle, Thunderwave; 1/day each: Animal Messenger, Longstrider, Moonbeam

Verdant Wisp. Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 90 ft. Hit: 10 (3d6) Radiant damage.
Description: Druids are powerful spellcasters who are deeply connected to nature. They are known for their ability to cast spells that manipulate the natural world, their connection to animals, and their ability to heal others. Druids can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Dryad
Traits:
Magic Resistance. The dryad has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Speak with Beasts and Plants. The dryad can converse with Beasts and Plants as though sharing a common language.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dryad makes one Vine Lash or Thorn Burst attack, and it may use Spellcasting to cast Charm Monster.

Vine Lash. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) Slashing damage.

Spellcasting. The dryad casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14): At will: Animal Friendship, Charm Monster (lasts 24 hours; ends early if the dryad casts it again), Druidcraft; 1/day each: Entangle, Pass without Trace

Thorn Burst. Ranged Attack Roll: +6, range 60 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) Piercing damage.
Bonus Actions: Tree Stride. While within 5 feet of a Large or larger tree, the dryad teleports to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of another Large or larger tree within 60 feet.
Description: Dryads are woodland spirits deeply connected to the trees around them. Thanks to their natural charm and innate magic, they can influence hostile creatures, move swiftly through the trees, and manipulate the vegetation. Although they are not formidable fighters, their cunning and mastery of the terrain make them treacherous foes to anyone who threatens the forest.

Name: Dust Shardling
Traits: Shatter Burst. The shardling bursts into razor-like dust when it dies. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 10, each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the shardling. Failure: 5 (2d4) Bludgeoning damage. Success: Half damage.
Actions:
Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Slashing damage.

Blinding Gust (Recharge 6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 10, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: The target is Blinded until the end of the shardling's next turn.

Sleep Dust (1/Day). The shardling casts the Sleep spell, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 10).
Description: Dust shardlings are small and dangerous elemental creatures that are often found in the Elemental Planes. They are known for their ability to burst into razor-like dust when they die, their ability to blind others, and their resistance to certain types of damage. Dust shardlings can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Eagle
Actions: Talons. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 feet. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Slashing damage.
Description: Eagles are majestic birds of prey that are often found in coastal, grassland, hill, and mountain environments. They are known for their keen eyesight, their powerful talons, and their ability to fly. Eagles can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Earth Elemental
Senses: Darkvision 60 ft., Tremorsense 60 ft., passive Perception 10
Traits:
Earth Glide. The elemental can burrow through nonmagical, unworked earth and stone. While doing so, the elemental doesn't disturb the material it moves through.

Siege Monster. The elemental deals double damage to objects and structures.
Actions:
Multiattack. The elemental makes two attacks, using Slam or Rock Launch in any combination.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 10 ft. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) Bludgeoning damage.

Rock Launch. Ranged Attack Roll: +8, range 60 ft. Hit: 8 (1d6 + 5) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.
Environment: Mountain, Planar (Elemental Plane of Earth), Underdark
Description: Earth elementals are powerful and resilient creatures that are often found in mountainous, planar, and underground environments. They are known for their ability to burrow through earth and stone, their powerful attacks, and their resistance to certain types of damage. Earth elementals can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Efreeti
Traits:
Elemental Restoration. If the efreeti dies outside the Elemental Plane of Fire, its form collapses into glowing embers, and it reforms with full Hit Points somewhere on the Plane of Fire after 1d4 days.

Magic Resistance. The efreeti has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Wishes. The efreeti has a 30 percent chance of knowing the Wish spell. If it does, it can cast the spell only to fulfill the spoken desire of a non-genie creature. The efreeti suffers no strain from casting Wish this way. After casting it three times, it cannot cast it again for 365 days.
Actions:
Multiattack. The efreeti makes three attacks, choosing Heated Blade or Hurl Flame in any combination.

Heated Blade. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 6) Slashing damage plus 13 (2d12) Fire damage.

Hurl Flame. Ranged Attack Roll: +8, range 120 ft. Hit: 24 (7d6) Fire damage.

Spellcasting. The efreeti casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 16): At will: Detect Magic, Elementalism; 1/day each: Gaseous Form, Invisibility, Major Image, Plane Shift, Tongues, Wall of Fire (level 7 version)
Description: Efreet are fire genies, feared for their brutal strength and mastery of flames. They inhabit scorching deserts and cities forged from lava, where they wield power and cunning in equal measure. Their blazing weapons and fiery magic make them formidable foes, while their ability to grant wishes makes them figures as sought-after as they are dangerous.

Name: Elemental Cataclysm
Speed: 60 ft., Burrow 60 ft., Fly 80 ft. (hover), Swim 80 ft.
Damage Immunities: acid, cold, fire, lightning, poison, thunder
Condition Immunities: blinded, charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, stunned, unconscious
Traits:
Earth Glide. The cataclysm can burrow through nonmagical, unworked earth and stone. While doing so, the cataclysm doesn't disturb the material it moves through.

Legendary Resistance (4/Day). If the cataclysm fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Siege Monster. The cataclysm deals double damage to objects and structures.
Actions:
Multiattack. The cataclysm makes two Elemental Burst attacks.

Control Weather. The cataclysm casts the Control Weather spell, requiring no spell components and using Constitution as the spellcasting ability.

Elemental Burst. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +15, reach 30 ft. or range 150 ft. Hit: 25 (5d6 + 8) damage of a type chosen by the cataclysm: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder.

Cataclysmic Event (Recharge 4-6). The cataclysm creates one of the following effects at random (roll 1d4):1) Clinging Flames. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 23, each creature in a 60-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point the cataclysm can see within 150 feet. Failure: 45 (13d6) Fire damage. Success: Half damage. Failure or Success: The target starts burning.2) Freezing Waves. Strength Saving Throw: DC 23, each creature in a 90-foot Cone. Failure: 22 (5d8) Bludgeoning damage plus 22 (5d8) Cold damage, and the target has the Prone condition. Success: Half damage only. Failure or Success: The target's Speed is reduced to 0 until the end of its next turn.3) Raging Storm. A storm cloud fills a 60-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point the cataclysm can see within 150 feet. The cloud lasts for 1 minute or until the cataclysm uses Cataclysmic Event again. Creatures entirely in the cloud have the Blinded and Deafened conditions and can't cast spells with a Verbal component. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 23, each creature that enters the cloud for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there. Failure: 18 (4d8) Lightning damage plus 18 (4d8) Thunder damage. Success: Half damage.4) Swallowing Earth. Strength Saving Throw: DC 23, each creature in a 90-foot Cube originating from a point on the ground within 150 feet. Failure: 18 (4d8) Bludgeoning damage plus 18 (4d8) Acid damage, and the target has the Prone condition and is buried under rubble. A buried target has the Restrained condition, has Total Cover, and is suffocating. As an action, a buried creature or another creature within 5 feet of it can make a DC 18 Strength (Athletics) check. On a successful check, the creature is no longer buried. Success: Half damage only.
Legendary Actions:
Eruption. The cataclysm makes one Elemental Burst attack.

Rumbling Movement. The cataclysm moves up to its Speed, Fly Speed, or Swim Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks. Each creature within 5 feet of the cataclysm as it moves is targeted once by the following effect. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 23. Failure: The target has the Prone condition. Failure or Success: The cataclysm can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.
Description: Elemental cataclysms are massive and powerful elemental titans that are often found in the Elemental Chaos. They are known for their ability to control the weather, their powerful attacks, and their resistance to all types of damage. Elemental cataclysms can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Elemental Cultist
Actions:
Multiattack. The cultist makes three attacks, using Elemental Flail or Elemental Claw in any combination.

Elemental Flail. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 25 (6d6 + 4) damage of a type chosen by the cultist: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder.

Elemental Claw. Ranged Attack Roll: +7, range 120 ft. Hit: 22 (4d10) damage of a type chosen by the cultist: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, the cultist moves the target up to 10 feet straight toward or away from itself.

Spellcasting. The cultist casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15): At will: Elementalism, Mage Hand
Reactions: Elemental Absorption (1/Day). Trigger: The cultist takes Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage. Response: The cultist gives itself Resistance to that instance of damage and gains 10 Temporary Hit Points.
Description: Elemental cultists are fanatical followers of elemental forces who are often found in various environments. They are known for their ability to harness elemental magic, their powerful attacks, and their resistance to certain types of damage. Elemental cultists can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Elemental Spirit
Speed: 40 ft., Burrow 40 ft. (Earth only), Fly 40 ft. (hover; Air only), Swim 40 ft. (Water only)
Damage Resistances: lightning and thunder (Air only); piercing and slashing (Earth only); acid (Water only)
Languages: Primordial, understands the languages you know
CR: None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)
Traits: Amorphous Form (Air, Fire, and Water Only). The spirit can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without it counting as Difficult Terrain.
Actions:
Multiattack. The spirit makes a number of Slam attacks equal to half this spell's level (round down).

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d10 + 4 + the spell's level Bludgeoning (Earth only), Cold (Water only), Lightning (Air only), or Fire (Fire only) damage.
Description: Elemental spirits are powerful and versatile creatures that can be summoned by spellcasters using the Conjure Elemental spell. They are known for their ability to take on different forms and their resistance to certain types of damage. Elemental spirits can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Elephant
Actions:
Multiattack. The elephant makes two Gore attacks.

Gore. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 5 ft. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) Piercing damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature and the elephant moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target has the Prone condition.
Bonus Actions: Trample. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 16, one creature within 5 feet that has the Prone condition. Failure: 17 (2d10 + 6) Bludgeoning damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Elephants are massive and powerful beasts that are often found in grassland environments. They are known for their strong tusks, their ability to trample others, and their resistance to certain types of damage. Elephants can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Elk
Actions: Ram. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature and the elk moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 3 (1d6) Bludgeoning damage and has the Prone condition.
Description: Elks are large, graceful deer that inhabit forested and grassland areas. They are known for their speed and agility, as well as their ability to sense danger. Elks can be dangerous when cornered or when protecting their young.

Name: Empyrean
Traits:
Legendary Resistance (4/Day). If the empyrean fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Magic Resistance. The empyrean has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The empyrean makes two attacks, choosing Sacred Weapon or Divine Ray in any combination.

Sacred Weapon. Melee Attack Roll: +17, reach 10 ft. Hit: 31 (6d6 + 10) Force damage, and the target is Stunned until the start of the empyrean’s next turn. The target may choose to resist the Stunned condition; if it does, it instead takes 21 additional Force damage that ignores Resistance and Immunity.

Divine Ray. Ranged Attack Roll: +15, range 600 ft. Hit: 35 (6d8 + 8) Necrotic or Radiant damage (empyrean’s choice).

Spellcasting. The empyrean casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 23): At will: Calm Emotions, Greater Restoration, Pass without Trace, Water Breathing; 1/day each: Commune, Dispel Evil and Good, Plane Shift
Legendary Actions:
Bolster. The empyrean gains 10 Temporary Hit Points, and it and its allies within 30 feet gain Advantage on all D20 Tests until the end of its next turn. The empyrean cannot use this action again until the start of its next turn.

Shockwave of Glory. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 23, each creature in a 30-foot Emanation centered on the empyrean. Failure: 27 (6d8) Force damage and the creature is knocked Prone. Success: Half damage only. Failure or Success: The empyrean cannot use this action again until the start of its next turn.

Smite. The empyrean makes one Divine Ray attack.
Description: Empyreans are titans born of divine or infernal powers, colossal beings who embody pure celestial or abyssal energy. Their sacred weapons can stun even the mightiest foes, while their divine beams strike with devastating precision. They often act as emissaries or champions of their home planes, and their presence alters the course of battles and destinies.

Name: Empyrean Iota
Traits:
Incorporeal Movement. The empyrean can move through creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain. It takes 5 (1d10) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

Magic Resistance. The empyrean has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Otherworldly Strike. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. or range 30 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Necrotic or Radiant damage (empyrean’s choice).

Spellcasting. The empyrean casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Wisdom as its spellcasting ability:; 1/day each: Bless, Lesser Restoration (as an action)
Bonus Actions: Healing Word (1/Day). The empyrean casts Healing Word using the same spellcasting ability as its Spellcasting trait.
Description: Empyrean Iotas are minor fragments of the titanic essence, luminous spirits that drift between planes. Though they lack the strength of their greater counterparts, they are wise and perceptive, capable of healing allies and striking with otherworldly energy. They often serve as messengers or guides, bearing omens and warnings from higher powers.

Name: Erinjes
Traits:
Diabolical Restoration. If the Erinjes dies outside the Nine Hells, its form dissolves into acrid smoke, and it instantly reforms somewhere in the Nine Hells with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The Erinjes has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Magic Rope. The Erinjes carries an enchanted rope. While holding it, the Erinjes can use the Entangling Rope action. The rope has AC 20, 90 Hit Points, and Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage. It crumbles to dust if reduced to 0 Hit Points, if it remains more than 5 feet from the Erinjes for over an hour, or if the Erinjes dies. The Erinjes can restore the rope to full strength after finishing a Short or Long Rest.
Actions:
Multiattack. The Erinjes makes three Withering Sword attacks and may use Entangling Rope.

Withering Sword. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Slashing damage plus 11 (2d10) Necrotic damage.

Entangling Rope (Requires Magic Rope). Strength Saving Throw: DC 16, one creature the Erinjes can see within 120 feet. Failure: 14 (4d6) Force damage, and the target becomes Restrained until the rope is destroyed, the Erinjes releases it as a Bonus Action, or the Erinjes uses Entangling Rope again.
Reactions: Parry. Trigger: The Erinjes is hit by a melee attack while holding a weapon. Response: The Erinjes adds 4 to its AC against that attack, potentially causing it to miss.
Description: The Erinjes are female demon warriors from the Nine Hells, renowned for their deadly beauty and martial prowess. Armed with cursed weapons and an enchanted rope capable of immobilizing enemies, they serve as hunters, inquisitors, and special agents for the lords of the underworld. Their unyielding determination and resistance to damage make them formidable foes for any adventurer.

Name: Attercap
Traits:
Wall-Skitter. The attercap can climb difficult surfaces, including ceilings, without needing an ability check.

Websense. The attercap ignores movement restrictions caused by webs and instinctively perceives any creature touching the same web network.
Actions:
Multiattack. The attercap makes one Bite attack and one Claw attack.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage plus 2 (1d4) Poison damage. The target becomes Poisoned until the start of the attercap's next turn.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (2d4 + 2) Slashing damage.

Web Strand (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 12, one Large or smaller creature within 30 feet. Failure: The target becomes Restrained by sticky filament (AC 10; HP 5; Vulnerable to Fire; Immune to Bludgeoning, Poison, and Psychic damage).
Bonus Actions: Reel. The attercap pulls one creature within 30 feet that is Restrained by its Web Strand up to 25 feet straight toward itself.
Description: Attercaps are sinister and cunning monstrosities that are often found in forest environments. They are known for their ability to climb surfaces, their use of webs to trap prey, and their poisonous bite. Attercaps can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Ettin
Condition Immunities: blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, stunned, unconscious
Actions:
Multiattack. The ettin makes one Battleaxe attack and one Morningstar attack.

Battleaxe. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) Slashing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

Morningstar. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) Piercing damage, and the target has Disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.
Description: Ettins are large, powerful giants known for their strength and ferocity. They are often found in mountainous regions and are notorious for their aggressive nature and tendency to attack travelers.

Name: Faerie Dragon Adult
Languages: Draconic, Sylvan; telepathy 60 ft. (faerie dragons only)
Traits: Magic Resistance. The dragon has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d4 + 5) Piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) Psychic damage.

Euphoria Breath (Recharge 5-6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 13, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: The target has the Incapacitated condition and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically. While Incapacitated, the target uses all its movement on each of its turns to move in a random direction.

Spellcasting. The dragon casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13): At will: Dancing Lights, Mage Hand, Minor Illusion; 1/day each: Hallucinatory Terrain, Polymorph
Bonus Actions: Superior Invisibility. The dragon casts Greater Invisibility on itself, requiring no spell components and using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Faerie dragons are small, colorful dragons that are often found in forest environments. They are known for their playful and mischievous nature, as well as their ability to cast spells and their resistance to certain types of damage. Faerie dragons can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Faerie Dragon Youth
Languages: Draconic, Sylvan; telepathy 60 ft. (faerie dragons only)
Traits: Magic Resistance. The dragon has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Piercing damage plus 2 (1d4) Psychic damage.

Euphoria Breath (Recharge 5-6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: The target has the Incapacitated condition until the end of its next turn and uses all its movement on its turn to move in a random direction.

Spellcasting. The dragon casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12): At will: Dancing Lights, Mage Hand, Minor Illusion
Bonus Actions: Superior Invisibility. The dragon casts Greater Invisibility on itself, requiring no spell components and using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Faerie dragons are small, colorful dragons that are often found in forest environments. They are known for their playful and mischievous nature, as well as their ability to cast spells and their resistance to certain types of damage. Faerie dragons can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Fey Spirit
Languages: Sylvan, understands the languages you know
CR: None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)
Actions:
Multiattack. The spirit makes a number of Fey Blade attacks equal to half this spell's level (round down).

Fey Blade. Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 2d6 + 3 + the spell's level Force damage.
Bonus Actions:
Fey Step. The spirit magically teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. Then one of the following effects occurs, based on the spirit's chosen mood:

Fuming. The spirit has Advantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of this turn.

Mirthful. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC equals your spell save DC, one creature the spirit can see within 10 feet of itself. Failure: The target is Charmed by you and the spirit for 1 minute or until the target takes any damage.

Tricksy. The spirit fills a 10-foot Cube within 5 feet of it with magical Darkness, which lasts until the end of its next turn.
Description: Fey spirits are ethereal beings that can be summoned by spellcasters using the Conjure Fey spell. They are known for their ability to take on different moods and their resistance to being charmed. Fey spirits can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Fiend Cultist
Senses: Darkvision 90 ft. (unimpeded by magical Darkness), passive Perception 17
Actions:
Multiattack. The cultist makes three Pact Axe attacks.

Pact Axe. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (1d12 + 4) Slashing damage plus 13 (3d8) Fire damage.

Spellcasting. The cultist casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15, +7 to hit with spell attacks): At will: Scorching Ray (level 5 version), Thaumaturgy; 2/day: Fireball (level 6 version)
Reactions: Hellish Rebuke. The cultist casts Hellish Rebuke in response to that spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Fiend cultists are fanatical followers of fiendish entities, often found in dark and dangerous locations. They are known for their combat prowess, their use of fire-based magic, and their willingness to sacrifice themselves for their cause. Fiend cultists can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Fiendish Spirit
HP: 50 (Demon only) or 40 (Devil only) or 60 (Yugoloth only) + 15 for each spell level above 6
Speed: 40 ft., Climb 40 ft. (Demon only), Fly 60 ft. (Devil only)
CR: None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)
Traits:
Magic Resistance. The spirit has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Death Throes (Demon Only). When the spirit drops to 0 Hit Points or the spell ends, the spirit explodes. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC equals your spell save DC, each creature in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the spirit. Failure: 2d10 plus this spell's level Fire damage. Success: Half damage.

Devil's Sight (Devil Only). Magical Darkness doesn't impede the spirit's Darkvision.
Actions:
Multiattack. The spirit makes a number of attacks equal to half this spell's level (round down).

Bite (Demon Only). Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d12 + 3 + the spell's level Necrotic damage.

Claws (Yugoloth Only). Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d8 + 3 + the spell's level Slashing damage. Immediately after the attack hits or misses, the spirit can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

Fiery Strike (Devil Only). Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. or range 150 ft. Hit: 2d6 + 3 + the spell's level Fire damage.
Description: Fiendish spirits are malevolent entities that can be summoned by spellcasters using the Conjure Fiend spell. They are known for their resistance to certain types of damage, their ability to teleport, and their explosive death throes (in the case of demons). Fiendish spirits can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Fire Elemental
Condition Immunities: exhaustion, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, unconscious
Traits:
Fire Aura. At the end of each of the elemental's turns, each creature in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the elemental takes 5 (1d10) Fire damage. Creatures and flammable objects in the Emanation start burning.

Fire Form. The elemental can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so, and it can enter a creature's space and stop there. The first time it enters a creature's space on a turn, that creature takes 5 (1d10) Fire damage.

Illumination. The elemental sheds Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 30 feet.

Water Susceptibility. The elemental takes 3 (1d6) Cold damage for every 5 feet the elemental moves in water or for every gallon of water splashed on it.
Actions:
Multiattack. The elemental makes two Burn attacks.

Burn. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Fire damage. If the target is a creature or a flammable object, it starts burning.
Description: Fire elementals are powerful beings made of living flame. They are often found in fiery environments, such as the Elemental Plane of Fire, and are known for their ability to cause damage with their fiery attacks and their resistance to certain types of damage. Fire elementals can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Fire Giant
Actions:
Multiattack. The giant makes two attacks, using Flame Sword or Hammer Throw in any combination.

Flame Sword. Melee Attack Roll: +11, reach 10 ft. Hit: 21 (4d6 + 7) Slashing damage plus 10 (3d6) Fire damage.

Hammer Throw. Ranged Attack Roll: +11, range 60/240 ft. Hit: 23 (3d10 + 7) Bludgeoning damage plus 4 (1d8) Fire damage, and the target is pushed up to 15 feet straight away from the giant and has Disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.
Description: Fire giants are massive and powerful giants that are often found in volcanic or fiery environments. They are known for their strength, their ability to wield flaming weapons, and their resistance to fire damage. Fire giants can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Flameskull
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, poisoned, prone
Traits:
Illumination. The flameskull sheds Bright Light in a 15-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 15 feet.

Magic Resistance. The flameskull has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Undead Restoration. If the flameskull is destroyed, it regains all its Hit Points in 1 hour unless Holy Water is sprinkled on its remains or the Dispel Evil and Good spell is cast on them.
Actions:
Multiattack. The flameskull makes two Fire Ray attacks.

Fire Ray. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) Fire damage.

Spellcasting. The flameskull casts one of the following spells, requiring no Somatic or Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13): At will: Mage Hand; 2/day: Magic Missile (level 2 version); 1/day: Fireball
Description: Flameskulls are undead creatures that are often found in dark and dangerous locations. They are known for their ability to cast spells, their resistance to certain types of damage, and their ability to restore themselves if destroyed. Flameskulls can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Flaming Skeleton
Languages: Understands Common plus one other language but can't speak
Traits:
Death Burst. The skeleton explodes when it dies. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the skeleton. Failure: 14 (4d6) Fire damage. Success: Half damage.

Illumination. The skeleton sheds Bright Light in a 15-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 15 feet.
Actions:
Multiattack. The skeleton makes two attacks, using Flame Scepter or Hurl Flame in any combination.

Flame Scepter. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Bludgeoning damage plus 3 (1d6) Fire damage.

Hurl Flame. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 60 ft. Hit: 7 (1d10 + 2) Fire damage.
Description: Flaming skeletons are undead creatures that are often found in dark and dangerous locations. They are known for their ability to cause fire damage with their attacks, their resistance to certain types of damage, and their explosive death burst. Flaming skeletons can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Flesh Golem
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned
Languages: Understands Common plus one other language but can't speak
Traits:
Aversion to Fire. If the golem takes Fire damage, it has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the end of its next turn.

Berserk. Whenever the golem starts its turn Bloodied, roll 1d6. On a 6, the golem goes berserk. On each of its turns while berserk, the golem attacks the nearest creature it can see. If no creature is near enough to move to and attack, the golem attacks an object. Once the golem goes berserk, it remains so until it is destroyed or it is no longer Bloodied.

The golem's creator, if within 60 feet of the berserk golem, can try to calm it by taking an action to make a DC 15 Charisma (Persuasion) check; the golem must be able to hear its creator. If this check succeeds, the golem ceases being berserk until the start of its next turn, at which point it resumes rolling for the Berserk trait again if it is still Bloodied.

Immutable Form. The golem can't shape-shift.

Lightning Absorption. Whenever the golem is subjected to Lightning damage, it regains a number of Hit Points equal to the Lightning damage dealt.

Magic Resistance. The golem has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The golem makes two Slam attacks.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Bludgeoning damage plus 4 (1d8) Lightning damage.
Description: Flesh golems are constructs made from the bodies of dead creatures, often created by necromancers or other dark magic users. They are known for their strength, their resistance to certain types of damage, and their tendency to go berserk when they are Bloodied. Flesh golems can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Aetherfloat
Languages: Understands Undercommon but can't speak; telepathy 60 ft.
Traits:
Advanced Telepathy. The aetherfloat perceives the content of any telepathic communication within 60 feet of it.

Prone Deficiency. If the aetherfloat receives the Prone condition, roll a die. On an odd number, it has the Incapacitated condition. At the end of each of its turns, the aetherfloat makes a DC 10 Dexterity saving throw, ending the Incapacitated condition on a success.

Telepathic Shroud. The aetherfloat's thoughts can't be read by any means, and magic can't detect its location or observe it remotely.
Actions:
Tentacle. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 feet. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Acid damage.

Stench Spray (1/Day). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 10, one creature the aetherfloat can see within 15 feet. Failure: The target is coated in a foul-smelling liquid, exudes a stench for 1d4 hours, and has the Poisoned condition while the stench lasts. Other creatures have the Poisoned condition while in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the coated target. The target can remove the stench on itself if it bathes during a Short or Long Rest.
Description: Aetherfloats are gentle, drifting aberrations that glide through the deep caverns of the Underdark. They communicate solely through telepathy and instinctively shield their minds from intrusion. Though physically fragile, they possess remarkable psionic awareness and emit a defensive chemical spray when threatened. Scholars believe they absorb ambient thoughts and emotions, feeding on psychic resonance rather than flesh.

Name: Flying Snake
Traits: Flyby. The snake doesn't provoke an Opportunity Attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Piercing damage plus 5 (2d4) Poison damage.
Description: Flying snakes are small, venomous creatures that are often found in desert, forest, and grassland environments. They are known for their ability to fly, their venomous bite, and their resistance to certain types of damage. Flying snakes can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Deepcurse Giant
Actions:
Multiattack. The deepcurse giant makes two Stone Club attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Warping Hex if available.

Stone Club. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 15 ft. Hit: 24 (4d8 + 6) Bludgeoning damage.

Warping Hex (Recharge 4-6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 16, one creature the deepcurse giant can see within 120 feet. Failure: 21 (6d6) Psychic damage, and the target gains 1 Exhaustion level. Success: Half damage only.
Description: Deepcurse giants are massive, twisted beings warped by ancient subterranean magic. Their bodies bear grotesque mutations, and their gaze carries a psychic malediction capable of draining vitality and warping the mind. They roam the deepest caverns as tyrants and hunters, crushing intruders with brutal strength while cursing them with hexes that erode body and spirit alike.

Name: Frog
Traits:
Amphibious. The frog can breathe air and water.

Standing Leap. The frog's Long Jump is up to 10 feet and its High Jump is up to 5 feet with or without a running start.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Piercing damage.
Description: Frogs are small amphibious creatures that are often found in forest and swamp environments. They are known for their ability to breathe both air and water, their jumping abilities, and their resistance to certain types of damage. Frogs can pose a minor threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Frost Giant
Actions:
Multiattack. The giant makes two attacks, using Frost Axe or Great Bow in any combination.

Frost Axe. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 10 ft. Hit: 19 (2d12 + 6) Slashing damage plus 9 (2d8) Cold damage.

Great Bow. Ranged Attack Roll: +9, range 150/600 ft. Hit: 17 (2d10 + 6) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Cold damage, and the target's Speed decreases by 10 feet until the end of its next turn.
Bonus Actions: War Cry (Recharge 5-6). The giant or one creature of its choice that can see or hear it gains 16 (2d10 + 5) Temporary Hit Points and has Advantage on attack rolls until the start of the giant's next turn.
Description: Frost giants are massive and powerful giants that are often found in cold and icy environments. They are known for their strength, their ability to cause cold damage with their attacks, and their resistance to cold damage. Frost giants can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Stoneheart Sentinel
Speed: 15 ft. (30 ft. when rolling, 60 ft. rolling downhill)
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned
Senses: Darkvision 60 ft., Tremorsense 60 ft., passive Perception 11
Actions:
Avalanche Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature and the stoneheart sentinel moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 7 (2d6) Bludgeoning damage and has the Prone condition.

Animate Boulders (1/Day). The stoneheart sentinel magically animates one or two boulders it can see within 60 feet of itself. Each boulder uses the Stoneheart Sentinel stat block, except it has Intelligence and Charisma scores of 1 and lacks this action. The boulder takes its turn immediately after the sentinel on the same Initiative count, and it obeys the sentinel. A boulder remains animate for 1 minute or until it or the sentinel dies.
Description: Stoneheart sentinels are ancient elemental guardians formed from living rock. They slumber within mountainsides and cliff faces, awakening only when their territory is disturbed. Their bodies can roll with tremendous force, and their command over nearby stones allows them to animate boulders as temporary allies. Stoic and unyielding, they defend the natural order of the highlands with patient, implacable strength.

Actions:

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (2d4 + 2) Slashing damage.

Name: Gas Spore Fungus
Condition Immunities: blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, paralyzed, poisoned, prone
Traits: Death Burst. The gas spore bursts when it dies. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 10, each creature in a 20-foot Emanation originating from the gas spore. Failure: The target takes 10 (3d6) Poison damage and has the Poisoned condition for 1d12 hours. Unless the Poisoned condition is removed, the target dies at the end of that time and sprouts 2d4 Tiny Gas Spore Fungi (each with 1 Hit Point). After 2d6 days, they become Large and have 13 Hit Points.
Actions: Tendril. Melee Attack Roll: +0, reach 5 ft. Hit: 3 (1d6) Poison damage, and the target has the Poisoned condition until the end of its next turn.
Description: Gas spore fungi are large, floating plants that are often found in the Underdark. Name: Translucent Devourer
Condition Immunities: blinded, charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, prone
Traits:
Ooze Form. The devourer fills its entire space and is nearly invisible. Other creatures can enter that space, but doing so subjects them to the devourer's Engulf and imposes Disadvantage on the saving throw.

Creatures inside the devourer have Total Cover, and it can contain one Large creature or up to four Medium or Small creatures at a time.

As an action, a creature within 5 feet can pull a creature or object out of the devourer with a successful DC 12 Strength (Athletics) check, taking 10 (3d6) Acid damage in the process.

Near-Invisible. Even when motionless in plain sight, a creature must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check to notice the devourer unless it has seen it move or act.
Actions:
Pseudopod. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (3d6 + 2) Acid damage.

Engulf. The devourer moves up to its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks. It can move through the spaces of Large or smaller creatures if it has room inside itself to contain them (see Ooze Form). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature whose space the devourer enters for the first time during this move. Failure: 10 (3d6) Acid damage, and the target is engulfed. An engulfed target is suffocating, can't cast spells with a Verbal component, is Restrained, and takes 10 (3d6) Acid damage at the start of each of the devourer's turns. When the devourer moves, engulfed targets move with it.

An engulfed target can escape by taking an action to make a DC 12 Strength (Athletics) check. On a success, the target escapes to the nearest unoccupied space. Success on the initial save: Half damage, and the target moves to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the devourer. If no such space exists, the target fails instead.
Description: Translucent devourers are large, nearly invisible oozes that are often found in the Underdark. They are known for their ability to engulf and digest creatures, their resistance to certain types of damage, and their tendency to attack with their pseudopods. Translucent devourers can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Actions:
Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Piercing damage plus 9 (2d8) Necrotic damage.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Slashing damage. If the target is a non-Undead creature, it is subjected to the following effect. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 10. Failure: The target has the Paralyzed condition until the end of its next turn.

Actions:
Multiattack. The ghast makes two Horrific Necrosis attacks. It can replace one attack with a Claw attack.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) Slashing damage. If the target isn't an Undead, it has the Paralyzed condition until the end of its next turn.

Horrific Necrosis. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) Necrotic damage, and the target has the Frightened condition until the end of its next turn.

Spellcasting. The ghast casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability: At will: Speak with Dead, Thaumaturgy
Description: Ghast gravecallers are powerful undead creatures that are often found in dark and damp places. Name: Ghost
Damage Resistances: acid, bludgeoning, cold, fire, lightning, piercing, slashing, thunder
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained
Traits:
Ethereal Sight. The ghost can see 60 feet into the Ethereal Plane when it is on the Material Plane.

Incorporeal Movement. The ghost can move through other creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain. It takes 5 (1d10) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.
Actions:
Multiattack. The ghost makes two Withering Touch attacks.

Etherealness. The ghost casts the Etherealness spell, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability. The ghost is visible on the Material Plane while on the Border Ethereal and vice versa, but it can't affect or be affected by anything on the other plane.

Withering Touch. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 19 (3d10 + 3) Necrotic damage.

Horrific Visage. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 13, each creature in a 60-foot Cone that can see the ghost and isn't an Undead. Failure: 10 (2d6 + 3) Psychic damage, and the target has the Frightened condition until the start of the ghost's next turn. Success: The target is immune to this ghost's Horrific Visage for 24 hours.

Possession (Recharge 6). Charisma Saving Throw: DC 13, one Humanoid the ghost can see within 5 feet. Failure: The target is possessed by the ghost; the ghost disappears, and the target has the Incapacitated condition and loses control of its body. The ghost now controls the body, but the target retains awareness. The ghost can't be targeted by any attack, spell, or other effect, except ones that specifically target Undead. The ghost's game statistics are the same, except it uses the possessed target's Speed, as well as the target's Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution modifiers.

The possession lasts until the body drops to 0 Hit Points or the ghost leaves as a Bonus Action. When the possession ends, the ghost appears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the target, and the target is immune to this ghost's Possession for 24 hours. Success: The target is immune to this ghost's Possession for 24 hours.
Description: Ghosts are the spirits of the dead that are often found in haunted locations. They are known for their ability to become incorporeal, their resistance to certain types of damage, and their tendency to haunt and torment the living. Ghosts can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Standing Leap. The toad's Long Jump is up to 20 feet and its High Jump is up to 10 feet with or without a running start.
Actions:
Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage plus 5 (2d4) Poison damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 12).

Swallow. The toad swallows a Medium or smaller target it is grappling. While swallowed, the target isn't Grappled but has the Blinded and Restrained conditions, and it has Total Cover against attacks and other effects outside the toad. In addition, the target takes 10 (3d6) Acid damage at the end of each of the toad's turns. The toad can have only one target swallowed at a time, and it can't use Bite while it has a swallowed target. If the toad dies, a swallowed creature is no longer Restrained and can escape from the corpse using 5 feet of movement, exiting with the Prone condition.
Description: Giant toads are large and aggressive amphibians that are often found in coastal, forest, swamp, and Underdark environments. They are known for their ability to breathe both air and water, their powerful bites, and their tendency to attack with their bites and swallow their prey whole. Giant toads can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Giant Venomous Snake
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. Hit: 6 (1d4 + 4) Piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) Poison damage.
Environment: Coastal, Desert, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Swamp
Description: Giant venomous snakes are large and dangerous reptiles that are often found in a variety of environments. They are known for their ability to swim, their tendency to attack with their bites, and their ability to inject venom into their prey. Giant venomous snakes can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Giant Vulture
Traits: Pack Tactics. The vulture has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the vulture's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions: Gouge. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (2d6 + 2) Piercing damage, and the target has the Poisoned condition until the end of its next turn.
Description: Giant vultures are large and aggressive birds that are often found in desert, grassland, and hill environments. They are known for their scavenging habits, their tendency to attack with their beaks, and their ability to fly. Giant vultures can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Giant Wasp
Traits: Flyby. The wasp doesn't provoke an Opportunity Attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.
Actions: Sting. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage plus 5 (2d4) Poison damage.
Description: Giant wasps are large and aggressive insects that are often found in forest, grassland, and urban environments. They are known for their ability to fly, their tendency to attack with their stings, and their ability to inject venom into their prey. Giant wasps can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Giant Weasel
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Piercing damage.
Description: Giant weasels are large and agile mammals that are often found in forest, grassland, and hill environments. They are known for their ability to climb, their tendency to attack with their bites, and their ability to move quickly. Giant weasels can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Giant Wolf Spider
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 13
Traits: Spider Climb. The spider can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Piercing damage plus 5 (2d4) Poison damage.
Description: Giant wolf spiders are large and agile arachnids that are often found in coastal, desert, forest, grassland, and hill environments. They are known for their ability to climb, their tendency to attack with their bites, and their ability to spin webs. Giant wolf spiders can pose a significant threat to adventurers who encounter them, especially if they are found in groups or if they are defending their territory.

Name: Whispering Maw Horror
Traits:
Warped Ground. The terrain in a 10-foot Emanation around the horror twists and pulses, becoming Difficult Terrain.

Whispers of Madness. The horror constantly mutters and gurgles unless Incapacitated. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 10, any creature starting its turn within 20 feet of the horror. Failure: The creature rolls 1d8 to determine its behavior this turn:

1-4. The creature takes no actions.

5-6. The creature takes no action or Bonus Action and uses all its movement to move in a random direction.

7-8. The creature makes a melee attack against a random creature within reach or does nothing if no target is available.
Actions:
Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (2d6) Piercing damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, it is knocked Prone. If this attack reduces the target to 0 Hit Points, the horror devours the body, leaving behind only equipment.

Caustic Spittle (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 10, each creature in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within 30 feet. Failure: 7 (2d6) Radiant damage, and the creature is Blinded until the end of the horror’s next turn.
Description: Whispering Maw Horrors are misshapen aberrations that lurk in the depths of the Underdark. Their seething flesh warps the surrounding terrain, while their alien whispers can drive anyone who comes too close to madness. Though slow and shapeless in appearance, their jaws are deadly and capable of devouring their victims whole.

Name: Astrythar Starbinder
Actions:
Multiattack. The starbinder makes three Astral Slash attacks.

Astral Slash. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +10, reach 10 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Slashing damage plus 17 (5d6) Fire damage, and the target becomes Frightened until the start of the starbinder's next turn.

Stellar Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 18, each creature in a 90-foot Cone. Failure: 27 (6d8) Fire damage plus 27 (6d8) Force damage. Success: Half damage.

Spellcasting. The starbinder casts one of the following spells, requiring no components and using Intelligence (DC 18, +10 to hit): At will: Mage Hand (invisible); 2/day each: Nondetection (self only), Plane Shift, Tongues
Bonus Actions: Astral Step (3/Day). The starbinder casts Misty Step, requiring no components.
Description: Astrythar Starbinder is a powerful aberration that dwells in the Astral Plane, known for its mastery of astral magic and its ability to manipulate the fabric of reality.

Name: Astrythar Void Knight
Actions:
Multiattack. The void knight makes three Voidblade attacks. It may replace one attack with Spellcasting to cast Telekinesis if available.

Voidblade. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Slashing damage plus 14 (4d6) Psychic damage. Critical Hit: If the target is in an astral body, the void knight can sever its astral tether instead of dealing damage.

Spellcasting. The void knight casts one of the following spells, requiring no components and using Intelligence (DC 13): At will: Mage Hand (invisible); 2/day each: Nondetection (self only), Tongues; 1/day each: Plane Shift, Telekinesis
Bonus Actions: Astral Step (2/Day). The void knight casts Misty Step, requiring no components.
Description: Astrythar Void Knight is a formidable aberration that serves as a guardian of the Astral Plane, known for its martial prowess and its ability to manipulate the void.

Name: Astrythar Riftblade
Actions:
Multiattack. The riftblade makes two Astral Edge attacks.

Astral Edge. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (2d6 + 2) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Psychic damage.

Spellcasting. The riftblade casts one of the following spells, requiring no components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability: At will: Mage Hand (invisible); 2/day: Nondetection (self only)
Bonus Actions: Astral Step (2/Day). The riftblade casts Misty Step, requiring no components.
Description: Astrythar Riftblade is a skilled aberration that serves as a scout and enforcer for the Astral Plane, known for its agility and its ability to manipulate rifts in space.

Name: Astrythar Mind-Ascetic
Actions:
Multiattack. The mind-ascetic makes two Psionic Palm attacks.

Psionic Palm. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Bludgeoning damage plus 9 (2d8) Psychic damage.

Spellcasting. The mind-ascetic casts one of the following spells, requiring no components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability: At will: Mage Hand (invisible); 1/day: See Invisibility
Bonus Actions: Psionic Leap (2/Day). The mind-ascetic casts Jump, requiring no components.
Reactions: Psionic Guard (2/Day). The mind-ascetic casts Feather Fall or Shield in response to the spell's trigger, requiring no components.
Description: Astrythar Mind-Ascetic is a disciplined aberration that dwells in the chaotic realm of Limbo, known for its mastery of psionic powers and its ability to manipulate the minds of others.

Name: Astrythar Mind-Breaker
Actions:
Multiattack. The mind-breaker makes three Psychic Rend attacks.

Psychic Rend. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +8, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 26 (4d10 + 4) Psychic damage, and the target gains the mind-breaker's choice of (A) the Charmed condition until the start of the mind-breaker's next turn or (B) the Prone condition if it is Large or smaller.

Spellcasting. The mind-breaker casts one of the following spells, requiring no components and using Intelligence (spell save DC 16): At will: Mage Hand (invisible); 1/day each: Plane Shift, See Invisibility
Reactions: Psionic Guard. The mind-breaker casts Feather Fall or Shield in response to the spell's trigger, requiring no components.
Description: Astrythar Mind-Breaker is a powerful aberration that dwells in the chaotic realm of Limbo, known for its mastery of psychic powers and its ability to manipulate the minds of others.

Name: Astrythar Rift-Sage
Actions:
Multiattack. The rift-sage makes two Psionic Strike attacks.

Psionic Strike. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage plus 13 (3d8) Psychic damage.

Spellcasting. The rift-sage casts one of the following spells, requiring no components and using Wisdom (spell save DC 14): At will: Mage Hand (invisible); 1/day each: Phantasmal Killer (6th level), Plane Shift, See Invisibility
Bonus Actions: Psionic Leap (2/Day). The rift-sage casts Jump, requiring no components.
Reactions: Psionic Guard (2/Day). The rift-sage casts Feather Fall or Shield in response to the spell's trigger, requiring no components.
Description: Astrythar Rift-Sage is a knowledgeable aberration that serves as a scholar and advisor in the chaotic realm of Limbo, known for its mastery of psionic powers and its ability to manipulate rifts in space.

Name: Glaprezu
Traits:
Demonic Restoration. If the Glaprezu dies outside the Abyss, its form melts into foul ichor, and it instantly reforms somewhere in the Abyss with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The Glaprezu has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The Glaprezu makes two Pincer attacks and then uses Pummel or Spellcasting.

Pincer. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 10 ft. Hit: 16 (2d10 + 5) Slashing damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, it becomes Grappled (escape DC 15) by one of the Glaprezu’s pincers.

Pummel. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 17, one creature Grappled by the Glaprezu. Failure: 15 (3d6 + 5) Bludgeoning damage. Success: Half damage.

Spellcasting. The Glaprezu casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Intelligence as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 16): At will: Darkness, Detect Magic, Dispel Magic; 1/day each: Confusion, Fly, Power Word Stun
Description: Glaprezu are imposing demons that combine brute strength with manipulative cunning. They serve as commanders and strategists in the Abyss, using both their physical power and magic to dominate their enemies. Their enormous claws can crush their victims, while their spells confuse and destabilize opponents, making them formidable foes in any battle.

Name: Gladiator
Actions:
Multiattack. The gladiator makes three Spear attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Shield Bash.

Spear. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Piercing damage.

Shield Bash. Strength Saving Throw: DC 15, one creature within 5 feet that the gladiator can see. Failure: 9 (2d4 + 4) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.
Reactions: Parry. Trigger: The gladiator is hit by a melee attack roll while holding a weapon. Response: The gladiator adds 3 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.
Description: Gladiators are skilled combatants who have honed their abilities in arenas and battlefields. They are known for their prowess with weapons, their ability to entertain crowds, and their willingness to fight for glory and survival.

Name: Gnoll Demoniac
Actions:
Multiattack. The gnoll makes two Abyssal Strike attacks.

Abyssal Strike. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. Hit: 20 (5d6 + 3) Poison damage.

Hunger of Yenoghu (Recharge 5-6).
