The gnoll conjures a 30-foot Cube of magical Darkness originating from a point it can see within 60 feet, which lasts for 1 minute or until the gnoll's Concentration ends on it. This area is Difficult Terrain. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 14, any creature that starts its turn in this area or enters it for the first time on a turn. Failure: 28 (8d6) Necrotic damage, and the gnoll or a creature of its choice it can see gains 10 Temporary Hit Points. Success: Half damage only.
Bonus Actions: Rampage (2/Day). Immediately after dealing damage to a creature that is already Bloodied, the gnoll moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Abyssal Strike attack.
Description: Gnoll Demoniacs are fierce fiends that serve as elite warriors in the Abyss, known for their brutal combat skills and their ability to command other gnolls.

Name: Gnoll Fang of Yenoghu
Actions:
Multiattack. The gnoll makes one Bite attack and two Bone Flail attacks.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Poison damage, and the target becomes Poisoned until the start of the gnoll’s next turn.

Bone Flail. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 10 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Piercing damage.
Bonus Actions: Rampage (2/Day). Immediately after damaging a creature that is already Bloodied, the gnoll moves up to half its Speed and makes one Bite attack.
Description: The Fang of Yenoghu are gnolls corrupted by the direct influence of the Demon Lord, transformed into ferocious and relentless predators. Their venomous fangs and crude yet deadly weapons make them brutal fighters, while their bloodthirsty fury drives them to pursue and finish off wounded enemies. Wherever they go, they spread chaos and destruction in the name of their dark master.

Name: Gnoll Pack Lord
Actions:
Multiattack. The gnoll makes two attacks, using Bone Whip or Bone Javelin in any combination, and it uses Incite Rampage if available.

Bone Whip. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 10 ft. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) Slashing damage.

Bone Javelin. Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 30/120 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Piercing damage.

Incite Rampage (Recharge 5-6). The gnoll targets another creature it can see within 60 feet of itself that has the Rampage Bonus Action. The target can take a Reaction to make one melee attack.
Bonus Actions: Rampage (2/Day). Immediately after dealing damage to a creature that is already Bloodied, the gnoll moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Bone Whip attack.
Description: Gnoll Pack Lords are cunning fiends that serve as leaders of gnoll packs, known for their strategic minds and their ability to rally their followers into a frenzy of violence.

Name: Gnoll Warrior
Actions:
Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage.

Bone Bow. Ranged Attack Roll: +3, range 150/600 ft. Hit: 6 (1d10 + 1) Piercing damage.
Bonus Actions: Rampage (1/Day). Immediately after dealing damage to a creature that is already Bloodied, the gnoll moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Rend attack.
Description: Gnoll Warriors are savage fiends that serve as the backbone of gnoll packs, known for their ferocity in combat and their willingness to attack in overwhelming numbers.

Name: Goat
Actions: Ram. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Bludgeoning damage, or 2 (1d4) Bludgeoning damage if the goat moved 20+ feet straight toward the target immediately before the hit.
Description: Goats are hardy animals that can be found in a variety of environments, known for their sure-footedness and their ability to climb steep terrain.

Name: Goblin Boss
Actions:
Multiattack. The goblin makes two attacks, using Scimitar or Shortbow in any combination.

Scimitar. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Slashing damage, plus 2 (1d4) Slashing damage if the attack roll had Advantage.

Shortbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 80/320 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage, plus 2 (1d4) Piercing damage if the attack roll had Advantage.
Bonus Actions: Nimble Escape. The goblin takes the Disengage or Hide action.
Reactions: Redirect Attack. Trigger: A creature the goblin can see makes an attack roll against it. Response: The goblin chooses a Small or Medium ally within 5 feet of itself. The goblin and that ally swap places, and the ally becomes the target of the attack instead.
Environment: Forest, Grassland, Hill, Planar (Acheron), Planar (Feywild), Underdark
Description: Goblin Bosses are cunning and ruthless leaders of goblin tribes, known for their tactical acumen and their ability to inspire loyalty in their followers.

Name: Goblin Hexer
Actions:
Multiattack. The goblin makes two Hex Stick attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

Hex Stick. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) Psychic damage.

Spellcasting. The goblin casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13): At will: Minor Illusion; 1/day each: Blindness/Deafness, Faerie Fire, Grease
Reactions: Jinx. Trigger: A creature the goblin can see hits it with an attack roll. Response—Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 13, the triggering creature. Failure: The attack misses instead.
Environment: Forest, Grassland, Hill, Planar (Acheron), Planar (Feywild), Underdark
Description: Goblin Hexers are cunning and dangerous spellcasters that serve as the mystical leaders of goblin tribes, known for their ability to weave powerful illusions and their mastery of arcane arts.

Name: Goblin Minion
Actions: Dagger. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Piercing damage.
Bonus Actions: Nimble Escape. The goblin takes the Disengage or Hide action.
Environment: Forest, Grassland, Hill, Planar (Acheron), Planar (Feywild), Underdark
Description: Goblin Minions are the lowest-ranking members of goblin tribes, known for their cowardice and their tendency to flee when faced with danger.

Name: Goblin Warrior
Actions:
Scimitar. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Slashing damage, plus 2 (1d4) Slashing damage if the attack roll had Advantage.

Shortbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 80/320 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage, plus 2 (1d4) Piercing damage if the attack roll had Advantage.
Bonus Actions: Nimble Escape. The goblin takes the Disengage or Hide action.
Environment: Forest, Grassland, Hill, Planar (Acheron), Planar (Feywild), Underdark
Description: Goblin Warriors are the standard foot soldiers of goblin tribes, known for their agility and their ability to strike quickly before retreating into the shadows.

Name: Gold Dragon Wyrmling
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 14
Traits: Amphibious. The dragon can breathe air and water.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) Slashing damage.

Fire Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 13, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: 22 (4d10) Fire damage. Success: Half damage.

Weakening Breath. Strength Saving Throw: DC 13, each creature that isn't currently affected by this breath in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: The target has Disadvantage on Strength-based D20 Tests and subtracts 2 (1d4) from its damage rolls. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.
Description: Gold Dragon Wyrmlings are the youngest and smallest members of the gold dragon family, known for their playful nature and their innate magical abilities.

Name: Gorgon
Actions:
Gore. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 5 ft. Hit: 18 (2d12 + 5) Piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature and the gorgon moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target has the Prone condition.

Petrifying Breath (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 15, each creature in a 30-foot Cone. First Failure: The target has the Restrained condition and repeats the save at the end of its next turn if it is still Restrained, ending the effect on itself on a success. Second Failure: The target has the Petrified condition instead of the Restrained condition.
Bonus Actions: Trample. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 16, one creature within 5 feet that has the Prone condition. Failure: 16 (2d10 + 5) Bludgeoning damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Gorgons are fearsome metallic beasts that roam the wilderness, known for their powerful goring attacks and their ability to petrify their victims with a breath weapon.

Name: Goristo
Traits:
Demonic Restoration. If the goristo dies outside the Abyss, its form collapses into foul ichor, and it instantly reforms somewhere in the Abyss with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The goristo has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Siege Monster. The goristo deals double damage to objects and structures.
Actions:
Multiattack. The goristo makes one Brutal Gore attack and two Slam attacks.

Brutal Gore. Melee Attack Roll: +13, reach 10 ft. Hit: 40 (6d10 + 7) Piercing damage. If the target is Huge or smaller, it is hurled up to 20 feet directly away from the goristo and knocked Prone.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +13, reach 10 ft. Hit: 29 (4d10 + 7) Bludgeoning damage.
Bonus Actions: Charge. The goristo moves up to half its Speed in a straight line toward a creature it can see.
Description: Goristos are colossal, brutal demons used as living battering rams in the wars of the Abyss. Their devastating strength and ability to plow through any obstacle make them perfect for breaking through defensive lines and demolishing fortifications. Although they lack great cunning, they make up for it with sheer ferocity and superhuman endurance.

Name: Graveyard Revenant
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, stunned, unconscious
Traits: Undead Restoration. If the revenant dies, it revives 24 hours later unless Dispel Evil and Good is cast on its remains. If it revives, it animates another group of corpses elsewhere on the same plane of existence; it now looks different but uses the same stat block and returns with all its Hit Points.
Actions:
Multiattack. The revenant makes two Suffocate attacks.

Suffocate. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 10 ft. Hit: 10 (1d10 + 5) Bludgeoning damage plus 10 (3d6) Necrotic damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 15). Until the grapple ends, the target is suffocating. The revenant can have up to two targets Grappled in this way at a time.

Haunting Glare (Recharge 5-6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 15, each creature in a 30-foot Emanation originating from the revenant. Failure: The target has the Paralyzed condition and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.
Description: Graveyard Revenants are the animated remains of the dead, risen to serve as guardians of cemeteries and battlefields.

Name: Gray Ooze
Condition Immunities: blinded, charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, prone, restrained
Traits:
Amorphous. The ooze can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.

Corrosive Form. Nonmagical ammunition is destroyed immediately after hitting the ooze and dealing any damage. Any nonmagical weapon takes a cumulative -1 penalty to attack rolls immediately after dealing damage to the ooze and coming into contact with it. The weapon is destroyed if the penalty reaches -5. The penalty can be removed by casting the Mending spell on the weapon.

The ooze can eat through 2-inch-thick, nonmagical metal or wood in 1 round.
Actions: Pseudopod. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d8 + 1) Acid damage. Nonmagical armor worn by the target takes a -1 penalty to the AC it offers. The armor is destroyed if the penalty reduces its AC to 10. The penalty can be removed by casting the Mending spell on the armor.
Description: Gray Oozes are amorphous creatures that dwell in dark and damp environments, known for their corrosive touch and their ability to dissolve organic and inorganic matter alike.

Name: Ashen Mutant
Traits:
Magic Resistance. The ashen mutant has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Regeneration. The ashen mutant regains 10 Hit Points at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 Hit Point.

Innate Spellcasting. The mutant's innate spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 14). It can innately cast the following spells, requiring no components: At will: Detect Magic, Mage Hand; 2/day each: Fear, Fireball, Fly; 1/day: Plane Shift (self only)
Actions:
Multiattack. The ashen mutant makes three Claw attacks.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 10 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Necrotic damage.
Description: Ashen Mutants are aberrant creatures that have been twisted by exposure to the chaotic energies of Limbo, known for their regenerative abilities and their innate spellcasting powers.

Name: Green Dragon Wyrmling
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 14
Traits: Amphibious. The dragon can breathe air and water.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d10 + 2) Slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) Poison damage.

Poison Breath (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 11, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: 21 (6d6) Poison damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Green Dragon Wyrmlings are the youngest and smallest members of the green dragon family, known for their cunning and their ability to blend into their forest surroundings.

Name: Green Hag
Traits:
Amphibious. The hag can breathe air and water.

Coven Magic. While within 30 feet of at least two hag allies, the hag can cast one of the following spells, requiring no Material components, using the spell's normal casting time, and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 11): Augury, Find Familiar, Identify, Locate Object, Scrying, or Unseen Servant. The hag must finish a Long Rest before using this trait to cast that spell again.

Mimicry. The hag can mimic animal sounds and humanoid voices. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations only with a successful DC 14 Wisdom (Insight) check.
Actions:
Multiattack. The hag makes two Claw attacks.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) Slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) Poison damage.

Spellcasting. The hag casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12, +4 to hit with spell attacks): At will: Dancing Lights, Disguise Self (24-hour duration), Invisibility (self only, and the hag leaves no tracks while Invisible), Minor Illusion, Ray of Sickness (level 3 version)
Description: Green Hags are malevolent fey creatures that dwell in swamps and forests, known for their cunning and their ability to manipulate others through magic and deception.

Name: Emerald Mutant
Traits:
Magic Resistance. The emerald mutant has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Regeneration. The emerald mutant regains 10 Hit Points at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 Hit Point.

Innate Spellcasting. The mutant's innate spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 13). It can innately cast the following spells, requiring no components: At will: Detect Magic, Mage Hand; 2/day each: Fear, Fireball; 1/day: Plane Shift (self only)
Actions:
Multiattack. The emerald mutant makes two Claw attacks.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Poison damage.
Description: Emerald Mutants are aberrant creatures that have been twisted by exposure to the chaotic energies of Limbo, known for their regenerative abilities and their innate spellcasting powers.

Name: Neurovile Stalker
Traits: Neural Snare. The stalker doesn't spend extra movement to drag or carry a creature it has grappled.
Actions:
Multiattack. The stalker makes one Cerebral Beak attack and one Paralytic Tendrils attack.

Cerebral Beak. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d8 + 2) Piercing damage.

Paralytic Tendrils. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 10 ft. Hit: 7 (1d10 + 2) Piercing damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, it becomes Grappled (escape DC 12) by two of the stalker's tendrils. The target is also subjected to the following effect. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 11. Failure: The target is Poisoned and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically. While Poisoned, the target is also Paralyzed.
Description: Neurovile Stalkers are aberrant creatures that dwell in the Underdark, known for their ability to paralyze their victims with their tendrils and their insatiable hunger for brains.

Name: Ravener
Actions:
Multiattack. The ravener makes one Maw attack and one Tendril Lash attack.

Maw. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (2d6 + 2) Piercing damage.

Tendril Lash. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d10 + 2) Slashing damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, it becomes Grappled (escape DC 12) by all four tendrils.
Description: Raveners are aberrant creatures that dwell in forests and the Underdark, known for their ravenous hunger and their ability to grapple their victims with their tendrils.

Name: Ravener Prime
Actions:
Multiattack. The ravener prime makes one Maw attack, one Slam attack, and one Tendril Rend attack.

Maw. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 22 (4d8 + 4) Piercing damage.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is Large or smaller, it is knocked Prone.

Tendril Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) Slashing damage. If the target is Large or smaller, it becomes Grappled (escape DC 14) by all four tendrils.
Description: Ravener Primes are the largest and most powerful members of the ravener family, known for their devastating attacks and their ability to grapple even larger prey with their tendrils.

Name: Griffon
Actions:
Multiattack. The griffon makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) Piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 14) from both of the griffon's front claws.
Description: Griffons are majestic and powerful creatures that have the body of a lion and the head and wings of an eagle, known for their fierce loyalty and their ability to fly at great speeds.

Name: Gloommaw Stalker
Actions: Bone Cudgel. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Bludgeoning damage plus 2 (1d4) Psychic damage.
Description: Gloommaw stalkers are blind subterranean predators that navigate the Underdark through vibration and scent. Their crude bone weapons are infused with lingering psychic residue from the creatures they’ve slain, allowing them to deliver disorienting mental shocks. They hunt in small packs, ambushing intruders with uncanny precision despite their lack of sight.

Name: Guard
Actions: Spear. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 4 (1d6 + 1) Piercing damage.
Description: Guards are the common soldiers and protectors of cities, castles, and other important locations, known for their discipline and their willingness to defend their charges at all costs.

Name: Guard Captain
Actions:
Multiattack. The guard makes two attacks, using Javelin or Longsword in any combination.

Javelin. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft. Hit: 14 (3d6 + 4) Piercing damage.

Longsword. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) Slashing damage.
Description: Guard Captains are the leaders of guards, responsible for training and commanding their troops, known for their combat prowess and their ability to inspire those under their command.

Name: Guardian Naga
Traits: Celestial Restoration. If the naga dies, it returns to life in 1d6 days and regains all its Hit Points unless Dispel Evil and Good is cast on its remains.
Actions:
Multiattack. The naga makes two Bite attacks. It can replace any attack with a use of Poisonous Spittle.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 10 ft. Hit: 17 (2d12 + 4) Piercing damage plus 22 (4d10) Poison damage.

Poisonous Spittle. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 16, one creature the naga can see within 60 feet. Failure: 31 (7d8) Poison damage, and the target has the Blinded condition until the start of the naga's next turn. Success: Half damage only.

Spellcasting. The naga casts one of the following spells, requiring no Somatic or Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 16): At will: Thaumaturgy; 1/day each: Clairvoyance, Cure Wounds (level 6 version), Flame Strike (level 6 version), Geas, True Seeing
Description: Guardian Nagas are celestial beings that serve as protectors of sacred places and powerful artifacts, known for their wisdom and their deadly venom.

Name: Gultias Blight
Traits: Blight Seeds. When the blight finishes a Long Rest, it releases 1d6 seeds into empty spaces on the ground within 30 feet. After 24 hours, each seed grows into a creature under the blight’s command. Roll 1d8 for each seed: 1-4 = Twig Blight, 5-6 = Needle Blight, 7-8 = Vine Blight.
Actions:
Multiattack. The blight makes two attacks, choosing Slam or Thorn Volley in any combination. It also uses Life-Draining Root.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +12, reach 10 ft. Hit: 25 (4d8 + 7) Bludgeoning damage.

Thorn Volley. Ranged Attack Roll: +12, range 60/180 ft. Hit: 20 (3d8 + 7) Piercing damage.

Life-Draining Root. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 20, one Huge or smaller creature the blight can see within 30 feet. Failure: 14 (2d6 + 7) Necrotic damage, and the target becomes Grappled (escape DC 17) by one of six roots. While Grappled, the target is Restrained and takes 14 (4d6) Necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns. The target’s Hit Point maximum is reduced by the Necrotic damage taken, and the blight regains that many Hit Points.
Description: IThe Gultias Blight is a massive plant creature corrupted by dark energy, born of unnatural rituals and steeped in ancient evil. Its presence spawns new tree-like creatures to serve it, while its roots drain the very life from its victims. This plant colossus poses a devastating threat to any forest and to anyone who dares to approach its domain.

Name: Half-Dragon
Damage Resistances: Damage type chosen for the Draconic Origin trait below
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 15
Traits: Draconic Origin. The half-dragon is related to a type of dragon associated with one of the following damage types (DM's choice): Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. This choice affects other aspects of the stat block.
Actions:
Multiattack. The half-dragon makes two Claw attacks.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 6 (1d4 + 4) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) damage of the type chosen for the Draconic Origin trait.

Dragon's Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 14, each creature in a 30-foot Cone. Failure: 28 (8d6) damage of the type chosen for the Draconic Origin trait. Success: Half damage.
Bonus Actions: Leap. The half-dragon jumps up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.
Description: Half-Dragons are the offspring of dragons and humanoids, known for their draconic features and their ability to breathe elemental energy based on their draconic heritage.

Name: Harpy
Actions:
Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) Slashing damage.

Luring Song. The harpy sings a magical melody, which lasts until the harpy's Concentration ends on it. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 11, each Humanoid and Giant in a 300-foot Emanation originating from the harpy when the song starts. Failure: The target has the Charmed condition until the song ends and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns. While Charmed, the target has the Incapacitated condition and ignores the Luring Song of other harpies. If the target is more than 5 feet from the harpy, the target moves on its turn toward the harpy by the most direct route, trying to get within 5 feet of the harpy. It doesn't avoid Opportunity Attacks; however, before moving into damaging terrain (such as lava or a pit) and whenever it takes damage from a source other than the harpy, the target repeats the save. Success: The target is immune to this harpy's Luring Song for 24 hours.
Description: Harpies are winged humanoid creatures that dwell in coastal areas, forests, and mountains, known for their enchanting songs and their predatory nature.

Name: Haunting Revenant
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, unconscious
Traits:
Haunted Zone. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 17, any creature that casts a spell while inside the revenant's space. Failure: The spell fails and is wasted.

Undead Restoration. If the revenant dies, it revives 24 hours later unless Dispel Evil and Good is cast on its remains. If it revives, it animates another Gargantuan object or structure elsewhere on the same plane of existence; it now looks different but uses the same stat block and returns with all its Hit Points.
Actions:
Multiattack. The revenant makes two Object Slam attacks and uses Invitation.

Object Slam. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +9 (with Advantage if the target is inside the revenant's space), reach 10 ft. or range 30/90 ft. Hit: 27 (5d8 + 5) Bludgeoning damage.

Invitation. Charisma Saving Throw: DC 17, each creature in a 60-foot Cone. Failure: The target is teleported inside the revenant's space and swallowed. A swallowed creature has Total Cover against attacks and other effects outside the revenant.

While the revenant has Hit Points, a swallowed creature can leave the revenant's space only by using magic that enables planar travel, such as the Plane Shift spell.
Description: Haunting Revenants are powerful undead creatures that are bound to a specific location or object, known for their ability to animate objects and their haunting presence that can disrupt magic.

Name: Hawk
Actions: Talons. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Slashing damage.
Environment: Arctic, Coastal, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Mountain
Description: Hawks are small and agile birds of prey that are found in a variety of environments, known for their keen eyesight and their ability to fly at high speeds.

Name: Hell Hound
Traits: Pack Tactics. The hound has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the hound's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions:
Multiattack. The hound makes two Bite attacks.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) Fire damage.

Fire Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: 17 (5d6) Fire damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Hell Hounds are fierce canine creatures from the infernal planes, known for their loyalty to their masters and their devastating fire breath.

Name: Helmed Horror
Condition Immunities: blinded, charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, stunned
Languages: Understands Common plus one other language but can't speak
Traits:
Magic Resistance. The helmed horror has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Spell Immunity. The helmed horror is immune to three spells chosen by its creator. Typical choices include Heat Metal, Lightning Bolt, and Magic Missile.
Actions:
Multiattack. The helmed horror makes two Arcane Sword attacks.

Arcane Sword. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) Slashing damage plus 5 (1d10) Force damage.
Description: Helmed Horrors are magical constructs that are often used as guardians or enforcers, known for their resilience and their ability to resist magic.

Name: Hezru
Traits:
Demonic Restoration. If the Hezru dies outside the Abyss, its form liquefies into foul ichor, and it immediately reforms somewhere in the Abyss with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The Hezru has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Stench. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 16, any creature that starts its turn within a 10-foot Emanation around the Hezru. Failure: The creature becomes Poisoned until the start of its next turn.
Actions:
Multiattack. The Hezru makes three Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d4 + 4) Slashing damage plus 9 (2d8) Poison damage.
Bonus Actions: Leap. The Hezru jumps up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.
Description: Hezru are massive, brutal demons, often used as bodyguards or shock troops in the wars of the Abyss. Their leathery skin and supernatural resilience make them difficult to take down, while their toxic stench weakens anyone who dares to approach them. Despite their limited intelligence, they more than make up for it with sheer ferocity and terrifying tenacity.

Name: Hill Giant
Actions:
Multiattack. The giant makes two attacks, using Tree Club or Trash Lob in any combination.

Tree Club. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 10 ft. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

Trash Lob. Ranged Attack Roll: +8, range 60/240 ft. Hit: 16 (2d10 + 5) Bludgeoning damage, and the target has the Poisoned condition until the end of its next turn.
Description: Hill Giants are large and brutish humanoids that dwell in hilly regions, known for their strength and their tendency to raid nearby settlements for food and resources.

Name: Hippogriff
Traits: Flyby. The hippogriff doesn't provoke an Opportunity Attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.
Actions:
Multiattack. The hippogriff makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Slashing damage.
Description: Hippogriffs are majestic and powerful creatures that have the body of a horse and the wings and head of an eagle, known for their loyalty to those they bond with and their ability to fly at high speeds.

Name: Hippopotamus
Traits: Hold Breath. The hippopotamus can hold its breath for 10 minutes.
Actions:
Multiattack. The hippopotamus makes two Bite attacks.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 16 (2d10 + 5) Piercing damage.
Description: Hippopotamuses are large and powerful aquatic mammals that are found in rivers and lakes, known for their aggressive behavior and their ability to hold their breath for long periods of time.

Name: Hobgoblin Captain
Traits: Aura of Authority. While in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the hobgoblin, the hobgoblin and its allies have Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws, provided the hobgoblin doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions:
Multiattack. The hobgoblin makes two attacks, using Greatsword or Longbow in any combination.

Greatsword. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (2d6 + 2) Slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) Poison damage.

Longbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 150/600 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Piercing damage plus 5 (2d4) Poison damage.
Environment: Desert, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Mountain, Planar (Acheron), Underdark
Description: Hobgoblin Captains are experienced and disciplined leaders among hobgoblins, known for their tactical acumen and their ability to inspire their allies in battle.

Name: Hobgoblin Warlord
Traits: Aura of Authority. While in a 30-foot Emanation originating from the hobgoblin, the hobgoblin and its allies have Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws, provided the hobgoblin doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions:
Multiattack. The hobgoblin makes three attacks, using Javelin or Longsword in any combination.

Javelin. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Piercing damage, and the target's Speed decreases by 10 feet until the start of the hobgoblin's next turn.

Longsword. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) Slashing damage.
Reactions: Parry. Trigger: The hobgoblin is hit by a melee attack roll while holding a weapon. Response: The hobgoblin adds 3 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.
Environment: Desert, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Mountain, Planar (Acheron), Underdark
Description: Hobgoblin Warlords are powerful and charismatic leaders among hobgoblins, known for their strategic brilliance and their ability to command large groups of followers in battle.

Name: Hobgoblin Warrior
Traits: Pack Tactics. The hobgoblin has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the hobgoblin's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions:
Longsword. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (2d10 + 1) Slashing damage.

Longbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +3, range 150/600 ft. Hit: 5 (1d8 + 1) Piercing damage plus 7 (3d4) Poison damage.
Environment: Desert, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Mountain, Planar (Acheron), Underdark
Description: Hobgoblin Warriors are the common soldiers among hobgoblins, known for their discipline and their ability to work together in combat.

Name: Homunculus
Languages: Understands Common plus one other language but can't speak
Traits: Telepathic Bond. While the homunculus is on the same plane of existence as its master, the two of them can communicate telepathically with each other.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Piercing damage, and the target is subjected to the following effect. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12. Failure: The target has the Poisoned condition until the end of the homunculus's next turn. Failure by 5 or More: The target has the Poisoned condition for 1 minute. While Poisoned, the target has the Unconscious condition, which ends early if the target takes any damage.
Description: Homunculi are small magical constructs that are created by spellcasters, known for their loyalty to their masters and their ability to communicate telepathically.

Name: Hook Horror
Senses: Blindsight 60 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 15
Traits:
Echo-Shell. Le placche chitinoidi del hook horror vibrano a bassa frequenza, permettendogli di percepire l’ambiente circostante tramite risonanze echi.

Cliff Predator. Le sue membra ricurve sono perfette per arrampicarsi su pareti rocciose, dandogli una Velocità di Scalata pari alla sua Velocità di movimento.
Actions:
Multiattack. L’hook horror effettua due attacchi con i suoi Hook.

Hook. Tiro per Colpire in mischia: +6, portata 10 ft. Colpito: 11 (2d6 + 4) danni Perforanti. Se il bersaglio è Large o più piccolo, l’hook horror lo trascina di 5 piedi verso di sé o lontano da sé.
Description: Hookhorrors are monstrous and unsettling creatures that inhabit the depths of the Underdark. Their hooked arms and armored bodies make them formidable predators, while their ability to navigate by echolocation allows them to move nimbly through dark, winding caverns. They are often encountered in groups and fiercely defend their territory.

Name: Cornugot
Senses: Darkvision 150 ft. (unimpeded by magical Darkness), passive Perception 13
Traits:
Diabolical Restoration. If the devil dies outside the Nine Hells, its form bursts into acrid smoke, and it immediately reforms somewhere in the Nine Hells with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The devil has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The devil makes three attacks, choosing Searing Fork or Hurl Flame in any combination. It may replace one attack with Infernal Tail.

Searing Fork. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 10 ft. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) Piercing damage plus 9 (2d8) Fire damage.

Hurl Flame. Ranged Attack Roll: +8, range 150 ft. Hit: 26 (5d8 + 4) Fire damage. Flammable unattended objects ignite.

Infernal Tail. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 17, one creature the devil can see within 10 feet. Failure: 10 (1d8 + 6) Necrotic damage, and the target gains an infernal wound if it doesn’t already have one. While wounded, the creature loses 10 (3d6) Hit Points at the start of each of its turns. The wound closes after 1 minute, after receiving magical healing, or after a creature within 5 feet succeeds on a DC 17 Wisdom (Medicine) check to stanch it.
Description: Cornugots are warrior demons feared for their ferocity and military discipline. Armed with glowing pitchforks and mighty wings, they patrol the hellish skies as enforcers and guardians. Their hellish wounds continue to ooze malevolent magic, making them ruthless foes who are difficult to face in direct combat.

Name: Hunter Shark
Traits: Water Breathing. The shark can breathe only underwater.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +6 (with Advantage if the target doesn't have all its Hit Points), reach 5 ft. Hit: 14 (3d6 + 4) Piercing damage.
Description: Hunter Sharks are aggressive and territorial predators that dwell in coastal waters, known for their powerful jaws and their ability to sense prey through vibrations in the water.

Name: Hydra
Condition Immunities: blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, stunned, unconscious
Traits:
Hold Breath. The hydra can hold its breath for up to 1 hour.

Multiple Heads. The hydra begins with five heads. If it takes 25 or more damage during a single turn, one of its heads is destroyed. The hydra dies if all heads are destroyed. At the end of each of its turns, if it has at least one remaining head, it regrows two heads for each one lost since its previous turn unless it has taken Fire damage since then. Whenever it regrows heads, it regains 20 Hit Points.

Reactive Heads. The hydra gains one additional Reaction for each head it has beyond the first. These extra Reactions can only be used to make Opportunity Attacks.
Actions:
Multiattack. The hydra makes a Bite attack for each head it currently has.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 10 ft. Hit: 10 (1d10 + 5) Piercing damage.
Description: Hydras are enormous multi-headed predators that lurk in marshes and coastal shallows. Their regenerative biology allows them to sprout new heads when injured, making them exceptionally difficult to bring down. Each head acts independently, snapping at anything that comes close, turning the creature into a whirlwind of fangs and fury.

Name: Hyena
Traits: Pack Tactics. The hyena has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the hyena's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 3 (1d6) Piercing damage.
Description: Hyenas are scavenging and opportunistic predators that are found in a variety of habitats, known for their distinctive calls and their ability to work together in packs to take down larger prey.

Name: Ice Devil
Traits:
Diabolical Restoration. If the devil dies outside the Nine Hells, its form erupts into choking sulfurous vapor, and it immediately reforms elsewhere in the Nine Hells with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The devil has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The devil makes three Ice Spear attacks. It may replace one of these attacks with a Tail attack.

Ice Spear. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +10, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) Piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) Cold damage. Until the end of its next turn, the target cannot take a Bonus Action or Reaction, its Speed is reduced by 10 feet, and it can either move or take one action on its turn, not both. Hit or Miss: After a ranged attack, the spear instantly reappears in the devil’s grasp.

Ice Wall (Recharge 6). The devil casts Wall of Ice (8th‑level version) without components, using Intelligence as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17).

Tail. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 10 ft. Hit: 15 (3d6 + 5) Bludgeoning damage plus 18 (4d8) Cold damage.
Description: Ice devils are elite enforcers of the frozen hellscapes, combining ruthless discipline with lethal mastery over cold. Their conjured spears sap agility and willpower, while their towering ice walls reshape the battlefield. Among the infernal legions, they serve as tacticians and executioners, feared for their precision and unyielding loyalty.

Name: Ice Shardling
Traits: Shatter Burst. The shardling explodes into icy fragments when it dies. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 10, each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the shardling. Failure: 5 (2d4) Cold damage. Success: Half damage.
Actions:
Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 3 (1d4 + 1) Slashing damage plus 2 (1d4) Cold damage.

Mist Veil (1/Day). The shardling casts Fog Cloud, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability.

Frost Breath (Recharge 6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 10, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: 7 (3d4) Cold damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Ice Shardlings are small and mischievous elemental creatures that dwell in the Elemental Planes, known for their icy attacks and their ability to create fog and cold damage.

Name: Imp
Senses: Darkvision 120 ft. (unimpeded by magical Darkness), passive Perception 11
Traits: Magic Resistance. The imp has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Invisibility. The imp turns itself invisible, requiring no components and using Charisma as its spellcasting ability.

Sting. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Poison damage.

Shape-Shift. The imp transforms into a rat (Speed 20 ft.), a raven (Speed 20 ft., Fly 60 ft.), or a spider (Speed 20 ft., Climb 20 ft.), or returns to its true form. Its game statistics remain unchanged except for its Speed. Equipment worn or carried does not transform.
Description: Imps are sly, opportunistic devils often bound into service as familiars or spies. Their ability to vanish at will and assume small animal forms makes them excellent infiltrators, while their natural resistance to magic allows them to survive dangers that would destroy lesser creatures.

Name: Incubus
Traits: Succubus Form. When the incubus completes a Long Rest, it can assume the form of a Succubus, using that stat block instead of this one. Any equipment it carries or wears remains unchanged.
Actions:
Multiattack. The incubus makes two Restless Touch attacks.

Restless Touch. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 15 (3d6 + 5) Psychic damage, and the target becomes cursed for 24 hours or until the incubus dies. While cursed, the target gains no benefit from finishing Short Rests.

Spellcasting. The incubus casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15): At will: Disguise Self, Etherealness; 1/day each: Dream, Hypnotic Pattern
Bonus Actions: Nightmare (Recharge 6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 15, one creature the incubus can see within 60 feet. Failure: If the target has 20 Hit Points or fewer, it falls Unconscious for 1 hour, until it takes damage, or until a creature within 5 feet uses an action to wake it. Otherwise, the target takes 18 (4d8) Psychic damage.
Description: Incubi are manipulative fiends who thrive on emotional vulnerability and psychic influence. They slip between mortal societies with ease, shifting their appearance and invading dreams to weaken or control their targets. Their touch carries a lingering curse that drains resolve, making them dangerous predators of the mind as well as the body.

Name: Mind Eater
Languages: Understands Deep Speech but can't speak; telepathy 60 ft.
Traits: Psionic Locator. The mind eater senses the presence and direction of any creature with Intelligence 3 or higher within 300 feet, ignoring barriers.
Actions:
Multiattack. The mind eater makes one Claw attack and uses Mind Rend.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (2d4 + 2) Slashing damage.

Mind Rend. Intelligence Saving Throw: DC 12, one creature within 5 feet. Failure: 11 (2d10) Psychic damage, and the target is Stunned until the end of the mind eater's next turn.

Body Seizure. Intelligence Saving Throw: DC 12, one Small or Medium Humanoid or Beast within 5 feet that is Incapacitated and has 10 HP or fewer. Failure: The mind eater consumes the target’s neural core and teleports inside its skull, taking control of the body. While inside, it has Total Cover from outside effects. It retains its Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma, telepathy, and Psionic Locator trait, but otherwise uses the host’s statistics and memories.

If the host dies, the mind eater is expelled. It is also forced out if the host’s brain is restored by a Wish spell. The mind eater can voluntarily exit by spending 5 feet of movement, appearing in the nearest unoccupied space. The host dies unless its brain is restored before the end of the mind eater’s next turn.
Description: Mind Eaters are small and insidious aberrations that dwell in the Underdark, known for their ability to sense and consume the minds of their victims, taking control of their bodies and using them as hosts for their own survival.

Name: Invisible Stalker
Condition Immunities: exhaustion, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, unconscious
Traits:
Air Form. The stalker can enter an enemy's space and stop there. It can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.

Invisibility. The stalker has the Invisible condition.
Actions:
Multiattack. The stalker makes three Wind Swipe attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Vortex.

Wind Swipe. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Force damage.

Vortex. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 14, one Large or smaller creature in the stalker's space. Failure: 7 (1d8 + 3) Thunder damage, and the target has the Grappled condition (escape DC 13). Until the grapple ends, the target can't cast spells with a Verbal component and takes 7 (2d6) Thunder damage at the start of each of the stalker's turns.
Description: Invisible Stalkers are elusive and deadly elementals that serve as agents of powerful spellcasters, known for their ability to become invisible and their swift and silent movements.

Name: Iron Golem
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned
Languages: Understands Common plus two other languages but can't speak
Traits:
Fire Absorption. Whenever the golem is subjected to Fire damage, it regains a number of Hit Points equal to the Fire damage dealt.

Immutable Form. The golem can't shape-shift.

Magic Resistance. The golem has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The golem makes two attacks, using Bladed Arm or Fiery Bolt in any combination.

Bladed Arm. Melee Attack Roll: +12, reach 10 ft. Hit: 20 (3d8 + 7) Slashing damage plus 10 (3d6) Fire damage.

Fiery Bolt. Ranged Attack Roll: +10, range 120 ft. Hit: 36 (8d8) Fire damage.

Poison Breath (Recharge 6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 18, each creature in a 60-foot Cone. Failure: 55 (10d10) Poison damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Iron Golems are massive and powerful constructs that serve as guardians and enforcers, known for their incredible strength, their resistance to magic, and their devastating attacks that combine slashing and fire damage.

Name: Jackal
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +1, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 (1d4 - 1) Piercing damage.
Description: Jackals are small and agile predators that are found in deserts and grasslands, known for their keen senses and their ability to work together in packs to take down larger prey.

Name: Hekkylwere
Traits: Pack Tactics. The Hekkylwere has Advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of its allies is within 5 feet of the target and that ally is not Incapacitated.
Actions:
Multiattack. The Hekkylwere makes two attacks, choosing Rend or Slam.

Rend (Jackal or Hybrid Form Only). Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage.

Slam (Human or Hybrid Form Only). Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Bludgeoning damage.

Sleep Gaze (Recharge 5-6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 10, one creature the Hekkylwere can see within 30 feet (Constructs and Undead automatically succeed). Failure: The target falls Unconscious for 10 minutes, or until it takes damage or a creature within 5 feet uses an action to wake it. Success: The target becomes immune to this Hekkylwere’s Sleep Gaze for 24 hours.
Bonus Actions: Shape-Shift. The Hekkylwere transforms into a Medium human or a Medium jackal‑humanoid hybrid, or returns to its true form (a Small jackal). Aside from its size and movement options, its statistics remain unchanged. Equipment worn or carried does not transform.
Description: Hekkylweres are deceitful fiends that stalk open plains and arid regions, blending into mortal society through shapeshifting and ambush tactics. Their hypnotic gaze can drop victims into sudden slumber, allowing them to strike with ruthless efficiency. They hunt in coordinated packs, combining cunning and feral instinct.

Name: Juvenile Shadow Dragon
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 14
Traits:
Living Shadow. While in Dim Light or Darkness, the dragon has Resistance to damage that isn't Force, Psychic, or Radiant.

Sunlight Sensitivity. While in sunlight, the dragon has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 10 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) Necrotic damage.

Shadow Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 13, each creature in a 30-foot Cone. Failure: 17 (5d6) Necrotic damage. Success: Half damage. Failure or Success: A Humanoid reduced to 0 Hit Points by this damage dies, and a Shadow rises from its corpse. The shadow is under the dragon's control and shares the dragon's Initiative count but acts immediately after the dragon.
Bonus Actions: Shadow Stealth. While in Dim Light or Darkness, the dragon takes the Hide action.
Description: Juvenile Shadow Dragons are young and dangerous dragons that dwell in dark and shadowy environments, known for their necrotic breath weapon and their ability to blend into the darkness to ambush their prey.

Name: Echo Ravenfolk
Traits: Mimicry. The echo ravenfolk can mimic any sounds it has heard, including voices. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a successful DC 14 Wisdom (Insight) check.
Actions: Shadow Blade. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Necrotic damage. Hit or Miss: The blade magically returns to the echo ravenfolk's hand immediately after a ranged attack.
Bonus Actions: Eldritch Lantern (Recharge 4-6). The echo ravenfolk casts Faerie Fire, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 10).
Description: Echo ravenfolk are elusive avian humanoids known for their uncanny ability to reproduce any sound they hear. Their talent for mimicry makes them exceptional scouts, spies, and infiltrators. Many wander between shadowed forests and dim alleyways, trading information or performing covert tasks for those who earn their trust. Their conjured shadow blades and flickering lantern magic hint at a mysterious connection to the gloaming energies of the Shadowfell.

Name: Killer Whale
Traits: Hold Breath. The whale can hold its breath for 30 minutes.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 21 (5d6 + 4) Piercing damage.
Description: Killer Whales, also known as Orcas, are large and powerful marine predators that are found in oceans around the world, known for their intelligence, social behavior, and their ability to take down large prey with their powerful bites.

Name: Knight
Actions:
Multiattack. The knight makes two attacks, using Greatsword or Heavy Crossbow in any combination.

Greatsword. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) Radiant damage.

Heavy Crossbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +2, range 100/400 ft. Hit: 11 (2d10) Piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) Radiant damage.
Reactions: Parry. Trigger: The knight is hit by a melee attack roll while holding a weapon. Response: The knight adds 2 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.
Description: Knights are skilled and honorable warriors that serve as protectors and champions, known for their martial prowess, their code of conduct, and their ability to inspire others with their bravery and valor.

Name: Kobold Warrior
Traits:
Pack Tactics. The kobold has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the kobold's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

Sunlight Sensitivity. While in sunlight, the kobold has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.
Actions: Dagger. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Piercing damage.
Environment: Arctic, Coastal, Desert, Forest, Hill, Mountain, Swamp, Underdark, Urban
Description: Kobold Warriors are small and cunning reptilian humanoids that dwell in various environments, known for their ability to work together in packs and their skill with daggers and traps.

Name: Kraken
Languages: Understands Abyssal, Celestial, Infernal, and Primordial but can't speak; telepathy 120 ft.
Traits:
Amphibious. The kraken can breathe both air and water.

Legendary Resistance (4/Day, or 5/Day in Lair). If the kraken fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Siege Titan. The kraken deals double damage to objects and structures.
Actions:
Multiattack. The kraken makes two Tentacle attacks and uses Fling, Storm Bolt, or Swallow.

Tentacle. Melee Attack Roll: +17, reach 30 ft. Hit: 24 (4d6 + 10) Bludgeoning damage. The target is Grappled (escape DC 20) by one of the kraken’s ten tentacles and is Restrained until the grapple ends.

Fling. The kraken throws a Large or smaller creature it has Grappled to a space within 60 feet that is not in the air. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 25 for the thrown creature and any creature in the destination space. Failure: 18 (4d8) Bludgeoning damage and the target is knocked Prone. Success: Half damage.

Storm Bolt. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 23, one creature the kraken can see within 120 feet. Failure: 33 (6d10) Lightning damage. Success: Half damage.

Swallow. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 25, one creature Grappled by the kraken (up to four swallowed at once). Failure: 23 (3d8 + 10) Piercing damage and the target is swallowed. A swallowed creature is Restrained, has Total Cover, and takes 24 (7d6) Acid damage at the start of each of the kraken’s turns.If the kraken takes 50 or more damage in a single turn from inside, it must succeed on a DC 25 Constitution saving throw or expel all swallowed creatures, which fall Prone within 10 feet. If the kraken dies, swallowed creatures can escape using 15 feet of movement, exiting Prone.
Legendary Actions:
Storm Bolt. The kraken uses Storm Bolt.

Abyssal Ink. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 23, each creature in a 15-foot Emanation originating from the kraken while underwater. Failure: The creature is Blinded and Poisoned until the end of the kraken’s next turn. The kraken then moves up to its Speed. This action cannot be used again until the start of the kraken’s next turn.
Regional Effects:
The kraken’s presence warps the surrounding ocean:

Tyrant of the Deep. All Beasts within 1 mile of the lair are Charmed by the kraken’s overwhelming influence.

Stormcall. While in its lair, the kraken can cast Control Weather without components, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability.

These effects end immediately if the kraken dies or abandons its lair.
Description: Krakens are ancient sea titans feared across the world’s oceans. Their vast intelligence and raw physical might allow them to shatter ships, command storms, and drag entire crews into the depths. Legends speak of krakens as living catastrophes—creatures whose wrath can reshape coastlines and whose presence bends the will of lesser sea life.

Name: Brine-Scaled Skulker
Senses: Darkvision 120 ft., Truesight 30 ft., passive Perception 14
Traits:
Amphibious. The skulker can breathe air and water.

Light Sensitivity. While in direct sunlight, the skulker has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.
Actions:
Spear. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 5 (1d8 + 1) Piercing damage.

Gum Net (1/Day). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 10, one Large or smaller creature the skulker can see within 15 feet. Failure: The target is Restrained by a sticky net until it is destroyed (AC 10; HP 5; immune to Bludgeoning, Poison, and Psychic damage). A creature can take an action to make a DC 10 Strength (Athletics) check to free itself or another creature within 5 feet, destroying the net on a success.
Reactions: Clinging Shield. Trigger: A creature misses the skulker with a melee weapon attack. Response—Strength Saving Throw: DC 11, the triggering creature. Failure: The weapon sticks to the skulker's shield. If the creature doesn't release the weapon, it is Grappled (escape DC 11) while the weapon is stuck. The weapon can't be used while stuck. The creature can take an action to make a DC 11 Strength (Athletics) check, freeing the weapon on a success.
Description: Brine-Scaled Skulkers are small and elusive aberrations that dwell in coastal and underwater environments, known for their ability to ambush prey with spears and their use of sticky nets to restrain victims.

Name: Brine-Scaled High Oracle
Senses: Darkvision 120 ft., Truesight 30 ft., passive Perception 19
Traits:
Amphibious. The oracle can breathe air and water.

Light Sensitivity. While in sunlight, the oracle has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.
Actions:
Multiattack. The oracle makes three Storm Scepter attacks.

Spellcasting. The oracle casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14): At will: Detect Magic, Thaumaturgy; 1/day each: Destructive Wave, Divination, Hold Monster (level 6 version), Scrying, Tongues

Storm Scepter. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 20 (5d6 + 3) Lightning damage.
Bonus Actions: Abyssal Ward (2/Day). The oracle casts Shield of Faith, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Brine-Scaled High Oracles are powerful and enigmatic aberrations that serve as seers and mystics, known for their ability to cast spells, their control over lightning, and their role as intermediaries between the mortal world and the mysterious forces of the sea.

Name: Brine-Scaled Enforcer
Senses: Darkvision 120 ft., Truesight 30 ft., passive Perception 16
Traits:
Amphibious. The enforcer can breathe air and water.

Light Sensitivity. While in sunlight, the enforcer has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.
Actions:
Multiattack. The enforcer makes two Spine Lash attacks.

Spine Lash. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 10 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Lightning damage, and the target can't make Opportunity Attacks until the start of the enforcer's next turn.
Description: Brine-Scaled Enforcers are formidable and relentless aberrations that serve as guardians and enforcers, known for their powerful spine lash attacks that combine slashing and lightning damage, and their ability to hinder their foes' movements in combat.

Name: Brine-Scaled Zealot
Senses: Darkvision 120 ft., Truesight 30 ft., passive Perception 16
Traits:
Amphibious. The zealot can breathe air and water.

Light Sensitivity. While in sunlight, the zealot has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.
Actions:
Hooked Staff. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 10 ft. Hit: 9 (2d6 + 2) Piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is Grappled (escape DC 12). Until the grapple ends, the zealot can't make Hooked Staff attacks.

Hurl Caustic Glob. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 60 ft. Hit: 9 (3d4 + 2) Acid damage.
Bonus Actions: Abyssal Ward (2/Day). The zealot casts Shield of Faith, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability.
Description: Brine-Scaled Zealots are fanatical and devoted aberrations that serve as loyal followers and fierce combatants, known for their unwavering dedication to their cause and their ability to cast spells.

Name: Lacedon Ghoul
Actions:
Multiattack. The ghoul makes two Icy Bite attacks.

Icy Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (2d6 + 2) Cold damage, and the target’s Speed is reduced by 5 feet until the start of the ghoul’s next turn.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Slashing damage. If the target is a creature that is not an Undead or an elf, it must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or become Paralyzed until the end of its next turn.
Bonus Actions: Watery Rush. While underwater, the ghoul moves up to half its Swim Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.
Description: Lacedon ghouls are aquatic variants of common ghouls, haunting drowned ruins, murky swamps, and coastal shallows. Their freezing bite slows prey in the water, while their claws can lock victims in place with a sudden paralytic jolt. These undead hunters often lurk beneath the surface, striking from below with unnerving speed.

Name: Lamia
Actions:
Multiattack. The lamia makes two Claw attacks. It may replace one of these attacks with Corrupting Touch.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Psychic damage.

Corrupting Touch. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 13, one creature the lamia can see within 5 feet. Failure: 13 (3d8) Psychic damage, and the target becomes cursed for 1 hour. While cursed, the target is both Charmed and Poisoned.

Spellcasting. The lamia casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13): At will: Disguise Self (may appear as a Large or Medium biped), Minor Illusion; 1/day each: Geas, Major Image, Scrying
Bonus Actions: Leap. The lamia jumps up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.
Description: Lamias are predatory fiends that haunt desolate deserts, weaving illusions and deceit to lure travelers into their grasp. Their claws tear flesh while their corrupting magic twists the mind, leaving victims enthralled and weakened. Masters of disguise and manipulation, lamias often rule over hidden lairs filled with stolen treasures and beguiled servants.

Name: Larva
Languages: Understands Common plus one other language but can't speak
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +1, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 (1d4 - 1) Necrotic damage.
Description: Larvae are small and weak fiends that dwell in the lower planes, known for their necrotic bite attacks and their ability to survive in harsh and inhospitable environments.

Name: Lemure
Senses: Darkvision 120 ft. (unimpeded by magical Darkness), passive Perception 10
Traits: Hellish Restoration. If the lemure dies in the Nine Hells, it reforms with full Hit Points after 1d10 days unless it is slain by a creature under the effects of a Bless spell or its remains are purified with Holy Water.
Actions: Vile Slime. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 2 (1d4) Poison damage.
Description: Lemures are the lowest rung of infernal existence—mindless, shapeless masses of flesh molded into obedient servants. They shuffle through the Nine Hells as expendable troops, endlessly recycled unless destroyed by holy means. Though individually weak, their persistence and sheer numbers make them a constant presence in infernal armies.

Name: Lich
Traits:
Legendary Resistance (4/Day, or 5/Day in Lair). If the lich fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Spirit Jar. If the lich is destroyed, its essence reforms a new body after 1d10 days as long as its spirit jar remains intact. The new body appears in an unoccupied space within the lich’s lair and returns with full Hit Points.
Actions:
Multiattack. The lich makes three attacks, choosing Eldritch Burst or Paralyzing Touch in any combination.

Eldritch Burst. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +12, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 31 (4d12 + 5) Force damage.

Paralyzing Touch. Melee Attack Roll: +12, reach 5 ft. Hit: 15 (3d6 + 5) Cold damage, and the target becomes Paralyzed until the start of the lich’s next turn.

Spellcasting. The lich casts one of the following spells using Intelligence as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 20): At will: Detect Magic, Detect Thoughts, Dispel Magic, Fireball (5th‑level), Invisibility, Lightning Bolt (5th‑level), Mage Hand, Prestidigitation; 2/day each: Animate Dead, Dimension Door, Plane Shift; 1/day each: Chain Lightning, Finger of Death, Power Word Kill, Scrying
Reactions: Protective Magic. The lich casts Counterspell or Shield in response to the appropriate trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as its Spellcasting action.
Legendary Actions:
Deathly Teleport. The lich teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. Each creature within 10 feet of the space it left takes 11 (2d10) Necrotic damage.

Disrupt Life. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 20, each creature that isn’t Undead in a 20‑foot Emanation centered on the lich. Failure: 31 (9d6) Necrotic damage. Success: Half damage. The lich cannot use this action again until the start of its next turn.

Frightening Gaze. The lich casts Fear using the same spellcasting ability as its Spellcasting action. The lich cannot use this action again until the start of its next turn.
Regional Effects:
A lich’s lair twists the surrounding region with its necromantic influence:

All‑Seeing. While inside its lair, the lich can cast Clairvoyance without components, using the same spellcasting ability as its Spellcasting action.

Inevitable Siphon. When a Humanoid dies within 1 mile of the lair, the lich instantly consumes its soul. A soul taken this way can only be restored by True Resurrection or Wish.

These effects end if the lich is destroyed or relocates its lair, and resume if it reforms via its Spirit Jar trait.
Description: Liches are master spellcasters who have traded mortality for eternal undeath, binding their souls to phylacteries to ensure their return. Their intellect and magical power make them terrifying foes, capable of reshaping battles with devastating spells and draining the life from those who dare oppose them. Many liches rule hidden sanctums, plotting across centuries with cold, calculating patience.

Name: Lion
Traits:
Pack Tactics. The lion has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the lion's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

Running Leap. With a 10-foot running start, the lion can Long Jump up to 25 feet.
Actions:
Multiattack. The lion makes two Rend attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Roar.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Slashing damage.

Roar. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 11, one creature within 15 feet. Failure: The target has the Frightened condition until the start of the lion's next turn.
Description: Lions are majestic and powerful beasts that roam the savannas and grasslands, known for their strength, agility, and social behavior, often hunting in prides to take down larger prey.

Name: Lizard
Traits: Spider Climb. The lizard can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Piercing damage.
Description: Lizards are small and adaptable reptiles that can be found in a variety of environments, known for their ability to climb surfaces and their quick reflexes, often serving as prey for larger predators.

Name: Lizardfolk Geomancer
Actions:
Multiattack. The lizardfolk makes two Earth Burst attacks.

Earth Burst. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. Hit: 9 (2d6 + 2) Bludgeoning damage.

Hail of Stone (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 20-foot-radius, 40-foot-high Cylinder centered on a point the lizardfolk can see within 60 feet. Failure: 15 (6d4) Bludgeoning damage, and the target has the Prone condition. Success: Half damage only.

Spellcasting. The lizardfolk casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12): At will: Elementalism; 1/day each: Meld into Stone, Speak with Plants, Spike Growth
Description: Lizardfolk Geomancers are elemental creatures that dwell in forest and swamp environments, known for their connection to the earth and their ability to cast spells that manipulate stone and plants, as well as their powerful Earth Burst attacks.

Name: Lizardfolk Sovereign
Actions:
Multiattack. The lizardfolk makes one Bite attack and one Earthen Maul attack.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is a creature that isn't a Construct or an Undead, the lizardfolk gains Temporary Hit Points equal to the damage dealt.

Earthen Maul. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.
Bonus Actions: Charge. The lizardfolk moves up to its Speed or Swim Speed straight toward an enemy it can see.
Description: Lizardfolk Sovereigns are powerful and commanding elemental creatures that serve as leaders and rulers among their kind, known for their ability to inspire and protect their allies, as well as their formidable combat abilities.

Name: Mage
Actions:
Multiattack. The mage makes three Arcane Burst attacks.

Arcane Burst. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 16 (3d8 + 3) Force damage.

Spellcasting. The mage casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14): At will: Detect Magic, Light, Mage Armor (included in AC), Mage Hand, Prestidigitation; 2/day each: Fireball (level 4 version), Invisibility; 1/day each: Cone of Cold, Fly
Bonus Actions: Misty Step (3/Day). The mage casts Misty Step, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Reactions: Protective Magic (3/Day). The mage casts Counterspell or Shield in response to the spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Mages are skilled and knowledgeable spellcasters who have dedicated their lives to the study of magic, known for their ability to cast a wide variety of spells, their formidable Arcane Burst attacks, and their role as powerful and influential figures in the world of magic.

Name: Mage Apprentice
Actions:
Arcane Burst. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 14 (2d10 + 3) Force damage.

Spellcasting. The mage casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13, +5 to hit with spell attacks): At will: Mage Hand, Prestidigitation; 1/day each: Disguise Self, Ice Knife, Mage Armor (included in AC), Thunderwave
Description: Mage Apprentices are aspiring and eager spellcasters who are in the early stages of their magical training, known for their ability to cast a limited selection of spells, their developing Arcane Burst attacks, and their potential to grow into powerful mages in the future.

Name: Magma Shardling
Traits: Shatter Burst. The shardling erupts in molten fragments when it dies. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 11, each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the shardling. Failure: 7 (2d6) Fire damage. Success: Half damage.
Actions:
Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 3 (1d4 + 1) Slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) Fire damage.

Lava Breath (Recharge 6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 11, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: 7 (2d6) Fire damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Magma Shardlings are small and volatile elemental creatures that dwell in the fiery environments of the Elemental Planes, known for their explosive nature, their ability to fly, and their dangerous attacks that combine physical strikes with fiery damage.

Name: Magmin
Traits: Death Burst. The magmin explodes when it dies. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 11, each creature in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the magmin. Failure: 7 (2d6) Fire damage. Success: Half damage.
Actions: Touch. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (2d4 + 2) Fire damage. If the target is a creature or a flammable object that isn't being worn or carried, it starts burning.
Bonus Actions: Ignited Illumination. The magmin sets itself ablaze or extinguishes its flames. While ablaze, the magmin sheds Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet.
Description: Magmins are small and mischievous elemental creatures that dwell in the fiery environments of the Elemental Plane of Fire, known for their explosive nature, their ability to ignite themselves and others, and their role as both pests and playthings in the fiery realms.

Name: Mammoth
Actions:
Multiattack. The mammoth makes two Gore attacks.

Gore. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 10 ft. Hit: 18 (2d10 + 7) Piercing damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature and the mammoth moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target has the Prone condition.
Bonus Actions: Trample. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 18, one creature within 5 feet that has the Prone condition. Failure: 29 (4d10 + 7) Bludgeoning damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Mammoths are massive and powerful beasts that roam the frozen tundras and arctic regions, known for their thick fur, long tusks, and formidable strength, often serving as mounts or beasts of burden for those who can tame them.

Name: Manes
Actions: Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (2d4) Slashing damage.
Description: Manes are the most basic and disposable form of demon, created from tormented mortal souls and used as fodder in the endless conflicts of the Abyss. They possess little will or intelligence, shambling forward in overwhelming numbers to claw at anything their demonic masters command them to attack.

Name: Manes Vaporspawn
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, poisoned, restrained
Traits:
Contortionist. The manes can squeeze through openings as narrow as 1 inch without spending extra movement.

Sickening Vapors. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 5-foot Emanation centered on the manes at the end of its turn. Failure: The creature becomes Incapacitated until the end of its next turn. Success: The creature is immune to this manes’s Sickening Vapors for 24 hours.
Actions: Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Slashing damage plus 5 (2d4) Necrotic damage.
Bonus Actions: Shadow Stealth. While in Dim Light or Darkness, the manes takes the Hide action.
Description: Manes Vaporspawn are twisted evolutions of lesser manes, their bodies leaking noxious fumes that overwhelm nearby creatures. More agile and dangerous than their base form, these demons slip through impossibly tight spaces and strike from the shadows, spreading their debilitating vapors as they hunt.

Name: Manticore
Actions:
Multiattack. The manticore makes three attacks, using Rend or Tail Spike in any combination.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Slashing damage.

Tail Spike. Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 100/200 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Piercing damage.
Description: Manticores are large, cat-like creatures with a scorpion's tail and a fearsome reputation for their deadly attacks and their ability to fly.

Name: Marid
Senses: Blindsight 30 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 13
Traits:
Amphibious. The marid can breathe air and water.

Elemental Restoration. If the marid dies outside the Elemental Plane of Water, its body dissolves into brine, and it gains a new body in 1d4 days, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere on the Plane of Water.

Wishes. The marid has a 30 percent chance of knowing the Wish spell. If the marid knows it, the marid can cast it only on behalf of a non-genie creature who communicates a wish in a way the marid can understand. If the marid casts the spell for the creature, the marid suffers none of the spell's stress. Once the marid has cast it three times, the marid can't do so again for 365 days.
Actions:
Multiattack. The marid makes three Aquatic Lash attacks.

Aquatic Lash. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 15 ft. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) Slashing damage plus 9 (2d8) Cold damage.

Spellcasting. The marid casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 16): At will: Create or Destroy Water, Detect Evil and Good, Detect Magic, Purify Food and Drink; 1/day each: Control Water, Gaseous Form, Invisibility, Plane Shift, Tongues

Water Jet. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 18, each creature in a 60-foot-long, 10-foot-wide Line. Failure: 31 (9d6) Cold damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it is pushed up to 20 feet straight away from the marid and has the Prone condition. Success: Half damage only.
Bonus Actions: Misty Veil (Recharge 5-6). The marid casts Fog Cloud, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Environment: Coastal, Planar (Elemental Plane of Water), Underwater
Description: Marids are powerful and majestic genies that dwell in the watery realms of the Elemental Plane of Water, known for their control over water and their ability to grant wishes, as well as their formidable combat abilities and their role as both allies and adversaries in the world of genies.

Name: Marilith
Traits:
Demonic Restoration. If the marilith dies outside the Abyss, its body collapses into foul ichor, and it instantly reforms elsewhere in the Abyss with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The marilith has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Reactive. The marilith can take one Reaction on every turn in combat.
Actions:
Multiattack. The marilith makes six Pact Blade attacks and uses Constrict.

Pact Blade. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (1d10 + 5) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Necrotic damage.

Constrict. Strength Saving Throw: DC 17, one Medium or smaller creature the marilith can see within 5 feet. Failure: 15 (2d10 + 4) Bludgeoning damage, and the target becomes Grappled (escape DC 14) and Restrained until the grapple ends.
Bonus Actions: Teleport (Recharge 5-6). The marilith teleports up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.
Reactions: Parry. Trigger: The marilith is hit by a melee attack while holding a weapon. Response: The marilith adds 5 to its AC against that attack, potentially causing it to miss.
Description: Mariliths are elite demon warlords, infamous for their six-armed mastery of combat and their serpentine agility. They command demonic legions with ruthless precision, cutting down foes with a whirlwind of blades while teleporting across the battlefield to strike where they are least expected. Their tactical brilliance and ferocity make them among the most feared generals of the Abyss.

Name: Mastiff
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d6 + 1) Piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.
Description: Mastiffs are large and loyal dogs that are often used as guard animals or companions, known for their protective nature, their keen senses, and their ability to intimidate and subdue threats with their powerful bites.

Name: Medusa
Actions:
