Multiattack. The medusa makes two Claw attacks and one Snake Hair attack, or it makes three Poison Ray attacks.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Slashing damage.

Snake Hair. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) Poison damage.

Poison Ray. Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 150 ft. Hit: 11 (2d8 + 2) Poison damage.
Bonus Actions: Petrifying Gaze (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 13, each creature in a 30‑foot Cone. If the medusa sees its own reflection within the Cone, it must also make this save. First Failure: The creature becomes Restrained and repeats the save at the end of its next turn if still Restrained, ending the effect on a success. Second Failure: The creature becomes Petrified instead of Restrained.
Description: Medusas are cursed beings whose serpentine hair and venomous magic make them deadly predators. Their gaze can slowly harden flesh into stone, trapping victims in a creeping petrification. Skilled in deception and ambush, medusas often dwell in isolated ruins or desert lairs, surrounding themselves with statues of those who underestimated them.

Name: Merfolk Skirmisher
Traits: Amphibious. The merfolk can breathe air and water.
Actions: Ocean Spear. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 3 (1d6) Piercing damage plus 2 (1d4) Cold damage. If the target is a creature, its Speed decreases by 10 feet until the end of its next turn. Hit or Miss: The spear magically returns to the merfolk's hand immediately after a ranged attack.
Description: Merfolk Skirmishers are agile and quick-witted elemental creatures that dwell in coastal and underwater environments, known for their ability to breathe both air and water, their proficiency with spears, and their role as scouts and skirmishers in aquatic settings.

Name: Merfolk Wavebender
Traits: Amphibious. The merfolk can breathe air and water.
Actions:
Multiattack. The merfolk makes two Aquatic Burst attacks.

Aquatic Burst. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. Hit: 20 (3d10 + 4) Cold damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

Spellcasting. The merfolk casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15): At will: Elementalism, Light; 1/day each: Control Water, Create or Destroy Water
Reactions: Watery Rebuke. Trigger: An enemy the merfolk can see enters a space within 5 feet of the merfolk. Response—Strength Saving Throw: DC 15, the triggering enemy. Failure: 14 (4d6) Cold damage. If the target is Large or smaller, it is pushed up to 30 feet straight away from the merfolk by conjured water.
Description: Merfolk Wavebenders are powerful and commanding elemental creatures that dwell in coastal and underwater environments, known for their ability to control water, their proficiency with cold-based attacks, and their role as leaders and spellcasters among aquatic beings.

Name: Merrow
Traits: Amphibious. The merrow can breathe air and water.
Actions:
Multiattack. The merrow makes two attacks, using Bite, Claw, or Harpoon in any combination.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d4 + 4) Piercing damage, and the target has the Poisoned condition until the end of the merrow's next turn.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (2d4 + 4) Slashing damage.

Harpoon. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, the merrow pulls the target up to 15 feet straight toward itself.
Description: Merrows are brutish and aggressive aquatic creatures that dwell in coastal and underwater environments, known for their amphibious nature, their proficiency with harpoons, and their role as marauders and predators in aquatic settings.

Name: Mezzorak Bladebound
Senses: Blindsight 60 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 15
Traits:
Fiendish Restoration. If the Mezzorak Bladebound dies outside Gehenna, its form melts into caustic sludge, and it immediately reforms elsewhere in Gehenna with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The Mezzorak Bladebound has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The Mezzorak Bladebound makes two attacks, choosing Claws or Mercurial Trident in any combination.

Claws. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (2d4 + 4) Slashing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it becomes Grappled (escape DC 14) by two of the fiend’s four claws and is Restrained until the grapple ends.

Mercurial Trident. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) Piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) Force damage. Hit or Miss: After a ranged attack, the trident instantly reappears in the Mezzorak Bladebound’s grasp.

Spellcasting. The Mezzorak Bladebound casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Wisdom as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13):; 1/day each: Cloudkill, Darkness, Dispel Magic
Bonus Actions: Teleport (Recharge 5-6). The Mezzorak Bladebound teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. If it is grappling a creature, it may teleport that creature to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of its destination.
Description: Mezzorak Bladebounds are ruthless yugoloth enforcers forged in the volatile slopes of Gehenna. Their four bladed limbs can seize and immobilize foes, while their enchanted tridents strike with explosive planar force. As mercenaries, they are valued for their brutality, resilience, and their unnerving ability to reconstitute themselves when slain beyond their home plane.

Name: Mimic
Traits: Adhesive (Object Form Only). The mimic secretes a resinous film that bonds instantly to anything touching its surface. A Huge or smaller creature adhered to the mimic is Grappled (escape DC 13), and checks to escape have Disadvantage.
Actions:
Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5 (with Advantage if the target is Grappled), reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Piercing damage—or 12 (2d8 + 3) if the target is Grappled—plus 4 (1d8) Acid damage.

Pseudopod. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Bludgeoning damage plus 4 (1d8) Acid damage. If the target is Large or smaller, it becomes Grappled (escape DC 13), and checks to escape have Disadvantage.
Bonus Actions: Shape-Shift. The mimic alters its outer membrane to crystallize into the texture and shape of a Medium or Small object, though its internal mass remains amorphous. Its statistics do not change.
Description: Mimics are shape-shifting monstrosities that can take the form of inanimate objects, known for their adhesive properties, their ability to ambush unsuspecting prey, and their role as both predators and guardians in various environments.

Name: Psion Devourer
Traits: Magic Resistance. The psion devourer has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Spellcasting. The psion devourer casts one of the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15): At will: Detect Thoughts; 1/day each: Dominate Monster, Plane Shift (self only)

Psychic Tendrils. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 22 (4d8 + 4) Psychic damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is Grappled (escape DC 14) by the devourer's tendrils, and the target is Stunned until the grapple ends.

Cerebral Extraction. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 15, one creature Grappled by the devourer's Psychic Tendrils. Failure: 55 (10d10) Piercing damage. Success: Half damage. If this damage reduces the target to 0 Hit Points, the devourer kills it and consumes its mind.

Psionic Burst (Recharge 5-6). Intelligence Saving Throw: DC 15, each creature in a 60-foot Cone. Failure: 31 (6d8 + 4) Psychic damage, and the target is Stunned until the end of the devourer's next turn. Success: Half damage only.
Description: Psion Devourers are sinister and malevolent aberrations that dwell in the Underdark, known for their psychic abilities, their hunger for minds, and their role as both predators and manipulators in the dark and twisted realms of the Underdark.

Name: Psion Archon
Traits: Magic Resistance. The psion archon has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The psion archon makes three Psychic Lash attacks.

Psychic Lash. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 27 (4d10 + 5) Psychic damage, and the archon can teleport the target up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see on a surface or liquid capable of supporting the target. If this damage reduces the target to 0 Hit Points, the archon kills it and consumes its mind essence.

Psionic Shockwave (Recharge 5-6). Intelligence Saving Throw: DC 17, each creature in a 40-foot Emanation originating from the archon. Failure: 41 (8d8 + 5) Psychic damage, and the target is Stunned until the end of the archon's next turn. Success: Half damage only.

Spellcasting. The psion archon casts one of the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17): At will: Detect Magic, Detect Thoughts, Disguise Self, Mage Hand (the hand is Invisible); 1/day each: Clairvoyance, Dimension Door, Fireball (level 5 version), Lightning Bolt (level 5 version), Plane Shift (self only), Sending
Reactions: Psychic Shield (2/Day). The psion archon manifests a protective barrier in response to that spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Psion Archons are powerful and enigmatic aberrations that dwell in the Underdark, known for their psychic prowess, their ability to manipulate minds and space, and their role as both predators and enigmatic entities in the dark and mysterious realms of the Underdark.

Name: Minotaur Skeleton
Actions:
Gore. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature and the skeleton moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 9 (2d8) Piercing damage and has the Prone condition.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Minotaur Skeletons are reanimated remains of minotaurs, often created through necromantic magic, known for their relentless aggression, their powerful gore attacks, and their role as guardians or minions in various settings, particularly in the Shadowfell and the Underdark.

Name: Minotaur of Baphomet
Actions:
Abyssal Glaive. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. Hit: 10 (1d12 + 4) Slashing damage plus 10 (3d6) Necrotic damage.

Gore (Recharge 5-6). Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 18 (4d6 + 4) Piercing damage. If the minotaur moved 10 or more feet straight toward the target immediately before the hit and the target is Large or smaller, it takes an additional 10 (3d6) Piercing damage and is knocked Prone.
Description: Minotaurs of Baphomet are savage brutes empowered by the Prince of Beasts. Wielding corrupted glaives and driven by feral cunning, they stalk the twisting tunnels of the Underdark as hunters, executioners, and champions of their demonic master. Their brutal charges and abyss‑tainted weapons make them terrifying foes in close‑quarters combat.

Name: Bicron
Traits: System Collapse. When the bicron is destroyed, its body collapses into fine metallic dust, leaving behind anything it was wearing or carrying.
Actions:
Multiattack. The bicron makes two Gyro Blade attacks.

Gyro Blade. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. or range 30 ft. Hit: 4 (1d6 + 1) Force damage. Hit or Miss: The blade instantly re-materializes in the bicron's hand after a ranged attack.
Description: Bicrons are small and efficient constructs that serve as laborers and messengers in the mechanized realms, known for their compact design, their proficiency with gyro blades, and their role as both workers and defenders in the orderly and clockwork-like environments of the mechanized planes.

Name: Unicron
Traits: System Collapse. When the unicron is destroyed, its body dissolves into fine metallic dust, leaving behind anything it was wearing or carrying.
Actions:
Gear Strike. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Force damage.

Gear Launcher. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 120 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Force damage.
Description: Unicrons are small and basic constructs that serve as simple laborers and messengers in the mechanized realms, known for their minimalistic design, their proficiency with basic gear-based attacks, and their role as both workers and minor defenders in the orderly and clockwork-like environments of the mechanized planes.

Name: Pentacron
Traits: System Collapse. When the pentacron is destroyed, its body disintegrates into metallic dust, leaving behind anything it was wearing or carrying.
Actions:
Multiattack. The pentacron makes five Slam attacks or five Arc Discharge attacks.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Force damage.

Arc Discharge. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 120 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Lightning damage.

Stasis Gas (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 11, each creature in a 30-foot Cone. Failure: The target is Paralyzed and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.
Description: Pentacrons are large and formidable constructs that serve as guardians and enforcers in the mechanized realms, known for their robust design, their proficiency with both physical and electrical attacks, and their role as powerful defenders in the orderly and clockwork-like environments of the mechanized planes.

Name: Quadricron
Traits: System Collapse. When the quadricron is destroyed, its body dissolves into metallic dust, leaving behind anything it was wearing or carrying.
Actions:
Multiattack. The quadricron makes four Slam attacks or four Gear Barrage attacks.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Force damage.

Gear Barrage. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 320 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Force damage.
Description: Quadricrons are medium-sized and versatile constructs that serve as messengers and minor enforcers in the mechanized realms, known for their agile design, their proficiency with both physical and ranged attacks, and their role as adaptable workers and defenders in the orderly and clockwork-like environments of the mechanized planes.

Name: Tricron
Traits: System Collapse. When the tricron is destroyed, its body disintegrates into metallic dust, leaving behind anything it was wearing or carrying.
Actions:
Multiattack. The tricron makes three Vector Spear attacks.

Vector Spear. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 4 (1d6 + 1) Force damage. Hit or Miss: The spear instantly re-materializes in the tricron's hand after a ranged attack.
Description: Tricrons are small and efficient constructs that serve as messengers and minor laborers in the mechanized realms, known for their compact design, their proficiency with vector spears, and their role as both workers and minor defenders in the orderly and clockwork-like environments of the mechanized planes.

Name: Mud Shardling
Traits: Shatter Burst. The shardling collapses into a surge of clinging sludge when it dies. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 11, each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the shardling. Failure: The target is Restrained until the end of its next turn.
Actions:
Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d6 + 1) Bludgeoning damage.

Mire Breath (Recharge 6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 11, one creature the shardling can see within 15 feet. Failure: The target is Restrained until the end of the shardling's next turn.
Description: Mud Shardlings are small and elusive elementals that dwell in the murky and treacherous environments of the Elemental Planes, known for their ability to blend into their surroundings, their proficiency with both physical attacks and their mire breath, and their role as both predators and survivors in the harsh and unforgiving realms of the Elemental Planes.

Name: Mule
Traits: Beast of Burden. The mule counts as one size larger for the purpose of determining its carrying capacity.
Actions: Hooves. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Mules are sturdy and reliable beasts of burden that are commonly used for transportation and carrying loads, known for their endurance, their ability to navigate difficult terrain, and their role as both working animals and companions in various settings.

Name: Mummy
Actions:
Multiattack. The mummy makes two Rotting Fist attacks and uses Dreadful Glare.

Rotting Fist. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) Bludgeoning damage plus 10 (3d6) Necrotic damage. If the target is a creature, it is cursed. While cursed, the target can't regain Hit Points, its Hit Point maximum doesn't return to normal when finishing a Long Rest, and its Hit Point maximum decreases by 10 (3d6) every 24 hours that elapse. A creature dies and turns to dust if reduced to 0 Hit Points by this attack.

Dreadful Glare. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 11, one creature the mummy can see within 60 feet. Failure: The target has the Frightened condition until the end of the mummy's next turn. Success: The target is immune to this mummy's Dreadful Glare for 24 hours.
Description: Mummies are reanimated corpses that have been preserved through magical means, often serving as guardians of tombs and ancient sites, known for their decaying touch, their ability to instill fear, and their role as both protectors and threats in various settings, particularly in desert and swamp environments.

Name: Mummy Lord
Traits:
Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the mummy fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Magic Resistance. The mummy has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Undead Restoration. If the mummy is destroyed, it reforms a new body after 24 hours as long as its preserved heart remains intact. The new body appears in an unoccupied space within the mummy’s lair and returns with full Hit Points. The heart is a Tiny object with AC 17, 10 Hit Points, and Immunity to all damage except Fire.
Actions:
Multiattack. The mummy makes one Rotting Fist or Channel Negative Energy attack, and it uses Dreadful Glare.

Rotting Fist. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) Bludgeoning damage plus 10 (3d6) Necrotic damage. A creature hit by this attack is cursed. While cursed, the creature cannot regain Hit Points, gains no benefit from finishing a Long Rest, and its Hit Point maximum decreases by 10 (3d6) every 24 hours. A creature reduced to 0 Hit Points by this effect crumbles into dust.

Channel Negative Energy. Ranged Attack Roll: +9, range 60 ft. Hit: 25 (6d6 + 4) Necrotic damage.

Dreadful Glare. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 17, one creature the mummy can see within 60 feet. Failure: 25 (6d6 + 4) Psychic damage, and the creature becomes Paralyzed until the end of the mummy’s next turn.

Spellcasting. The mummy casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Wisdom as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17, +9 to hit with spell attacks): At will: Dispel Magic, Thaumaturgy; 1/day each: Animate Dead, Harm, Insect Plague (7th‑level)
Reactions: Whirlwind of Sand. Trigger: The mummy is hit by an attack. Response: The mummy gains +2 AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss, and teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. Each creature of its choice within 5 feet of the destination is Blinded until the end of the mummy’s next turn.
Legendary Actions:
Dread Command. The mummy casts Command (2nd‑level), using the same spellcasting ability as its Spellcasting action. The mummy cannot use this action again until the start of its next turn.

Glare. The mummy uses Dreadful Glare. The mummy cannot use this action again until the start of its next turn.

Necrotic Strike. The mummy makes one Rotting Fist or Channel Negative Energy attack.
Regional Effects:
A mummy lord’s lair radiates a lingering curse that alters the surrounding region:

Cursed Fate. When a creature other than the mummy or its allies casts a Divination spell within 1 mile of the lair, it must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or the spell fizzles with no effect. The action, Bonus Action, or Reaction used to cast the spell is wasted, but spell slots or other resources are not expended.

Soul Drain. Creatures within 1 mile of the lair have Disadvantage on Death Saving Throws.

These effects end if the mummy lord is destroyed or relocates its lair. They resume if the mummy reforms via its Undead Restoration trait.
Description: Mummy Lords are ancient undead priests preserved through dark rites, ruling over tombs and forgotten temples with unwavering authority. Their curses drain vitality over time, their magic commands the dead, and their preserved hearts anchor them to unlife, allowing them to rise again even after apparent destruction. Revered as tyrants or feared as divine punishments, they are among the most formidable undead found in desert and swamp ruins.

Name: Sporekin Adult
Traits: Sun Frailty. While in sunlight, the sporekin has Disadvantage on D20 Tests and dies if it spends more than 1 hour in sunlight.
Actions:
Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d8) Bludgeoning damage plus 3 (1d6) Poison damage.

Pacifying Spores (1/Day). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 11, one creature the sporekin can see within 10 feet. Failure: The target is Stunned and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.

Mindlink Spores. The sporekin releases spores in a 30-foot Emanation. Creatures with Intelligence 2+ that aren't Constructs, Elementals, or Undead gain telepathy (30 ft.) for 1 hour.
Description: Sporekin Adults are mature and resilient plant creatures that thrive in the dark and damp environments of the Underdark, known for their ability to release spores that can pacify or communicate with other creatures, their vulnerability to sunlight, and their role as both guardians and symbiotic organisms in the subterranean ecosystems of the Underdark.

Name: Sporekin Sovereign
Traits: Sun Frailty. While in sunlight, the sporekin has Disadvantage on D20 Tests and dies if it spends more than 1 hour in sunlight.
Actions:
Multiattack. The sovereign makes one Slam attack and uses Pacifying Spores.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) Bludgeoning damage plus 5 (2d4) Poison damage.

Animating Spores (3/Day). The sovereign releases spores at a Medium or Small corpse within 5 feet that wasn't a Construct or Undead. In 24 hours, it rises as a Sporekin Husk for 1d4 + 1 weeks.

Pacifying Spores. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12, one creature the sovereign can see within 10 feet. Failure: The target is Stunned and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.

Mindlink Spores. The sovereign releases spores in a 30-foot Emanation. Creatures with Intelligence 2+ that aren't Constructs, Elementals, or Undead gain telepathy (30 ft.) for 1 hour.
Description: Sporekin Sovereigns are powerful and influential plant creatures that serve as leaders and protectors of sporekin communities in the Underdark, known for their ability to release spores that can animate the dead or pacify other creatures, their vulnerability to sunlight, and their role as both rulers and guardians in the subterranean ecosystems of the Underdark.

Name: Sporekin Husk
Actions: Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Bludgeoning damage plus 2 (1d4) Poison damage.
Description: Sporekin Husks are reanimated corpses that have been transformed into mindless servants by the spores of sporekin, known for their relentless aggression, their ability to spread spores that can pacify or communicate with other creatures, and their role as both minions and hazards in the subterranean ecosystems of the Underdark.

Name: Sporekin Sprout
Traits: Sun Frailty. While in sunlight, the sporekin has Disadvantage on D20 Tests and dies if it spends more than 1 hour in sunlight.
Actions:
Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +1, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 (1d4 - 1) Bludgeoning damage plus 2 (1d4) Poison damage.

Mindlink Spores. The sporekin releases spores in a 30-foot Emanation. Creatures with Intelligence 2+ that aren't Constructs, Elementals, or Undead gain telepathy (30 ft.) for 1 hour.
Description: Sporekin Sprouts are young and vulnerable plant creatures that are in the early stages of growth, known for their ability to release spores that can communicate with other creatures, their vulnerability to sunlight, and their role as both potential threats and symbiotic organisms in the subterranean ecosystems of the Underdark.

Name: Nalfeshni
Traits:
Demonic Restoration. If the Nalfeshni dies outside the Abyss, its form collapses into foul ichor, and it instantly reforms elsewhere in the Abyss with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The Nalfeshni has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The Nalfeshni makes three Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 10 ft. Hit: 16 (2d10 + 5) Slashing damage plus 11 (2d10) Force damage.

Teleport. The Nalfeshni teleports up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.
Bonus Actions: Horror Nimbus (Recharge 5-6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 15, each creature in a 15‑foot Emanation centered on the Nalfeshni. Failure: 28 (8d6) Psychic damage, and the creature becomes Frightened for 1 minute, until it takes damage, or until it ends its turn without the Nalfeshni in line of sight. Success: The creature becomes immune to this Nalfeshni’s Horror Nimbus for 24 hours.
Reactions: Pursuit. Trigger: A creature the Nalfeshni can see ends its movement within 120 feet of it. Response: The Nalfeshni uses Teleport, but must appear within 10 feet of the triggering creature.
Description: Nalfeshnis are grotesque demon overlords who combine brute strength with cruel cunning. Their claws tear both flesh and spirit, and their aura of terror can break the resolve of entire warbands. Often serving as judges, executioners, or battlefield commanders in the Abyss, they revel in chaos and suffering, using their teleportation and psychic might to dominate any conflict they enter.

Name: Needle Blight
Actions:
Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) Slashing damage.

Needles. Ranged Attack Roll: +3, range 30/60 ft. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) Piercing damage.
Description: Needle Blights are malevolent plant creatures that infest forests and other wooded areas, known for their ability to attack with both claws and ranged needles, their resilience, and their role as both predators and hazards in the natural environments they inhabit.

Name: Night Hag
Traits:
Coven Magic. While within 30 feet of at least two hag allies, the hag can cast one of the following spells, requiring no Material components, using the spell's normal casting time, and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14): Augury, Find Familiar, Identify, Locate Object, Scrying, or Unseen Servant. The hag must finish a Long Rest before using this trait to cast that spell again.

Magic Resistance. The hag has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Soul Bag. The hag has a soul bag. While holding or carrying the bag, the hag can use its Nightmare Haunting action.

The bag has AC 15, HP 20, and Resistance to all damage. The bag turns to dust if reduced to 0 Hit Points. If the bag is destroyed, any souls the bag is holding are released. The hag can create a new bag after 7 days.
Actions:
Multiattack. The hag makes two Claw attacks.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Slashing damage.

Nightmare Haunting (1/Day; Requires Soul Bag). While on the Ethereal Plane, the hag casts Dream, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting. Only the hag can serve as the spell's messenger, and the target must be a creature the hag can see on the Material Plane. The spell fails and is wasted if the target is under the effect of the Protection from Evil and Good spell or within a Magic Circle spell.

If the target takes damage from the Dream spell, the target's Hit Point maximum decreases by an amount equal to that damage. If the spell kills the target, its soul is trapped in the hag's soul bag, and the target can't be raised from the dead until its soul is released.

Spellcasting. The hag casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14): At will: Detect Magic, Etherealness, Magic Missile (level 4 version); 2/day each: Phantasmal Killer, Plane Shift (self only)
Bonus Actions: Shape-Shift. The hag shape-shifts into a Small or Medium Humanoid, or it returns to its true form. Other than its size, its game statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.
Description: Night Hags are malevolent and cunning fiends that dwell in the lower planes, known for their ability to manipulate dreams, their coven magic, and their role as both tormentors and schemers in the nightmarish realms they inhabit.

Name: Nightmare
Languages: Understands Abyssal, Common, and Infernal but can't speak
Traits:
Confer Fire Resistance. The nightmare can grant Resistance to Fire damage to a rider while it is on the nightmare.

Illumination. The nightmare sheds Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet.
Actions:
Hooves. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Bludgeoning damage plus 10 (3d6) Fire damage.

Ethereal Stride. The nightmare and up to three willing creatures within 5 feet of it teleport to the Ethereal Plane from the Material Plane or vice versa.
Description: Nightmares are fiery and malevolent steeds that serve as mounts for fiends and other dark entities, known for their ability to traverse the Ethereal Plane, their fiery hooves, and their role as both mounts and harbingers of doom in the nightmarish realms they inhabit.

Name: Noble
Actions: Rapier. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d8 + 1) Piercing damage.
Reactions: Parry. Trigger: The noble is hit by a melee attack roll while holding a weapon. Response: The noble adds 2 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.
Description: Nobles are individuals of high social status and influence, often hailing from wealthy or powerful families, known for their refined manners, their ability to navigate social situations, and their role as both allies and adversaries in various settings, particularly in urban environments.

Name: Noble Prodigy
Actions:
Multiattack. The noble makes three Beguiling Strike attacks.

Beguiling Strike. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +8, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. Hit: 18 (4d6 + 4) Psychic damage, and the target has the Charmed condition until the start of the noble's next turn.

Spellcasting. The noble casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 16): At will: Mage Armor (included in AC), Mage Hand, Minor Illusion; 1/day each: Befuddlement, Detect Thoughts, Fly, Scrying, Shatter (level 7 version)
Reactions: Shield (2/Day). The noble casts Shield in response to that spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Noble Prodigies are exceptional individuals born into noble families who possess extraordinary talents and abilities, known for their charm, intelligence, and magical prowess, and their role as both influential figures and formidable opponents in various settings, particularly in urban environments.

Name: Gloomseer
Actions:
Multiattack. The gloomseer makes two Claw attacks.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) Slashing damage.

Withering Gaze. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 13, one creature the gloomseer can see within 120 feet. Failure: 17 (5d6) Necrotic damage. Success: Half damage.
Bonus Actions: Insightful Probe (Recharge 6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 14, one creature the gloomseer can see within 120 feet. Failure: The gloomseer magically extracts a hidden fact, memory, or secret from the target.
Description: Gloomseers are mysterious and malevolent aberrations that dwell in the dark and twisted environments of the Underdark, known for their ability to extract secrets from the minds of others, their withering gaze that can sap the life from their victims, and their role as both predators and manipulators in the subterranean ecosystems of the Underdark.

Name: Nycarat Fiend
Traits:
Fiendish Restoration. If the Nycarat fiend dies outside Gehenna, its form collapses into corrosive ichor, and it instantly reforms elsewhere in Gehenna with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The Nycarat fiend has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The Nycarat fiend makes two Mercurial Axe attacks.

Mercurial Axe. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +9, reach 10 ft. or range 30/90 ft. Hit: 18 (2d12 + 5) Slashing damage plus 10 (3d6) Force damage. Hit or Miss: After a ranged attack, the axe instantly reappears in the fiend’s hand.
Bonus Actions: Shadowy Teleport. The Nycarat fiend becomes Invisible for 1 minute and teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. The invisibility ends early immediately after it deals damage.
Description: Nycarat fiends are elite yugoloth warriors forged in the volcanic wastes of Gehenna. Their enchanted axes carve through armor and magical defenses alike, and their ability to vanish into shadow before striking makes them deadly ambushers. Bound by infernal contracts, they serve as assassins, commanders, and enforcers in planar conflicts, feared for their precision and ruthless efficiency.

Name: Ochre Jelly
Condition Immunities: charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, prone, restrained
Traits:
Amorphous. The jelly can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.

Spider Climb. The jelly can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.
Actions: Pseudopod. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (3d6 + 2) Acid damage.
Reactions: Split. Trigger: While the jelly is Large or Medium and has 10+ Hit Points, it becomes Bloodied or is subjected to Lightning or Slashing damage. Response: The jelly splits into two new Ochre Jellies. Each new jelly is one size smaller than the original jelly and acts on its Initiative. The original jelly's Hit Points are divided evenly between the new jellies (round down).
Description: Ochre Jellies are large and amorphous oozes that dwell in dark and damp environments, known for their acidic touch, their ability to split into smaller jellies when damaged, and their role as both predators and hazards in the subterranean ecosystems they inhabit.

Name: Octopus
Traits:
Compression. The octopus can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.

Water Breathing. The octopus can breathe only underwater.
Actions: Tentacles. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Bludgeoning damage.
Reactions: Ink Cloud (1/Day). Trigger: A creature ends its turn within 5 feet of the octopus while underwater. Response: The octopus releases ink that fills a 5-foot Cube centered on itself, and the octopus moves up to its Swim Speed. The Cube is Heavily Obscured for 1 minute or until a strong current or similar effect disperses the ink.
Description: Octopuses are small and elusive sea creatures that inhabit coastal waters and underwater environments, known for their ability to squeeze through tight spaces, their ink cloud defense mechanism, and their role as both predators and prey in the aquatic ecosystems they inhabit.

Name: Ogre
Actions:
Greatclub. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Bludgeoning damage.

Javelin. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Piercing damage.
Environment: Arctic, Desert, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Mountain, Swamp, Underdark
Description: Ogres are large and brutish giants that inhabit a variety of environments, known for their immense strength, their aggressive nature, and their role as both predators and marauders in the wilds they call home.

Name: Ogre Zombie
Languages: Understands Common and Giant but can't speak
Traits: Undead Fortitude. If damage reduces the zombie to 0 Hit Points, it makes a Constitution saving throw (DC 5 plus the damage taken) unless the damage is Radiant or from a Critical Hit. On a successful save, the zombie drops to 1 Hit Point instead.
Actions: Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Ogre Zombies are reanimated corpses of ogres that have been transformed into undead creatures, known for their relentless nature, their ability to endure damage through Undead Fortitude, and their role as both minions and threats in various settings, particularly in areas associated with death and decay.

Name: Ogrillon Ogre
Actions:
Battleaxe. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Slashing damage.

Javelin. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Piercing damage.
Environment: Arctic, Desert, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Mountain, Swamp, Underdark
Description: Ogrillon Ogres are a distinct breed of ogres that are smaller and more agile than their larger counterparts, known for their cunning and ferocity in combat, and their role as both predators and marauders in the wilds they inhabit.

Name: Oni
Traits: Regeneration. The oni regains 10 Hit Points at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 Hit Point.
Actions:
Multiattack. The oni makes two Claw or Nightmare Ray attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 10 (1d12 + 4) Slashing damage plus 9 (2d8) Necrotic damage.

Nightmare Ray. Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 60 ft. Hit: 9 (2d6 + 2) Psychic damage, and the target has the Frightened condition until the start of the oni's next turn.

Shape-Shift. The oni shape-shifts into a Small or Medium Humanoid or a Large Giant, or it returns to its true form. Other than its size, its game statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

Spellcasting. The oni casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13):; 1/day each: Charm Person (level 2 version), Darkness, Gaseous Form, Sleep
Bonus Actions: Invisibility. The oni casts Invisibility on itself, requiring no spell components and using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Onis are powerful and malevolent fiends that serve as enforcers and agents of evil in various settings, known for their shape-shifting abilities, their regenerative powers, and their role as both predators and manipulators in the dark corners of the world they inhabit.

Name: Otherworldly Steed
HP: 5 + 10 per spell level (the steed has a number of Hit Dice [d10s] equal to the spell's level)
CR: None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)
Traits: Life Bond. When you regain Hit Points from a level 1+ spell, the steed regains the same number of Hit Points if you're within 5 feet of it.
Actions: Otherworldly Slam. Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d8 + the spell's level of Radiant (Celestial), Psychic (Fey), or Necrotic (Fiend) damage.
Bonus Actions:
Fell Glare (Fiend Only; Recharges after a Long Rest). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC equals your spell save DC, one creature within 60 feet the steed can see. Failure: The target has the Frightened condition until the end of your next turn.

Fey Step (Fey Only; Recharges after a Long Rest). The steed teleports, along with its rider, to an unoccupied space of your choice up to 60 feet away from itself.

Healing Touch (Celestial Only; Recharges after a Long Rest). One creature within 5 feet of the steed regains a number of Hit Points equal to 2d8 + the spell's level.
Description: Otherworldly Steeds are magical mounts that can be summoned through the Otherworldly Patron feature of the Warlock class, known for their celestial, fey, or fiendish nature, their ability to bond with their summoner, and their role as both companions and steeds in various adventures.

Name: Filthmaw Abomination
Traits:
Filth Dweller. The filthmaw abomination thrives in refuse, rot, and toxic environments. It is immune to diseases and can digest nearly any organic matter.

Toxic Symbiosis. The filthmaw’s body hosts colonies of corrosive bacteria that empower its bite and tentacles.
Actions:
Multiattack. The filthmaw abomination makes one Bite attack and two Tentacle attacks.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) Piercing damage, and the target becomes Poisoned. Whenever the Poisoned target finishes a Long Rest, it must make a DC 15 Constitution save. Failure: Its Hit Point maximum is reduced by 5 (1d10) until the Poisoned condition ends. Success: The Poisoned condition ends.

Tentacle. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, it is Grappled (escape DC 13).

Tentacle Slam. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 14, each creature Grappled by the filthmaw abomination. Failure: 16 (3d8 + 3) Bludgeoning damage and the target is Stunned until the start of the filthmaw’s next turn. Success: Half damage.
Description: Filthmaw abominations are grotesque scavengers that thrive in rot-choked tunnels and refuse pits. Their bodies teem with corrosive microbes that seep into their bites and tentacles, spreading debilitating toxins. Though slow-witted, they communicate through unsettling telepathic impressions, luring prey toward the stench-ridden lairs where they reign as apex scavengers.

Name: Owl
Traits: Flyby. The owl doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.
Actions: Talons. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Slashing damage.
Description: Owls are small and nocturnal birds of prey that inhabit a variety of environments, known for their keen senses, silent flight, and their role as both hunters and symbols of wisdom in various cultures.

Name: Grizzly Owlbeast
Actions:
Multiattack. The grizzly owlbeast makes two Rending Claw attacks.

Rending Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) Slashing damage.
Description: Grizzly Owlbeasts are large and fearsome monstrosities that inhabit dense forests, known for their powerful rending claws, their ability to climb and ambush prey, and their role as both predators and territorial guardians in the wilds they call home.

Name: Panther
Actions: Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Slashing damage.
Bonus Actions: Nimble Escape. The panther takes the Disengage or Hide action.
Description: Panthers are sleek and agile predators that inhabit a variety of environments, known for their stealthy nature, their powerful rend attack, and their role as both hunters and elusive creatures in the wilds they call home.

Name: Pegasus
Languages: Understands Celestial, Common, Elvish, and Sylvan but can't speak
Actions: Hooves. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage plus 5 (2d4) Radiant damage.
Description: Pegasi are majestic and noble celestial creatures that inhabit the skies and upper planes, known for their graceful flight, their radiant hooves, and their role as both mounts and symbols of freedom and heroism in various myths and legends.

Name: Performer
Actions: Shortsword. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Piercing damage.
Reactions: Uncanny Dodge. Trigger: The performer is hit by an attack roll. Response: The performer halves the damage (round down) it takes from that attack.
Description: Performers are skilled and charismatic individuals who entertain audiences with their talents, known for their agility, their charm, and their role as both entertainers and influential figures in various social settings.

Name: Performer Legend
Actions:
Multiattack. The performer makes three Bejeweled Baton attacks.

Bejeweled Baton. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d4 + 5) Bludgeoning damage plus 10 (3d6) Psychic damage.

Majestic Song. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 17, each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within 120 feet. Failure: 22 (4d8 + 4) Psychic damage, and the target has the Charmed or Frightened condition (performer's choice) until the end of the performer's next turn. Success: Half damage only.

Spellcasting. The performer casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17): At will: Mage Hand, Minor Illusion, Prestidigitation; 1/day each: Major Image, Project Image
Reactions: Warding Charm. Trigger: A creature hits the performer with an attack roll. Response—Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 17, the triggering creature. Failure: The attack roll misses the performer, and the target has the Charmed condition until the end of the performer's next turn.
Description: Performer Legends are renowned and legendary entertainers who have achieved great fame and influence, known for their exceptional talents, their charismatic presence, and their role as both cultural icons and powerful figures in various social settings.

Name: Performer Maestro
Actions:
Multiattack. The performer makes three Rapier attacks.

Rapier. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Psychic damage.

Beguiling Song. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 15, each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within 120 feet. Failure: 20 (3d10 + 4) Psychic damage, and the target has the Charmed condition until the end of the performer's next turn. Success: Half damage only.

Spellcasting. The performer casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15): At will: Minor Illusion, Prestidigitation; 1/day: Tasha's Hideous Laughter (level 3 version)
Description: Performer Maestros are highly skilled and influential entertainers who have achieved significant recognition, known for their exceptional talents, their charismatic presence, and their role as both cultural icons and powerful figures in various social settings.

Name: Periton
Languages: Understands Common and Elvish but can't speak
Traits: Flyby. The Periton doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks when it flies out of a creature’s reach.
Actions:
Multiattack. The Periton makes one Gore attack and one Talons attack.

Gore. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Piercing damage. If the Periton moved 30 or more feet straight toward the target immediately before the hit, the attack deals an extra 9 (2d8) Piercing damage.

Talons. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) Piercing damage. If this attack reduces a Humanoid to 0 Hit Points, the Periton kills the creature by tearing out its heart.
Description: Peritons are savage flying hunters that stalk high cliffs and rugged mountain passes. Their twisted bodies combine the grace of a great bird with the brutality of a horned beast, allowing them to dive with lethal force. Drawn to the essence of humanoids, they strike with uncanny precision, ripping out hearts as part of a dark instinct tied to their unnatural origins. Their shadowy forms circling overhead are often the only warning before a deadly plunge from above.

Name: Phase Spider
Traits:
Ethereal Sight. The spider can see 60 feet into the Ethereal Plane while on the Material Plane and vice versa.

Spider Climb. The spider can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

Web Walker. The spider ignores movement restrictions caused by webs, and the spider knows the location of any other creature in contact with the same web.
Actions:
Multiattack. The spider makes two Bite attacks.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) Piercing damage plus 9 (2d8) Poison damage. If this damage reduces the target to 0 Hit Points, the target becomes Stable, and it has the Poisoned condition for 1 hour. While Poisoned, the target also has the Paralyzed condition.
Bonus Actions: Ethereal Jaunt. The spider teleports from the Material Plane to the Ethereal Plane or vice versa.
Environment: Desert, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Planar (Ethereal Plane), Underdark, Urban
Description: Phase Spiders are elusive and dangerous monstrosities that inhabit a variety of environments, known for their ability to shift between the Material and Ethereal Planes, their venomous bite, and their role as both predators and threats in the wilds they call home.

Name: Stalactite Lurker
Senses: Blindsight 30 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 8
Traits: Spider Climb. The stalactite lurker can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.
Actions:
Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d8 + 1) Piercing damage.

Drop. The stalactite lurker falls. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 11, one creature directly underneath the stalactite lurker. Failure: 10 (3d6) Piercing damage. Failure or Success: The stalactite lurker reduces any damage it takes from the fall by 20.
Description: Stalactite lurkers are camouflaged aberrations that cling to cavern ceilings, mimicking stone formations until prey passes beneath them. When they drop, their hardened bodies strike with deadly force. Though mindless, they are perfectly adapted to the silent, predatory life of the deep earth.

Name: Piranha
Traits: Water Breathing. The piranha can breathe only underwater.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5 (with Advantage if the target doesn't have all its Hit Points), reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Piercing damage.
Description: Piranhas are small and aggressive fish that inhabit freshwater environments, known for their sharp teeth, their ability to swarm and attack prey, and their role as both predators and threats in the waters they call home.

Name: Pirate
Actions:
Multiattack. The pirate makes two Dagger attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Enthralling Panache.

Dagger. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Piercing damage.

Enthralling Panache. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 12, one creature the pirate can see within 30 feet. Failure: The target has the Charmed condition until the start of the pirate's next turn.
Description: Pirates are daring and charismatic individuals who sail the seas in search of adventure and treasure, known for their agility, their charm, and their role as both swashbuckling adventurers and formidable foes in various coastal and maritime settings.

Name: Pirate Admiral
Actions:
Multiattack. The pirate makes three attacks, using Scimitar or Pistol in any combination.

Scimitar. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 5 ft. Hit: 16 (3d6 + 6) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Poison damage, and the target suffers one of the following effects of the pirate's choice:

Awestruck. The target has the Charmed condition until the start of the pirate's next turn.

Poison. The target has the Poisoned condition until the start of the pirate's next turn.

Pistol. Ranged Attack Roll: +10, range 30/90 ft. Hit: 28 (4d10 + 6) Piercing damage.
Bonus Actions: Rally (1/Day). The pirate chooses up to three other creatures it can see within 30 feet. Until the start of the pirate's next turn, the targets have Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws.
Reactions: Defensive Stance. Trigger: The pirate is hit by a melee attack roll while holding a weapon. Response: The pirate adds 4 to its AC against melee attack rolls (including the triggering attack) until the start of its next turn, possibly causing the attacks to miss.
Description: Pirate Admirals are seasoned and formidable leaders who command fleets of pirates, known for their exceptional combat skills, their strategic acumen, and their role as both influential figures and powerful adversaries in various coastal and maritime settings.

Name: Pirate Captain
Actions:
Multiattack. The pirate makes three attacks, using Rapier or Pistol in any combination.

Rapier. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Piercing damage, and the pirate has Advantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of this turn.

Pistol. Ranged Attack Roll: +7, range 30/90 ft. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) Piercing damage.
Bonus Actions: Captain's Charm. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 14, one creature the pirate can see within 30 feet. Failure: The target has the Charmed condition until the start of the pirate's next turn.
Reactions: Riposte. Trigger: The pirate is hit by a melee attack roll while holding a weapon. Response: The pirate adds 3 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss. On a miss, the pirate makes one Rapier attack against the triggering creature if within range.
Description: Pirate Captains are experienced and charismatic leaders who command crews of pirates, known for their combat prowess, their charm, and their role as both influential figures and formidable adversaries in various coastal and maritime settings.

Name: Pit Fiend
Traits:
Diabolical Restoration. If the pit fiend dies outside the Nine Hells, its form erupts into sulfurous smoke and it immediately reforms elsewhere in the Nine Hells with full Hit Points.

Fear Aura. While the pit fiend isn't Incapacitated, it radiates a 20‑foot aura of dread. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 21 at the start of an enemy’s turn in the aura. Failure: The creature becomes Frightened until the start of its next turn. Success: The creature becomes immune to this pit fiend’s aura for 24 hours.

Legendary Resistance (4/Day). If the pit fiend fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Magic Resistance. The pit fiend has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The pit fiend makes one Bite attack, two Devilish Claw attacks, and one Fiery Mace attack.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +14, reach 10 ft. Hit: 18 (3d6 + 8) Piercing damage. The target, if a creature, must succeed on a DC 21 Constitution saving throw or become Poisoned. While Poisoned in this way, the creature cannot regain Hit Points and takes 21 (6d6) Poison damage at the start of each of its turns. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. After 1 minute, the creature automatically succeeds.

Devilish Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +14, reach 10 ft. Hit: 26 (4d8 + 8) Necrotic damage.

Fiery Mace. Melee Attack Roll: +14, reach 10 ft. Hit: 22 (4d6 + 8) Force damage plus 21 (6d6) Fire damage.

Hellfire Spellcasting (Recharge 4–6). The pit fiend casts Fireball (5th‑level) twice, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 21). It may replace one Fireball with Hold Monster (7th‑level) or Wall of Fire.
Description: Pit fiends are the iron‑fisted generals of the Nine Hells, towering devils whose strength, cunning, and infernal magic make them among the most feared beings in existence. Their presence alone inspires terror, and their mastery of hellfire and venomous strikes allows them to dominate battlefields. As commanders, enforcers, and executioners of infernal law, pit fiends embody the ruthless hierarchy and burning ambition of the Hells.

Name: Pixie
Traits: Magic Resistance. The pixie has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Faerie Dust. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. Hit: 1 Radiant damage, and the target has the Charmed or Poisoned condition (pixie's choice) until the start of the pixie's next turn.

Spellcasting. The pixie casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12): At will: Dancing Lights, Druidcraft, Invisibility (self only); 1/day each: Detect Thoughts, Fly, Sleep
Description: Pixies are tiny and mischievous fey creatures that inhabit the forests and the Feywild, known for their playful nature, their magical abilities, and their role as both tricksters and protectors of the natural world in various myths and legends.

Name: Pixie Wonderbringer
Traits: Magic Resistance. The pixie has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The pixie makes two Faerie Dust attacks.

Faerie Dust. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) Radiant damage, and the target has the Charmed or Poisoned condition (pixie's choice) until the start of the pixie's next turn.

Spellcasting. The pixie casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15): At will: Dancing Lights, Druidcraft, Invisibility (self only); 1/day each: Detect Thoughts, Fly, Major Image
Bonus Actions: Burst of Wonder (Recharge 5-6). The pixie casts Entangle, Polymorph, or Tasha's Hideous Laughter, requiring no Material components and using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Pixie Wonderbringers are extraordinary and enchanting fey creatures that inhabit the forests and the Feywild, known for their radiant beauty, their powerful magical abilities, and their role as both guardians and bringers of wonder in various myths and legends.

Name: Planetar
Traits:
Divine Awareness. The planetar instantly recognizes spoken falsehoods.

Exalted Restoration. If the planetar dies outside Mount Celestia, its body vanishes in a flash of light, and it immediately reforms elsewhere on Mount Celestia with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The planetar has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The planetar makes three Radiant Sword attacks or uses Holy Burst twice.

Radiant Sword. Melee Attack Roll: +12, reach 10 ft. Hit: 14 (2d6 + 7) Slashing damage plus 18 (4d8) Radiant damage.

Holy Burst. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 20, each enemy in a 20‑foot‑radius Sphere centered on a point the planetar can see within 120 feet. Failure: 24 (7d6) Radiant damage. Success: Half damage.

Spellcasting. The planetar casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 20): At will: Detect Evil and Good; 1/day each: Commune, Control Weather, Dispel Evil and Good, Raise Dead
Bonus Actions: Divine Aid (2/Day). The planetar casts Cure Wounds, Invisibility, Lesser Restoration, or Remove Curse, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Planetars are mighty celestial champions who serve as the direct agents of higher divine powers. Radiant and awe‑inspiring, they wield immense strength and holy magic to defend the innocent, purge corruption, and enforce cosmic justice. Their presence on the battlefield is a beacon of hope to allies and a devastating force against evil.

Name: Plesiosaurus
Traits: Hold Breath. The plesiosaurus can hold its breath for 1 hour.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Piercing damage.
Description: Plesiosauruses are large and aquatic dinosaurs that inhabit the waters of the world, known for their long necks, their powerful swimming abilities, and their role as both predators and threats in the aquatic environments they call home.

Name: Polar Bear
Actions:
Multiattack. The bear makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (1d8 + 5) Slashing damage.
Description: Polar Bears are large and powerful predators that inhabit the icy regions of the world, known for their thick fur, their strength, and their role as both hunters and threats in the frigid environments they call home.

Name: Poltergeist
Damage Resistances: acid, bludgeoning, cold, fire, lightning, piercing, slashing, thunder
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, unconscious
Traits: Incorporeal Movement. The poltergeist can move through other creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain. It takes 5 (1d10) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.
Actions:
Multiattack. The poltergeist makes one Object Slam attack and uses Telekinetic Thrust.

Object Slam. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. or range 30 ft. Hit: 7 (2d4 + 2) Bludgeoning damage.

Telekinetic Thrust. Strength Saving Throw: DC 12, one creature the poltergeist can see within 30 feet. Failure: 9 (2d6 + 2) Force damage, and the target is pushed up to 30 feet straight away from the poltergeist.
Bonus Actions: Vanish. The poltergeist gives itself the Invisible condition or ends that condition on itself.
Description: Poltergeists are restless and mischievous spirits that haunt various locations, known for their ability to manipulate objects, their incorporeal nature, and their role as both nuisances and threats in the haunted environments they inhabit.

Name: Pony
Actions: Hooves. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Ponies are small and sturdy equines that inhabit various environments, known for their gentle nature, their endurance, and their role as both companions and beasts of burden in various cultures and settings.

Name: Priest
Actions:
Multiattack. The priest makes two attacks, using Mace or Radiant Flame in any combination.

Mace. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Bludgeoning damage plus 5 (2d4) Radiant damage.

Radiant Flame. Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 60 ft. Hit: 11 (2d10) Radiant damage.

Spellcasting. The priest casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability: At will: Light, Thaumaturgy; 1/day: Spirit Guardians
Bonus Actions: Divine Aid (3/Day). The priest casts Bless, Dispel Magic, Healing Word, or Lesser Restoration, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Priests are devoted and knowledgeable individuals who serve as spiritual leaders and healers, known for their divine magic, their wisdom, and their role as both guides and protectors in various religious and cultural contexts.

Name: Priest Acolyte
Actions:
Mace. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Bludgeoning damage plus 2 (1d4) Radiant damage.

Radiant Flame. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 60 ft. Hit: 7 (2d6) Radiant damage.

Spellcasting. The priest casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability: At will: Light, Thaumaturgy
Bonus Actions: Divine Aid (1/Day). The priest casts Bless, Healing Word, or Sanctuary, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Priest Acolytes are novice and dedicated followers who serve under the guidance of priests, known for their budding divine magic, their eagerness to learn, and their role as both assistants and aspiring spiritual leaders in various religious and cultural contexts.

Name: Ancient Owlbeast
Traits: Magic Resistance. The ancient owlbeast has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The ancient owlbeast makes two Ravaging Claw attacks.

Ravaging Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) Slashing damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature and the owlbeast moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 9 (2d8) Slashing damage and is knocked Prone.

Thunderous Cry (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 15, each creature in a 30-foot Emanation originating from the owlbeast. Failure: 27 (6d8) Thunder damage, and the target is Incapacitated until the end of the owlbeast's next turn. Success: Half damage only.
Description: Ancient Owlbeasts are massive and fearsome creatures that inhabit dense forests, known for their powerful claws, their keen senses, and their role as both predators and threats in the wilderness they call home.

Name: Pseudodragon
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 15
Languages: Understands Common and Draconic but can't speak
Traits: Magic Resistance. The pseudodragon has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The pseudodragon makes two Bite attacks.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Piercing damage.

Sting. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12, one creature the pseudodragon can see within 5 feet. Failure: 5 (2d4) Poison damage, and the target has the Poisoned condition for 1 hour. While Poisoned, the target also has the Unconscious condition, which ends early if the target takes damage or a creature within 5 feet of it takes an action to wake it.
Environment: Coastal, Desert, Forest, Hill, Mountain, Urban
Description: Pseudodragons are small and magical dragon-like creatures that inhabit various environments, known for their playful nature, their keen senses, and their role as both companions and elusive creatures in the world.

Name: Psychic Gray Ooze
Condition Immunities: blinded, charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, prone, restrained
Traits: Amorphous. The ooze can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.
Actions:
Pseudopod. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (3d6 + 1) Acid damage, and the target has Disadvantage on Intelligence saving throws until the end of the ooze's next turn.

Psychic Crush. Intelligence Saving Throw: DC 10, one creature the ooze can see within 60 feet. Failure: 13 (3d8) Psychic damage.
Reactions: Mind Corrosion. Trigger: The ooze fails a saving throw against a spell or another magical effect created by a creature. Response: The triggering creature takes 3 (1d6) Psychic damage.
Description: Psychic Gray Oozes are strange and dangerous creatures that inhabit the Underdark, known for their acidic touch, their psychic abilities, and their role as both hazards and threats in the subterranean environments they call home.

Name: Pteranodon
Traits: Flyby. The pteranodon doesn't provoke an Opportunity Attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Piercing damage.
Description: Pteranodons are flying reptiles that inhabit various environments, known for their large wingspans, their ability to soar through the skies, and their role as both predators and threats in the prehistoric and fantastical worlds they inhabit.

Name: Purple Worm
Senses: Blindsight 30 ft., Tremorsense 60 ft., passive Perception 9
Traits: Tunneler. The worm can burrow through solid rock at half its Burrow Speed and leaves a 10-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.
Actions:
Multiattack. The worm makes one Bite attack and one Tail Stinger attack.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +14, reach 10 ft. Hit: 22 (3d8 + 9) Piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 19), and it has the Restrained condition until the grapple ends.

Tail Stinger. Melee Attack Roll: +14, reach 10 ft. Hit: 16 (2d6 + 9) Piercing damage plus 35 (10d6) Poison damage.
Bonus Actions:
Swallow. Strength Saving Throw: DC 19, one Large or smaller creature Grappled by the worm (it can have up to three creatures swallowed at a time). Failure: The target is swallowed by the worm, and the Grappled condition ends. A swallowed creature has the Blinded and Restrained conditions, has Total Cover against attacks and other effects outside the worm, and takes 17 (5d6) Acid damage at the start of each of the worm's turns.

If the worm takes 30 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, the worm must succeed on a DC 21 Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, each of which falls in a space within 5 feet of the worm and has the Prone condition. If the worm dies, any swallowed creature no longer has the Restrained condition and can escape from the corpse using 20 feet of movement, exiting Prone.
Description: Purple Worms are massive and terrifying subterranean creatures that inhabit the Underdark and desert regions, known for their immense size, their powerful jaws, and their role as both predators and hazards in the underground environments they call home.

Name: Feraloth Ravager
Traits: Blood Frenzy. While Bloodied, the feraloth has Advantage on attack rolls.
Actions:
Multiattack. The feraloth makes two Claw attacks.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Slashing damage, or 13 (3d6 + 3) Slashing damage if the feraloth is Bloodied.
Description: Feraloth Ravagers are savage and aggressive creatures that inhabit the Underdark, known for their ferocity, their climbing abilities, and their role as both predators and threats in the subterranean environments they call home.

Name: Feraloth Mindshaper
Traits: Blood Frenzy. While Bloodied, the feraloth has Advantage on attack rolls.
Actions:
Multiattack. The feraloth makes two Claw attacks.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Slashing damage plus 5 (2d4) Psychic damage.

Spellcasting. The feraloth casts one of the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12): At will: Mage Hand (the hand is Invisible), Minor Illusion; 2/day: Mind Spike
Reactions: Psionic Reflex (3/Day). The feraloth casts Feather Fall or Shield in response to the spell's trigger, requiring no spell components and using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Feraloth Mindshapers are cunning and dangerous creatures that inhabit the Underdark, known for their psychic abilities, their climbing skills, and their role as both predators and threats in the subterranean environments they call home.

Name: Quasit
Traits:
Magic Resistance. The quasit has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Demonic Skulker. Abyssal energies ripple across the quasit's form, making its movements erratic and difficult to track when it hides or stalks prey.
Actions:
Invisibility. The quasit becomes Invisible, requiring no components and using Charisma as its spellcasting ability.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Slashing damage, and the target becomes Poisoned until the start of the quasit's next turn.

Scare (1/Day). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 10, one creature within 20 feet. Failure: The creature becomes Frightened. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. After 1 minute, it automatically succeeds.

Shape-Shift. The quasit transforms into a bat (Speed 10 ft., Fly 40 ft.), a centipede (Speed 40 ft., Climb 40 ft.), or a toad (Speed 40 ft., Swim 40 ft.), or returns to its true form. Its statistics remain unchanged except for Speed. Equipment does not transform.
Description: Quasits are spiteful, shape-shifting demons that thrive in the shadows of the Abyss. Small but cunning, they slip between forms to stalk victims, sow fear, and strike when least expected. Their toxic claws and natural invisibility make them dangerous ambushers, while their erratic, flickering bodies make them difficult to pin down.

Name: Questing Knight
Traits: Aura of Bravery. Creatures of the knight's choice in a 30-foot Emanation originating from it have Immunity to the Charmed and Frightened conditions while there.
Actions:
Multiattack. The knight makes three attacks, using Greatsword or Longbow in any combination.

Greatsword. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Slashing damage plus 22 (5d8) Radiant damage.

Longbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +7, range 150/600 ft. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) Piercing damage plus 22 (5d8) Radiant damage.

Spellcasting. The knight casts one of the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 16):; 1/day each: Daylight, Dispel Evil and Good, Greater Restoration, Phantom Steed
Description: Questing Knights are noble and valiant warriors who embark on epic quests, known for their martial prowess, their bravery, and their role as both champions and protectors in various heroic and fantastical settings.

Name: Rakshasa
Damage Vulnerabilities: piercing damage from weapons wielded by creatures under the effect of a Bless spell
Traits:
Fiendish Restoration. If the rakshasa dies outside the Nine Hells, its form collapses into foul ichor, and it immediately reforms elsewhere in the Nine Hells with full Hit Points.

Greater Magic Resistance. The rakshasa automatically succeeds on saving throws against spells and magical effects, and spell attack rolls always miss it. Without the rakshasa’s consent, no spell can remotely observe it or reveal its thoughts, creature type, or alignment.
Actions:
Multiattack. The rakshasa makes three Cursed Touch attacks.

Cursed Touch. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Slashing damage plus 19 (3d12) Necrotic damage. A creature hit by this attack is cursed. While cursed, the creature gains no benefit from finishing a Short or Long Rest.

Baleful Command (Recharge 5–6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 18, each enemy in a 30‑foot Emanation centered on the rakshasa. Failure: 28 (8d6) Psychic damage, and the creature becomes Frightened and Incapacitated until the start of the rakshasa’s next turn.

Spellcasting. The rakshasa casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 18): At will: Detect Magic, Detect Thoughts, Disguise Self, Mage Hand, Minor Illusion; 1/day each: Fly, Invisibility, Major Image, Plane Shift
Description: Rakshasas are master manipulators and fiendish schemers who blend charm, illusion, and ruthless cunning. Their twisted magic shields them from nearly all spells, allowing them to move unseen through mortal societies as advisors, tyrants, or hidden puppet‑masters. Their cursed touch drains vitality and hope, making them feared opponents both in the Nine Hells and in the cities they infiltrate.

Name: Rat
Traits: Agile. The rat doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks when it moves out of an enemy's reach.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Piercing damage.
Description: Rats are small and adaptable rodents that inhabit various environments, known for their agility, their keen senses, and their role as both pests and survivors in the world they inhabit.

Name: Raven
Traits: Mimicry. The raven can mimic simple sounds it has heard, such as a whisper or chitter. A hearer can discern the sounds are imitations with a successful DC 10 Wisdom (Insight) check.
Actions: Beak. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Piercing damage.
Description: Ravens are small and intelligent birds that inhabit various environments, known for their mimicry abilities, their keen perception, and their role as both scavengers and symbols in the world they inhabit.

Name: Red Dragon Wyrmling
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 14
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) Slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) Fire damage.

Fire Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 13, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: 24 (7d6) Fire damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Red Dragon Wyrmlings are young and fiery dragons that inhabit hills and mountains, known for their aggressive nature, their fiery breath, and their role as both threats and future rulers in the draconic hierarchy they belong to.

Name: Crimson Mutant
Traits:
Magic Resistance. The crimson mutant has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Regeneration. The crimson mutant regains 10 Hit Points at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 Hit Point.
Actions:
Multiattack. The crimson mutant makes three Infecting Talon attacks.

Infecting Talon. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is a Humanoid not already cursed by a Crimson Mutant, it is subjected to the following effect. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 14. Failure: The target becomes unknowingly cursed as a tiny embryonic spawn is implanted within. Removing the curse destroys the embryo.

Over 2d4 * 10 days, the embryo gestates. In the final 24 hours, the cursed target becomes feverish; its Speed is halved, and it has Disadvantage on all D20 Tests. At the end of this time, the embryo transforms into a Spawnling Tadpole, which tears free from the host, killing it.
Description: Crimson Mutants are twisted and dangerous aberrations that inhabit the chaotic plane of Limbo, known for their regenerative abilities, their infectious attacks, and their role as both threats and sources of new horrors in the ever-changing and unpredictable environment they call home.

Name: Reef Shark
Traits:
Pack Tactics. The shark has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the shark's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

Water Breathing. The shark can breathe only underwater.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (2d4 + 2) Piercing damage.
Description: Reef Sharks are aggressive and territorial predators that inhabit coastal waters and reefs, known for their pack tactics, their sharp teeth, and their role as both threats and important parts of the marine ecosystem they call home.

Name: Inferno Tunneler
Senses: Darkvision 60 ft., Tremorsense 60 ft., passive Perception 10
Traits: Heat Aura. At the end of each of the inferno tunneler's turns, each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the inferno tunneler takes 16 (3d10) Fire damage.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +11, reach 10 ft. Hit: 18 (2d10 + 7) Piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) Fire damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 17), and it has the Restrained condition until the grapple ends.
Bonus Actions:
Swallow. Strength Saving Throw: DC 19, one Large or smaller creature Grappled by the inferno tunneler (it can have up to two creatures swallowed at a time). Failure: The target is swallowed by the inferno tunneler, and the Grappled condition ends. A swallowed creature has the Blinded and Restrained conditions, it has Total Cover against attacks and other effects outside the inferno tunneler, and it takes 10 (3d6) Acid damage plus 10 (3d6) Fire damage at the start of each of the inferno tunneler's turns.

If the inferno tunneler takes 30 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, the inferno tunneler must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, each of which falls in a space within 5 feet of the inferno tunneler and has the Prone condition. If the inferno tunneler dies, any swallowed creature no longer has the Restrained condition and can escape from the corpse by using 15 feet of movement, exiting Prone.
Description: Inferno tunnelers are colossal subterranean predators that carve molten paths through frozen landscapes. Their bodies radiate intense heat, melting ice and stone as they burrow. When they erupt from beneath the ground, they strike with overwhelming force, swallowing prey whole and incinerating anything that draws too near. These creatures embody the violent clash of fire and frost that defines the harshest arctic realms.

Name: Revenant
