Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, poisoned, stunned
Traits:
Regeneration. The revenant regains 10 Hit Points at the start of each of its turns. If the revenant takes Fire or Radiant damage, this trait doesn't function at the start of its next turn. Its body is destroyed only if it starts its turn with 0 Hit Points and doesn't regenerate.

Undead Restoration. If the revenant dies, it revives 24 hours later in a different body unless Dispel Evil and Good is cast on its corpse. If it revives, it animates a Humanoid corpse elsewhere on the same plane of existence; it now looks different but uses the same stat block and returns with all its Hit Points.
Actions:
Multiattack. The revenant uses Vengeful Glare and makes two Slam attacks.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Necrotic damage.

Vengeful Glare. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 15, one creature the revenant can see within 30 feet. Failure: The target has the Frightened condition and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically. If the Frightened target is cursed by the revenant (see Vow of Revenge), the target also has the Paralyzed condition for the duration.
Bonus Actions: Vow of Revenge (1/Day). The revenant curses one creature it can see within 30 feet of itself. The revenant knows the distance to and direction of the cursed target, even if it is on a different plane of existence. The curse ends on the target if the revenant uses this Bonus Action on a different creature.
Description: Revenants are vengeful and relentless undead that seek retribution for their deaths, known for their regenerative abilities, their curses, and their role as both avengers and threats in the world they haunt.

Name: Rhinoceros
Actions: Gore. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) Piercing damage. If target is a Large or smaller creature and the rhinoceros moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 9 (2d8) Piercing damage and has the Prone condition.
Description: Rhinoceroses are large and powerful beasts that inhabit grasslands and savannas, known for their strength, their charging attacks, and their role as both majestic creatures and potential threats in the wild environments they call home.

Name: Riding Horse
Actions: Hooves. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Bludgeoning damage.
Description: Riding Horses are domesticated and versatile animals that serve as mounts and beasts of burden, known for their speed, their strength, and their role as both companions and tools in various settings where they are found.

Name: Roc
Actions:
Multiattack. The roc makes two Beak attacks. It can replace one attack with a Talons attack.

Beak. Melee Attack Roll: +13, reach 10 ft. Hit: 28 (3d12 + 9) Piercing damage.

Talons. Melee Attack Roll: +13, reach 5 ft. Hit: 23 (4d6 + 9) Slashing damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 19) from both talons, and it has the Restrained condition until the grapple ends.
Bonus Actions: Swoop (Recharge 5-6). If the roc has a creature Grappled, the roc flies up to half its Fly Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks and drops that creature.
Description: Rocs are massive and powerful flying creatures that inhabit various environments, known for their immense size, their predatory nature, and their role as both awe-inspiring and dangerous beings in the world they soar through.

Name: Gloomlurker
Traits: Wall Creeper. The gloomlurker can climb on walls and ceilings without needing to make an ability check.
Actions:
Multiattack. The gloomlurker makes two Tendril attacks, uses Drag, and then makes two Maw attacks.

Maw. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 17 (3d8 + 4) Piercing damage.

Tendril. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 60 ft. Hit: The target becomes Grappled (escape DC 14) by one of the gloomlurker’s six tendrils and gains the Poisoned condition until the grapple ends.

A tendril can be targeted and destroyed (AC 20, HP 10, immunity to Poison and Psychic damage). Destroying a tendril frees any creature it was Grappling. Damaging a tendril does not harm the gloomlurker, and a destroyed tendril regrows at the start of the gloomlurker’s next turn.

Drag. The gloomlurker pulls each creature Grappled by it up to 30 feet straight toward its body.
Description: Gloomlurkers are predatory aberrations that cling to cavern walls and ceilings, waiting for prey to wander beneath them. Their long, venomous tendrils lash out from the darkness to ensnare victims, dragging them toward a gaping maw lined with jagged teeth. Perfectly adapted to the oppressive depths of the Underdark, they are silent hunters whose presence is often noticed only when it is far too late.

Name: Corroder Beast
Traits: Metal Sense. The corroder beast can pinpoint the location of nearby worked metal within 30 feet of itself.
Actions:
Multiattack. The corroder beast makes one Bite attack and uses Corrosive Feelers twice.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d8 + 1) Piercing damage.

Corrosive Feelers. The corroder beast targets one nonmagical metal object—armor or a weapon—worn or carried by a creature within 5 feet. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 11, the creature holding or wearing the object. Failure: The object takes a -1 penalty to the AC it provides (armor) or to its attack rolls (weapon). Armor is destroyed if the penalty reduces its AC to 10, and a weapon is destroyed if its penalty reaches -5. The penalty can be removed by casting the Mending spell on the object.

Dissolve Metal. The corroder beast touches a nonmagical metal object within 5 feet that isn't being worn or carried. The touch dissolves a 1-foot cube of the object.
Reactions: Reactive Feelers. Trigger: An attack roll hits the corroder beast. Response: The corroder beast uses Corrosive Feelers.
Description: Corroder Beasts are strange and dangerous monstrosities that inhabit the Underdark, known for their corrosive abilities, their metal-sensing capabilities, and their role as both threats and hazards in the subterranean environments they call home.

Name: Saber-Toothed Tiger
Traits: Running Leap. With a 10-foot running start, the tiger can Long Jump up to 25 feet.
Actions:
Multiattack. The tiger makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Slashing damage.
Bonus Actions: Nimble Escape. The tiger takes the Disengage or Hide action.
Description: Saber-Toothed Tigers are fierce and agile predators that inhabit various environments, known for their powerful jaws, their stealthy hunting tactics, and their role as both threats and iconic creatures in the world they roam.

Name: Abyssalfin Baron
Traits:
Blood Frenzy. The abyssalfin has Advantage on attack rolls against any creature that doesn't have all its Hit Points.

Limited Amphibiousness. The abyssalfin can breathe air and water, but it must be submerged at least once every 4 hours to avoid suffocating outside water.

Shark Telepathy. The abyssalfin can magically control sharks within 120 feet of itself, using a special telepathy.
Actions:
Multiattack. The abyssalfin makes three Trident attacks.

Trident. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Piercing damage.
Reactions: Fiendish Blood. Trigger: The abyssalfin takes Piercing or Slashing damage. Response—Constitution Saving Throw: DC 14, each creature of the abyssalfin's choice in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the abyssalfin. Failure: 10 (3d6) Acid damage, and the target is cursed until it finishes a Short or Long Rest. While cursed, the target can't benefit from the Invisible condition, its Speed decreases by 10 feet, and all Fiends within 120 feet of the target can sense its location regardless of interposing obstacles.
Description: Abyssalfin barons rule underwater domains with ruthless authority. Their command over sharks and their corrosive blood magic make them feared warlords of the deep. They lead raids against coastal settlements and rival aquatic factions, enforcing their will with brutal efficiency.

Name: Abyssalfin Priest
Traits:
Blood Frenzy. The abyssalfin has Advantage on attack rolls against any creature that doesn't have all its Hit Points.

Limited Amphibiousness. The abyssalfin can breathe air and water, but it must be submerged at least once every 4 hours to avoid suffocating outside water.

Shark Telepathy. The abyssalfin can magically control sharks within 120 feet of itself, using a special telepathy.
Actions:
Multiattack. The abyssalfin makes two Spectral Jaws attacks.

Spectral Jaws. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 11 (2d8 + 2) Force damage.

Spellcasting. The abyssalfin casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12): At will: Thaumaturgy; 2/day each: Hold Person, Tongues
Bonus Actions: Fiendish Aid (2/Day). The abyssalfin casts Bless or Healing Word, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Description: Abyssalfin priests serve as spiritual leaders and occult advisors within their aquatic clans. They wield eerie force magic shaped like spectral jaws and use their rituals to command tides, sharks, and the loyalty of their kin.

Name: Abyssalfin Warrior
Traits:
Blood Frenzy. The abyssalfin has Advantage on attack rolls against any creature that doesn't have all its Hit Points.

Limited Amphibiousness. The abyssalfin can breathe air and water, but it must be submerged at least once every 4 hours to avoid suffocating outside water.

Shark Telepathy. The abyssalfin can magically control sharks within 120 feet of itself, using a special telepathy.
Actions:
Multiattack. The abyssalfin makes two Claw attacks.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d6 + 1) Slashing damage.
Bonus Actions: Aquatic Charge. The abyssalfin swims up to its Swim Speed straight toward an enemy it can see.
Description: Abyssalfin warriors form the disciplined ranks of their underwater legions. Swift swimmers and relentless fighters, they strike with coordinated ferocity, often accompanied by sharks guided through their innate telepathic bond.

Name: Salamander
Traits: Fire Aura. At the end of each of the salamander's turns, each creature of the salamander's choice in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the salamander takes 7 (2d6) Fire damage.
Actions:
Multiattack. The salamander makes two Flame Spear attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Constrict.

Flame Spear. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Fire damage. Hit or Miss: The spear magically returns to the salamander's hand immediately after a ranged attack.

Constrict. Strength Saving Throw: DC 15, one Large or smaller creature the salamander can see within 10 feet. Failure: 11 (2d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) Fire damage. The target has the Grappled condition (escape DC 14), and it has the Restrained condition until the grapple ends.
Description: Salamanders are fiery and agile elementals that inhabit the Elemental Plane of Fire, known for their burning bodies, their spear attacks, and their role as both threats and denizens in the scorching environments they call home.

Name: Salamander Fire Snake
Traits: Fire Aura. At the end of each of the salamander's turns, each creature of the salamander's choice in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the salamander takes 3 (1d6) Fire damage.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) Fire damage.
Description: Salamander Fire Snakes are smaller and more agile elementals that inhabit the Elemental Plane of Fire, known for their burning bodies, their bite attacks, and their role as both threats and denizens in the scorching environments they call home.

Name: Salamander Inferno Master
Traits:
Fire Aura. At the end of each of the salamander's turns, each creature of the salamander's choice in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the salamander takes 10 (3d6) Fire damage.

Magic Resistance. The salamander has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The salamander makes two Flame Trident attacks.

Flame Trident. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +12, reach 5 ft. or range 30/90 ft. Hit: 16 (2d8 + 7) Piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) Fire damage. Hit or Miss: The trident magically returns to the salamander's hand immediately after a ranged attack.

Inferno Blast (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 18, each creature in a 30-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point the salamander can see within 120 feet. Failure: 35 (10d6) Fire damage, and the target starts burning, taking 5 (1d10) Fire damage at the start of each of its turns instead of the normal burning damage. The target gains 1 Exhaustion level whenever it takes this burning damage. Success: Half damage only.
Bonus Actions: Blazing Movement. The salamander moves up to its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks. During this movement, fire fills a 5-foot Emanation originating from the salamander. When the Emanation enters a creature's space, that creature takes 7 (2d6) Fire damage. A creature can take this damage only once per turn.
Description: Salamander Inferno Masters are powerful and commanding elementals that inhabit the Elemental Plane of Fire, known for their intense flames, their spear attacks, and their role as both threats and rulers in the scorching environments they call home.

Name: Satyr
Traits: Magic Resistance. The satyr has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Hooves. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, the satyr pushes the target up to 10 feet straight away from itself.

Mockery. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 12, one creature the satyr can see within 90 feet. Failure: 5 (1d6 + 2) Psychic damage.
Description: Satyrs are whimsical and mischievous fey creatures that inhabit forests and the Feywild, known for their playful nature, their musical talents, and their role as both tricksters and entertainers in the magical environments they call home.

Name: Satyr Revelmaster
Traits: Magic Resistance. The satyr has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The satyr makes three Prance attacks.

Prance. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Bludgeoning damage, and the target has the Charmed condition until the start of the satyr's next turn.

Fey Melody (Recharge 4-6). The satyr conjures a charming or frightening song. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 14, each enemy in a 60-foot Emanation originating from the satyr. Failure: The target is subjected to the song's effect:

Charming. The target has the Charmed condition for 1 minute. While Charmed, the target has the Incapacitated condition and uses all its movement to dance in place. The effect ends on the target if it takes any damage.

Frightening. 10 (2d6 + 3) Psychic damage, and the target has the Frightened condition for 1 minute. If the target ends its turn out of line of sight from the satyr, the condition ends on it.
Description: Satyr Revelmasters are charismatic and enchanting fey creatures that inhabit forests and the Feywild, known for their captivating presence, their musical talents, and their role as both entertainers and leaders in the magical environments they call home.

Name: Scarecrow
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, unconscious
Actions:
Fearsome Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) Slashing damage, and the target has the Frightened condition until the end of the scarecrow's next turn.

Terrifying Glare. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 11, one creature the scarecrow can see within 30 feet. Failure: The target has the Frightened condition until the end of the scarecrow's next turn. While Frightened, the target has the Paralyzed condition.
Description: Scarecrows are eerie and unsettling constructs that inhabit rural areas, known for their fear-inducing abilities, their resilience, and their role as both guardians and threats in the environments they haunt.

Name: Scorpion
Actions: Sting. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) Poison damage.
Description: Scorpions are small and venomous arachnids that inhabit various environments, known for their stinging tails, their resilience, and their role as both predators and threats in the ecosystems they inhabit.

Name: Scout
Actions:
Multiattack. The scout makes two attacks, using Shortsword and Longbow in any combination.

Shortsword. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage.

Longbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 150/600 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Piercing damage.
Description: Scouts are skilled and resourceful humanoids that inhabit various environments, known for their keen senses, their stealthy nature, and their role as both explorers and threats in the world they traverse.

Name: Scout Captain
Actions:
Multiattack. The scout makes two attacks, using Shortsword or Longbow in any combination.

Shortsword. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Piercing damage, plus 10 (3d6) Piercing damage if the attack was made with Advantage.

Longbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 150/600 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Piercing damage, plus 10 (3d6) Piercing damage if the attack was made with Advantage.
Bonus Actions: Aim. The scout has Advantage on the next attack roll it makes during the current turn.
Reactions: Uncanny Dodge. Trigger: The scout is hit by an attack roll. Response: The scout halves the damage (round down) it takes from that attack.
Description: Scout Captains are experienced and tactical humanoids that inhabit various environments, known for their leadership skills, their combat prowess, and their role as both commanders and threats in the world they traverse.

Name: Sea Hag
Traits:
Amphibious. The hag can breathe air and water.

Coven Magic. While within 30 feet of at least two hag allies, the hag can cast one of the following spells, requiring no Material components, using the spell's normal casting time, and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 11): Augury, Find Familiar, Identify, Locate Object, Scrying, or Unseen Servant. The hag must finish a Long Rest before using this trait to cast that spell again.

Vile Appearance. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 11, any Beast or Humanoid that starts its turn within 30 feet of the hag and can see the hag's true form. Failure: The target has the Frightened condition until the start of its next turn. Success: The target is immune to this hag's Vile Appearance for 24 hours.
Actions:
Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Slashing damage.

Death Glare (Recharge 5-6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 11, one Frightened creature the hag can see within 30 feet. Failure: If the target has 20 Hit Points or fewer, it drops to 0 Hit Points. Otherwise, the target takes 13 (3d8) Psychic damage.

Illusory Appearance. The hag casts Disguise Self, using Constitution as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13). The spell's duration is 24 hours.
Description: Sea Hags are malevolent and cunning fey creatures that inhabit coastal areas and underwater environments, known for their dark magic, their fearsome appearance, and their role as both threats and denizens in the aquatic realms they call home.

Name: Seahorse
Traits: Water Breathing. The seahorse can breathe only underwater.
Actions: Bubble Dash. While underwater, the seahorse moves up to its Swim Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.
Description: Seahorses are small and graceful aquatic creatures that inhabit underwater environments, known for their unique appearance, their gentle nature, and their role as both peaceful denizens and potential mounts in the aquatic realms they call home.

Name: Shadow
Condition Immunities: exhaustion, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, unconscious
Traits:
Amorphous. The shadow can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.

Sunlight Weakness. While in sunlight, the shadow has Disadvantage on D20 Tests.
Actions: Draining Swipe. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Necrotic damage, and the target's Strength score decreases by 1d4. The target dies if this reduces that score to 0. If a Humanoid is slain by this attack, a Shadow rises from the corpse 1d4 hours later.
Bonus Actions: Shadow Stealth. While in Dim Light or Darkness, the shadow takes the Hide action.
Description: Shadows are eerie and malevolent undead creatures that inhabit the Shadowfell and other dark environments, known for their draining touch, their stealthy nature, and their role as both threats and denizens in the shadowy realms they call home.

Name: Shadow Demon
Damage Resistances: acid, bludgeoning, cold, fire, lightning, piercing, slashing, thunder
Condition Immunities: exhaustion, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained
Traits:
Demonic Restoration. When destroyed outside the Abyss, the shadow demon dissolves into black ichor and instantly reforms in the Abyss with full Hit Points.

Incorporeal Movement. The demon drifts through creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain, taking 5 (1d10) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

Light Sensitivity. While in Bright Light, the demon has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.
Actions: Umbral Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 16 (3d8 + 3) Psychic damage.
Bonus Actions: Shadow Stealth. While in Dim Light or Darkness, the demon takes the Hide action.
Description: Shadow Demons are malevolent and elusive fiendish creatures that inhabit the Abyss and other dark environments, known for their shadowy forms, their demonic nature, and their role as both threats and denizens in the shadowy realms they call home.

Name: Shadow Dragon
Senses: Blindsight 30 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 21
Traits:
Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Living Shadow. While in Dim Light or Darkness, the dragon has Resistance to damage that isn't Force, Psychic, or Radiant.

Sunlight Sensitivity. While in sunlight, the dragon has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes three Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 10 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) Necrotic damage.

Shadow Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 17, each creature in a 60-foot Cone. Failure: 35 (10d6) Necrotic damage. Success: Half damage. Failure or Success: A Humanoid reduced to 0 Hit Points by this damage dies, and a Shadow rises from the corpse. The shadow is under the dragon's control and shares the dragon's Initiative count but acts immediately after the dragon.
Bonus Actions: Shadow Stealth. While in Dim Light or Darkness, the dragon takes the Hide action.
Legendary Actions:
Pounce. The dragon moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Rend attack.

Veil of Shadow. The dragon uses Shadow Stealth, and one creature of its choice that it can see within 10 feet of it takes 10 (3d6) Necrotic damage. The dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.
Regional Effects:
The region around a shadow dragon's lair is twisted by its presence, creating the following effects:

Negative Energy Suffusion. Whenever a creature within 1 mile of the lair regains Hit Points from a spell, it subtracts 1d10 from the number of Hit Points regained.

Stifling Shadows. Within 1 mile of the lair, effects that normally create Bright Light instead create Dim Light, and creatures there have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

If the dragon dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.
Description: Shadow Dragons are malevolent and powerful draconic creatures that inhabit the Shadowfell and other dark environments, known for their shadowy forms, their necrotic breath, and their role as both threats and denizens in the shadowy realms they call home.

Name: Shambling Mound
Traits:
Lightning Absorption. When subjected to Lightning damage, the shambling mound regains Hit Points equal to the damage dealt.

Storm-Fed Growth. The mound’s body is interwoven with conductive vines that store electrical energy, causing faint arcs of static to dance across its surface.
Actions:
Multiattack. The shambling mound makes three Charged Tendril attacks. It can replace one attack with Engulf.

Charged Tendril. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage plus 5 (2d4) Lightning damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, it is pulled 5 feet toward the mound.

Engulf. Strength Saving Throw: DC 15, one Medium or smaller creature within 5 feet. Failure: The target is pulled inside the mound’s mass and becomes Grappled (escape DC 14). While Grappled, the target is Blinded and Restrained and takes 10 (3d6) Lightning damage at the start of each of its turns. The mound can move with the target without penalty and can have only one creature Engulfed at a time.
Description: Shambling Mounds are eerie and formidable plant creatures that inhabit forests and swamps, known for their ability to absorb lightning, their storm-fed growth, and their role as both threats and denizens in the wild environments they call home.

Name: Shield Guardian
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 10
Languages: Understands commands given in any language but can't speak
Traits:
Bound. The guardian is magically bound to an amulet. While the guardian and its amulet are on the same plane of existence, the amulet's wearer can telepathically call the guardian to travel to it, and the guardian knows the distance and direction to the amulet. If the guardian is within 60 feet of the amulet's wearer, half of any damage the wearer takes (round up) is transferred to the guardian.

Regeneration. The guardian regains 10 Hit Points at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 Hit Point.

Spell Storing. A spellcaster who wears the guardian's amulet can cause the guardian to store one spell of level 4 or lower. To do so, the wearer must cast the spell on the guardian while within 5 feet of it. The spell has no effect but is stored within the guardian. Any previously stored spell is lost when a new spell is stored. The guardian can cast the spell stored with any parameters set by the original caster, requiring no spell components and using the caster's spellcasting ability. The stored spell is then lost.
Actions:
Multiattack. The guardian makes two Fist attacks.

Fist. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) Force damage.
Reactions: Protection. Trigger: An attack roll hits the wearer of the guardian's amulet while the wearer is within 5 feet of the guardian. Response: The wearer gains a +5 bonus to AC, including against the triggering attack and possibly causing it to miss, until the start of the guardian's next turn.
Description: Shield Guardians are powerful and resilient constructs that serve as protectors and enforcers, known for their bound nature, their regenerative abilities, and their role as both guardians and threats in the environments they inhabit.

Name: Shrieker Fungus
Reactions: Shriek. Trigger: A creature or a source of Bright Light moves within 30 feet of the shrieker. Response: The shrieker emits a shriek audible within 300 feet of itself for 1 minute or until the shrieker dies.
Description: Shrieker Fungi are eerie and reactive plant creatures that inhabit the Underdark, known for their ability to emit loud shrieks in response to stimuli, their passive nature, and their role as both environmental hazards and potential alarms in the subterranean realms they call home.

Name: Silver Dragon Wyrmling
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 14
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) Piercing damage.

Cold Breath (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 13, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: 18 (4d8) Cold damage. Success: Half damage.

Paralyzing Breath. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 13, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. First Failure: The target has the Incapacitated condition until the end of its next turn, when it repeats the save. Second Failure: The target has the Paralyzed condition, and it repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.
Description: Silver Dragon Wyrmlings are young and noble draconic creatures that inhabit mountainous and urban environments, known for their cold breath, their paralyzing breath, and their role as both protectors and threats in the realms they call home.

Name: Skeleton
Languages: Understands Common plus one other language but can't speak
Actions:
Shortsword. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Piercing damage.

Shortbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 80/320 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Piercing damage.
Description: Skeletons are reanimated and malevolent undead creatures that inhabit the Shadowfell, the Underdark, and urban environments, known for their skeletal forms, their martial abilities, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they call home.

Name: Spawnling Larva
Traits: Magic Resistance. The spawnling larva has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage.
Description: Spawnling Larvae are small and voracious aberrant creatures that inhabit the chaotic plane of Limbo, known for their burrowing abilities, their resistance to damage, and their role as both threats and denizens in the ever-shifting realms they call home.

Name: Smoke Shardling
Traits: Shatter Burst. The shardling collapses into choking fumes when it dies. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 11, each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the shardling. Failure: The target is Poisoned until the end of its next turn.
Actions:
Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Slashing damage.

Cinder Breath (Recharge 6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 11, one creature the shardling can see within 15 feet. Failure: The target is Blinded until the end of the shardling's next turn.
Description: Smoke Shardlings are small and elusive elemental creatures that inhabit the Elemental Planes, known for their smoky forms, their shatter burst ability, and their role as both threats and denizens in the ever-shifting realms they call home.

Name: Solar
Traits:
Divine Awareness. The solar instantly recognizes spoken lies.

Exalted Restoration. If the solar dies outside Mount Celestia, its body dissolves into radiant light, and it immediately reforms elsewhere on Mount Celestia with full Hit Points.

Legendary Resistance (4/Day). If the solar fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Magic Resistance. The solar has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The solar makes two Flying Sword attacks. It may replace one attack with a use of Slaying Bow.

Flying Sword. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +15, reach 10 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 22 (4d6 + 8) Slashing damage plus 36 (8d8) Radiant damage. Hit or Miss: After a ranged attack, the sword returns to the solar’s hand or hovers within 5 feet of it.

Slaying Bow. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 21, one creature the solar can see within 600 feet. Failure: If the creature has 100 Hit Points or fewer, it dies instantly. Otherwise, it takes 24 (4d8 + 6) Piercing damage plus 36 (8d8) Radiant damage.

Spellcasting. The solar casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 25): At will: Detect Evil and Good; 1/day each: Commune, Control Weather, Dispel Evil and Good, Resurrection
Bonus Actions: Divine Aid (3/Day). The solar casts Cure Wounds (2nd‑level), Lesser Restoration, or Remove Curse, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Legendary Actions:
Blinding Gaze. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 25, one creature the solar can see within 120 feet. Failure: The creature is Blinded for 1 minute. The solar cannot use this action again until the start of its next turn.

Radiant Teleport. The solar teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 25, each creature in a 10‑foot Emanation centered on the solar at its destination. Failure: 11 (2d10) Radiant damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Solars are the highest-ranking angelic warriors, embodiments of divine authority and radiant power. Their presence shines with overwhelming holiness, and their weapons channel pure celestial energy capable of smiting even the mightiest foes. As champions of the Upper Planes, they intervene only in the most dire cosmic conflicts, serving as protectors, judges, and executioners of divine will.

Name: Watcher Spawn
Actions:
Multiattack. The watcher spawn uses Gaze Bolts twice.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage.

Gaze Bolts. The watcher spawn fires one of the following magical bolts at a target it can see within 90 feet (roll 1d4; reroll if the spawn has already used that bolt this turn):1) Disorienting Bolt. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 12. Failure: 5 (2d4) Psychic damage, and the target can't take Reactions until the end of its next turn. On its next turn, the target can't move and must use its action to make a melee or ranged attack against a random creature within range. If it can't attack, it does nothing.2) Paralysis Bolt. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12. Failure: The target is Paralyzed and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.3) Dread Bolt. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 12. Failure: 5 (2d4) Psychic damage, and the target is Frightened until the end of its next turn.4) Withering Bolt. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12. Failure: 16 (3d10) Necrotic damage. Success: Half damage.
Reactions: Spell Rebound. Trigger: The watcher spawn succeeds on a saving throw against a spell, or a spell's attack roll misses it. Response—Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 12, one creature the spawn can see within 120 feet. Failure: 10 (3d6) Force damage.
Description: Watcher Spawns are enigmatic and vigilant aberrant creatures that inhabit the Underdark, known for their watchful nature, their gaze bolts, and their role as both threats and denizens in the subterranean realms they call home.

Name: Specter
Damage Resistances: acid, bludgeoning, cold, fire, lightning, piercing, slashing, thunder
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, unconscious
Languages: Understands Common plus one other language but can't speak
Traits:
Incorporeal Movement. The specter drifts through creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain, taking 5 (1d10) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

Sunlight Sensitivity. While in sunlight, the specter has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.

Echo of a Soul. The specter is a lingering emotional imprint, unable to form words but capable of understanding languages it once knew.
Actions: Life Drain. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (2d6) Necrotic damage. The target’s Hit Point maximum is reduced by the same amount.
Description: Specters are haunting and malevolent undead creatures that inhabit the Underdark and urban environments, known for their incorporeal nature, their life-draining abilities, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they call home.

Name: Sphinx of Lore
Traits:
Inscrutable. The sphinx cannot be magically spied upon or have its thoughts read without its consent. Creatures attempting to gauge its motives with Wisdom (Insight) checks do so with Disadvantage.

Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the sphinx fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The sphinx makes three Claw attacks.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 5 ft. Hit: 14 (3d6 + 4) Slashing damage.

Mind-Rending Roar (Recharge 5–6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 16, each enemy in a 300‑foot Emanation centered on the sphinx. Failure: 35 (10d6) Psychic damage, and the creature becomes Incapacitated until the start of the sphinx’s next turn.

Spellcasting. The sphinx casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Intelligence as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 16): At will: Detect Magic, Identify, Mage Hand, Minor Illusion, Prestidigitation; 1/day each: Dispel Magic, Legend Lore, Locate Object, Plane Shift, Remove Curse, Tongues
Legendary Actions:
Arcane Prowl. The sphinx teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see and makes one Claw attack.

Weight of Years. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 16, one creature the sphinx can see within 120 feet. Failure: The creature gains 1 level of Exhaustion and appears 3d10 years older while it has any Exhaustion levels. The sphinx cannot use this action again until the start of its next turn.
Regional Effects:
A sphinx of lore or valor alters the region around its lair:

Distant Sight. While in its lair, the sphinx can cast Clairvoyance without components, using its Spellcasting ability. When cast this way, the spell’s range is 1 mile.

Infusion of Knowledge. When the sphinx or an ally takes the Study action within 1 mile of the lair, it adds 1d6 to the ability check.

These effects end immediately if the sphinx dies or relocates its lair.
Description: Sphinxes of Lore are ancient celestial guardians who safeguard forgotten truths and cosmic mysteries. Their minds hold vast libraries of knowledge, and their riddles test the worth of those who seek their wisdom. Though impartial, they can be formidable foes when their sacred charge is threatened.

Name: Sphinx of Secrets
Traits:
Inscrutable. The sphinx cannot be magically observed or have its thoughts read without its permission. Wisdom (Insight) checks to discern its motives are made with Disadvantage.

Magic Resistance. The sphinx has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The sphinx makes three Claw attacks. It may replace one attack with Curse of the Riddle.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Radiant damage.

Curse of the Riddle. Intelligence Saving Throw: DC 15, one creature the sphinx can see within 60 feet. Failure: 21 (6d6) Psychic damage, and the creature becomes cursed with a confounding riddle. While cursed, it has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls. If it takes the Magic action, it must succeed on a DC 15 Intelligence saving throw or waste the action. The creature may take the Study action to attempt a DC 15 Intelligence check to solve the riddle and end the curse. The curse ends early if the sphinx curses a different target.

Spellcasting. The sphinx casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Intelligence as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15): At will: Detect Magic, Identify, Prestidigitation; 1/day each: Locate Object, Remove Curse
Description: Sphinxes of Secrets guard hidden truths and forbidden knowledge. Their riddles ensnare the minds of the unworthy, and their radiant claws punish those who seek answers without respect. They are impartial judges of curiosity, rewarding insight while punishing arrogance.

Name: Sphinx of Valor
Traits:
Inscrutable. The sphinx cannot be magically observed or have its thoughts read without its consent. Wisdom (Insight) checks to discern its motives are made with Disadvantage.

Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the sphinx fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.
Actions:
Multiattack. The sphinx makes two Claw attacks and uses Roar.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +12, reach 5 ft. Hit: 20 (4d6 + 6) Slashing damage.

Roar (3/Day). Each time the sphinx roars, it triggers a different magical effect. The sequence resets after a Long Rest:

First Roar. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 20, each enemy in a 500‑foot Emanation. Failure: The creature becomes Frightened for 1 minute.

Second Roar. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 20, each enemy in a 500‑foot Emanation. Failure: The creature becomes Paralyzed. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. After 1 minute, it automatically succeeds.

Third Roar. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 20, each enemy in a 500‑foot Emanation. Failure: 44 (8d10) Thunder damage and the creature is knocked Prone. Success: Half damage only.

Spellcasting. The sphinx casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Wisdom as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 20): At will: Detect Evil and Good, Thaumaturgy; 1/day each: Detect Magic, Dispel Magic, Greater Restoration, Heroes' Feast, Zone of Truth
Legendary Actions:
Arcane Prowl. The sphinx teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see and makes one Claw attack.

Weight of Years. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 16, one creature the sphinx can see within 120 feet. Failure: The creature gains 1 level of Exhaustion and appears 3d10 years older while it has any Exhaustion levels. The sphinx cannot use this action again until the start of its next turn.
Regional Effects:
A sphinx of lore or valor alters the region around its lair:

Distant Sight. While in its lair, the sphinx can cast Clairvoyance without components, using its Spellcasting ability. When cast this way, the spell’s range is 1 mile.

Infusion of Knowledge. When the sphinx or an ally takes the Study action within 1 mile of the lair, it adds 1d6 to the ability check.

These effects end immediately if the sphinx dies or relocates its lair.
Description: Sphinxes of Valor embody courage and unwavering judgment. They test the resolve of those who seek their guidance, unleashing mighty roars that challenge the spirit, body, and will of intruders. As guardians of sacred places and ancient truths, they reward bravery and integrity while punishing deceit and cowardice.

Name: Sphinx of Wonder
Traits: Magic Resistance. The sphinx has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions: Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) Radiant damage.
Reactions: Burst of Ingenuity (2/Day). Trigger: The sphinx or a creature within 30 feet makes an ability check or saving throw. Response: The sphinx adds 2 to the roll.
Description: Sphinxes of Wonder are small celestial guides who inspire creativity and curiosity. Though far less imposing than their larger kin, they possess radiant magic and a knack for nudging mortals toward clever solutions. Their presence brings insight, encouragement, and a spark of divine inspiration.

Name: Spider
Traits:
Spider Climb. The spider can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

Web Walker. The spider ignores movement restrictions caused by webs, and the spider knows the location of any other creature in contact with the same web.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Piercing damage plus 2 (1d4) Poison damage.
Description: Spiders are small and agile arachnids that inhabit a wide range of environments, known for their web-spinning abilities, their venomous bites, and their role as both predators and prey in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Spined Devil
Senses: Darkvision 120 ft. (unimpeded by magical Darkness), passive Perception 12
Traits:
Flyby. The devil does not provoke Opportunity Attacks when it flies out of a creature’s reach.

Magic Resistance. The devil has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The devil makes two attacks, choosing Infernal Fork or Tail Spine in any combination.

Infernal Fork. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) Fire damage.

Tail Spine. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 20/80 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) Fire damage.
Description: Spined devils are nimble, winged fiends that serve as scouts, messengers, and harassers within the Nine Hells. Their bodies bristle with burning spines they can fling from afar, while their quick aerial movements allow them to dart in and out of combat with ease. Though small, they are relentless and cruel, thriving in the infernal hierarchy as opportunistic predators and loyal servants to more powerful devils.

Name: Spirit Naga
Traits: Fiendish Restoration. If it dies, the naga returns to life in 1d6 days and regains all its Hit Points. Only a Wish spell can prevent this trait from functioning.
Actions:
Multiattack. The naga makes three attacks, using Bite or Necrotic Ray in any combination.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) Piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) Poison damage.

Necrotic Ray. Ranged Attack Roll: +6, range 60 ft. Hit: 21 (6d6) Necrotic damage.

Spellcasting. The naga casts one of the following spells, requiring no Somatic or Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14): At will: Detect Magic, Mage Hand, Minor Illusion, Water Breathing; 2/day each: Detect Thoughts, Dimension Door, Hold Person (level 3 version), Lightning Bolt (level 4 version)
Description: Spirit Nagas are large and malevolent fiendish creatures that inhabit the Lower Planes and the Underdark, known for their serpentine bodies, their necrotic powers, and their role as both threats and denizens in the dark realms they call home.

Name: Sprite
Actions:
Needle Sword. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d4 + 4) Piercing damage.

Enchanting Bow. Ranged Attack Roll: +6, range 40/160 ft. Hit: 1 Piercing damage, and the target has the Charmed condition until the start of the sprite's next turn.

Heart Sight. Charisma Saving Throw: DC 10, one creature within 5 feet the sprite can see (Celestials, Fiends, and Undead automatically fail the save). Failure: The sprite knows the target's emotions and alignment.

Invisibility. The sprite casts Invisibility on itself, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability.
Description: Sprites are small and elusive fey creatures that inhabit forests and the Feywild, known for their magical abilities, their charming nature, and their role as both protectors and tricksters in the realms they call home.

Name: Spy
Skills: Deception +5, Insight +4, Investigation +5, Perception +6, Sleight of Hand +4, Stealth +6
Actions:
Shortsword. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Poison damage.

Hand Crossbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 30/120 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Poison damage.
Bonus Actions: Cunning Action. The spy takes the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.
Description: Spies are skilled and elusive humanoids that inhabit a wide range of environments, known for their stealthy abilities, their deceptive nature, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they call home.

Name: Spy Master
Skills: Deception +7, Insight +7, Investigation +8, Perception +11, Sleight of Hand +9, Stealth +13
Actions:
Multiattack. The spy makes three attacks, using Rapier or Hand Crossbow in any combination.

Rapier. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Poison damage.

Hand Crossbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +9, range 30/120 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Piercing damage plus 9 (2d8) Poison damage.

Smoke Bomb (1/Day). The spy throws a bomb to a point it can see within 30 feet of itself. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 16, each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point. Failure: 28 (8d6) Poison damage, and the target has the Blinded condition until the end of the spy's next turn. Success: Half damage only.
Bonus Actions: Cunning Action. The spy takes the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.
Description: Spy Masters are highly skilled and elusive humanoids that inhabit a wide range of environments, known for their exceptional stealthy abilities, their deceptive nature, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they call home.

Name: Steam Shardling
Traits:
Blurred Form. Attack rolls against the shardling are made with Disadvantage unless it has the Incapacitated condition.

Shatter Burst. The shardling erupts into scalding vapor when it dies. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 10, each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the shardling. Failure: 5 (2d4) Fire damage. Success: Half damage.
Actions:
Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 2 (1d4) Slashing damage plus 2 (1d4) Fire damage.

Steam Breath (Recharge 6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 10, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: 5 (2d4) Fire damage, and the target's Speed is reduced by 10 feet until the end of the shardling's next turn. Success: Half damage only. Failure or Success: Being underwater doesn't grant Resistance to this Fire damage.
Description: Steam Shardlings are small and malevolent elemental creatures that inhabit the Elemental Planes, known for their scalding attacks, their blurred forms, and their role as both threats and denizens in the fiery realms they call home.

Name: Stirge
Actions:
Proboscis. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Piercing damage, and the stirge attaches to the target. While attached, the stirge can't make Proboscis attacks, and the target takes 5 (2d4) Necrotic damage at the start of each of the stirge's turns.

The stirge can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement. The target or a creature within 5 feet of it can detach the stirge as an action.
Environment: Desert, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Mountain, Swamp, Underdark, Urban
Description: Stirges are small and bloodsucking monstrosities that inhabit a wide range of environments, known for their parasitic nature, their proboscis attacks, and their role as both pests and threats in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Stone Giant
Actions:
Multiattack. The giant makes two attacks, using Stone Club or Boulder in any combination.

Stone Club. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 15 ft. Hit: 22 (3d10 + 6) Bludgeoning damage.

Boulder. Ranged Attack Roll: +9, range 60/240 ft. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.
Reactions: Deflect Missile (Recharge 5-6). Trigger: The giant is hit by a ranged attack roll and takes Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage from it. Response: The giant reduces the damage it takes from the attack by 11 (1d10 + 6), and if that damage is reduced to 0, the giant can redirect some of the attack's force. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 17, one creature the giant can see within 60 feet. Failure: 11 (1d10 + 6) Force damage.
Description: Stone Giants are large and powerful giants that inhabit mountains and the Underdark, known for their stone-based attacks, their formidable strength, and their role as both threats and denizens in the rocky realms they call home.

Name: Stone Golem
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned
Languages: Understands Common plus two other languages but can't speak
Traits:
Immutable Form. The golem can't shape-shift.

Magic Resistance. The golem has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The golem makes two attacks, using Slam or Force Bolt in any combination.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 5 ft. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) Bludgeoning damage plus 9 (2d8) Force damage.

Force Bolt. Ranged Attack Roll: +9, range 120 ft. Hit: 22 (4d10) Force damage.
Bonus Actions: Slow (Recharge 5-6). The golem casts the Slow spell, requiring no spell components and using Constitution as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17).
Description: Stone Golems are large and powerful constructs that can be found in a wide range of environments, known for their stone-based attacks, their formidable strength, and their role as both threats and guardians in the realms they call home.

Name: Storm Giant
Senses: Darkvision 120 ft., Truesight 30 ft., passive Perception 20
Traits: Amphibious. The giant can breathe air and water.
Actions:
Multiattack. The giant makes two attacks, using Storm Sword or Thunderbolt in any combination.

Storm Sword. Melee Attack Roll: +14, reach 10 ft. Hit: 23 (4d6 + 9) Slashing damage plus 13 (3d8) Lightning damage.

Spellcasting. The giant casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 18): At will: Detect Magic, Light; 1/day: Control Weather

Thunderbolt. Ranged Attack Roll: +14, range 500 ft. Hit: 22 (2d12 + 9) Lightning damage, and the target has the Blinded and Deafened conditions until the start of the giant's next turn.

Lightning Storm (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 18, each creature in a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high Cylinder originating from a point the giant can see within 500 feet. Failure: 55 (10d10) Lightning damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Storm Giants are large and majestic giants that inhabit coastal and underwater environments, known for their storm-based attacks, their formidable strength, and their role as both protectors and threats in the realms they call home.

Name: Succubus
Traits: Incubus Form. When the succubus finishes a Long Rest, it can assume the form of an Incubus, using that stat block instead of this one.
Actions:
Multiattack. The succubus makes one Fiendish Touch attack and uses Charm or Draining Kiss.

Fiendish Touch. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 16 (2d10 + 5) Psychic damage.

Charm. The succubus casts Dominate Person (8th‑level), requiring no components and using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15).

Draining Kiss. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 15, one creature Charmed by the succubus within 5 feet. Failure: 13 (3d8) Psychic damage. Success: Half damage. Failure or Success: The creature’s Hit Point maximum is reduced by the amount of damage taken.
Bonus Actions: Shape-Shift. The succubus transforms into a Medium or Small Humanoid or returns to its true form. Its statistics remain unchanged except that its Fly Speed is available only in its true form. Equipment does not transform.
Description: Succubi are manipulative fiends who thrive on emotional influence and psychic predation. They slip easily into mortal societies through shapeshifting and charm, feeding on devotion, desire, and weakness. Their touch drains vitality, and their kisses steal fragments of the soul, making them both alluring and deadly.

Name: Swarm of Bats
Condition Immunities: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, prone, restrained, stunned
Traits: Swarm. The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny bat. The swarm can't regain Hit Points or gain Temporary Hit Points.
Actions: Bites. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (2d4) Piercing damage, or 2 (1d4) Piercing damage if the swarm is Bloodied.
Description: Swarms of Bats are large and noisy groups of tiny beasts that inhabit a wide range of environments, known for their echolocation abilities, their swarming attacks, and their role as both pests and denizens in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Swarm of Crawling Claws
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, incapacitated, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, stunned
Traits: Swarm. The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny creature. The swarm can't regain Hit Points or gain Temporary Hit Points.
Actions: Swarm of Grasping Hands. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 20 (4d8 + 2) Necrotic damage, or 11 (2d8 + 2) Necrotic damage if the swarm is Bloodied. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.
Description: Swarms of Crawling Claws are medium-sized groups of tiny undead creatures that can be found in a wide range of environments, known for their necrotic attacks, their swarming nature, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they call home.

Name: Swarm of Dretches
Damage Resistances: bludgeoning, cold, fire, lightning, piercing, slashing
Condition Immunities: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, stunned
Languages: Abyssal; telepathy 60 ft. (works only with creatures that understand Abyssal)
Traits:
Fetid Aura. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12, any creature that starts its turn in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the swarm. Failure: The target has the Poisoned condition until the start of its next turn. While Poisoned, the target can take either an action or a Bonus Action on its turn, not both, and it can't take Reactions.

Swarm. The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Small creature. The swarm can't regain Hit Points or gain Temporary Hit Points.
Actions:
Multiattack. The swarm makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (3d6 + 2) Slashing damage, or 9 (3d4 + 2) Slashing damage if the swarm is Bloodied.
Description: Swarms of Dretches are large and foul-smelling groups of small fiendish creatures that inhabit the Abyss, known for their poisonous aura, their swarming attacks, and their role as both threats and denizens in the chaotic realms they call home.

Name: Swarm of Insects
Speed: 20 ft.; Climb or Fly 20 ft. (DM's choice)
Condition Immunities: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, prone, restrained, stunned
Traits:
Spider Climb. If the swarm has a Climb Speed, the swarm can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

Swarm. The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny insect. The swarm can't regain Hit Points or gain Temporary Hit Points.
Actions: Bites. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) Poison damage, or 3 (1d4 + 1) Poison damage if the swarm is Bloodied.
Environment: Desert, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Swamp, Underdark, Urban
Description: Swarms of Insects are medium-sized groups of tiny beasts that can be found in a wide range of environments, known for their swarming attacks, their ability to climb or fly, and their role as both pests and denizens in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Swarm of Larvae
Condition Immunities: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, prone, restrained, stunned
Traits: Swarm. The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through an opening large enough for a Medium creature. The swarm can't regain Hit Points or gain Temporary Hit Points.
Actions: Bites. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (2d6 + 2) Necrotic damage, or 7 (2d4 + 2) Necrotic damage if the swarm is Bloodied.
Description: Swarms of Larvae are large and writhing groups of medium fiendish creatures that inhabit the Lower Planes, known for their necrotic bites, their swarming nature, and their role as both threats and denizens in the dark realms they call home.

Name: Swarm of Lemures
Condition Immunities: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, stunned
Senses: Darkvision 120 ft. (unimpeded by magical Darkness), passive Perception 11
Traits:
Hellish Restoration. If the swarm dies in the Nine Hells, it revives with all its Hit Points in 1d10 days unless it is killed by a creature under the effects of a Bless spell or its remains are sprinkled with Holy Water.

Swarm. The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through an opening large enough for a Medium creature. The swarm can't regain Hit Points or gain Temporary Hit Points.
Actions:
Multiattack. The swarm makes two Vile Slime attacks.

Vile Slime. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d8 + 2) Poison damage, or 9 (2d6 + 2) Poison damage if the swarm is Bloodied.
Description: Swarms of Lemures are large and writhing groups of medium fiendish creatures that inhabit the Nine Hells, known for their poisonous attacks, their swarming nature, and their role as both threats and denizens in the infernal realms they call home.

Name: Swarm of Piranhas
Condition Immunities: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, prone, restrained, stunned
Traits:
Swarm. The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny piranha. The swarm can't regain Hit Points or gain Temporary Hit Points.

Water Breathing. The swarm can breathe only underwater.
Actions: Bites. Melee Attack Roll: +5 (with Advantage if the target doesn't have all its Hit Points), reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) Piercing damage, or 5 (1d4 + 3) Piercing damage if the swarm is Bloodied.
Description: Swarms of Piranhas are medium-sized groups of tiny beasts that inhabit underwater environments, known for their aggressive attacks, their ability to breathe underwater, and their role as both threats and denizens in the aquatic realms they call home.

Name: Swarm of Rats
Condition Immunities: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, prone, restrained, stunned
Traits: Swarm. The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny rat. The swarm can't regain Hit Points or gain Temporary Hit Points.
Actions: Bites. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (2d4) Piercing damage, or 2 (1d4) Piercing damage if the swarm is Bloodied.
Description: Swarms of Rats are medium-sized groups of tiny beasts that can be found in a wide range of environments, known for their swarming attacks, their ability to climb, and their role as both pests and denizens in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Swarm of Ravens
Condition Immunities: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, prone, restrained, stunned
Traits: Swarm. The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny raven. The swarm can't regain Hit Points or gain Temporary Hit Points.
Actions:
Beaks. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage, or 2 (1d4) Piercing damage if the swarm is Bloodied.

Cacophony (Recharge 6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 10, one creature in the swarm's space. Failure: The target has the Deafened condition until the start of the swarm's next turn. While Deafened, the target also has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.
Description: Swarms of Ravens are medium-sized groups of tiny birds that can be found in a wide range of environments, known for their swarming attacks, their ability to fly, and their role as both threats and denizens in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Swarm of Stirges
Condition Immunities: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, prone, restrained, stunned
Traits: Swarm. The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny creature. The swarm can't regain Hit Points or gain Temporary Hit Points.
Actions: Swarm of Proboscises. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 14 (2d10 + 3) Piercing damage, or 8 (1d10 + 3) Piercing damage if the swarm is Bloodied. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature in the swarm's space, the target has the Grappled condition (escape DC 13). Until the grapple ends, the target takes 7 (2d6) Necrotic damage at the end of each of its turns.
Environment: Desert, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Mountain, Swamp, Underdark, Urban
Description: Swarms of Stirges are medium-sized groups of tiny monstrosities that can be found in a wide range of environments, known for their blood-sucking attacks, their swarming nature, and their role as both threats and denizens in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Swarm of Venomous Snakes
Condition Immunities: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, prone, restrained, stunned
Traits: Swarm. The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny snake. The swarm can't regain Hit Points or gain Temporary Hit Points.
Actions: Bites. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) Piercing damage—or 6 (1d4 + 4) Piercing damage if the swarm is Bloodied—plus 10 (3d6) Poison damage.
Environment: Coastal, Desert, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Swamp
Description: Swarms of Venomous Snakes are medium-sized groups of tiny beasts that can be found in a wide range of environments, known for their venomous bites, their ability to swim, and their role as both threats and denizens in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Vorgathon, the World-Ravener
Traits:
Legendary Resistance (6/Day). If Vorgathon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Primordial Carapace. Vorgathon has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Molten Shell. If Vorgathon is targeted by Magic Missile or a spell requiring a ranged attack roll, roll 1d6. On a 1-5, Vorgathon is unaffected. On a 6, the spell is reflected back at the caster.

Siege Titan. Vorgathon deals double damage to objects and structures.
Actions:
Multiattack. Vorgathon makes one Maw attack and three other attacks, using Talon or Tail in any combination.

Maw. Melee Attack Roll: +19, reach 15 ft. Hit: 36 (4d12 + 10) Piercing damage, and the target becomes Grappled (escape DC 20). While Grappled, the target is Restrained and can't teleport.

Talon. Melee Attack Roll: +19, reach 15 ft. Hit: 28 (4d8 + 10) Slashing damage.

Tail. Melee Attack Roll: +19, reach 30 ft. Hit: 23 (3d8 + 10) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is Huge or smaller, it is knocked Prone.

Cataclysmic Roar (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 27, each creature and unattended object in a 150-foot Cone. Failure: 78 (12d12) Thunder damage, and the target becomes Deafened and Frightened until the end of Vorgathon's next turn. Success: Half damage.
Bonus Actions:
Devour. Strength Saving Throw: DC 27, one Large or smaller creature Grappled by Vorgathon. Failure: The target is swallowed and the Grappled condition ends. A swallowed creature is Blinded, Restrained, can't teleport, has Total Cover, and takes 56 (16d6) Acid damage at the start of each of Vorgathon's turns.

If Vorgathon takes 60+ damage in a single turn from inside, it must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution save or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, which fall Prone within 10 feet. If Vorgathon dies, swallowed creatures can escape using 20 feet of movement, exiting Prone.
Legendary Actions:
Onslaught. Vorgathon moves up to half its Speed and makes one Talon or Tail attack.

World-Tremor Stride. Vorgathon moves up to its Speed, then unleashes a shock wave in a 60-foot Emanation. Creatures in the area lose Concentration and, if Medium or smaller, fall Prone. Vorgathon can't use this action again until the start of its next turn.
Description: Vorgathon, the World-Ravener, is a gargantuan monstrosity that serves as a powerful and destructive force in the world, known for its immense strength, its ability to burrow and climb, and its role as a legendary threat in the realms it inhabits.

Name: Kryntar Skirmisher
Actions:
Multiattack. The kryntar makes two attacks, using Twinblade or Razor Disk in any combination.

Twinblade. Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d8 + 1) Slashing damage plus 2 (1d4) Poison damage.

Razor Disk. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 30/120 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Slashing damage.
Bonus Actions: Leap. The kryntar jumps up to 15 feet by spending 5 feet of movement.
Description: Kryntar Skirmishers are medium-sized monstrosities that serve as agile and deadly combatants in the world, known for their twinblade and razor disk attacks, their ability to leap, and their role as skilled warriors in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Kryntar Mindlance
Actions:
Multiattack. The kryntar makes three Psionic Javelin attacks.

Psionic Javelin. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 18 (4d6 + 4) Psychic damage.

Spellcasting. The kryntar casts one of the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15): At will: Mage Hand (the hand is Invisible); 1/day each: Detect Thoughts, Sending, Synaptic Static
Description: Kryntar Mindlances are medium-sized monstrosities that serve as powerful and intelligent combatants in the world, known for their psionic javelin attacks, their spellcasting abilities, and their role as formidable foes in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Tiger
Actions: Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Slashing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.
Bonus Actions: Nimble Escape. The tiger takes the Disengage or Hide action.
Description: Tigers are large and powerful beasts that can be found in a wide range of environments, known for their rend attacks, their ability to escape quickly, and their role as both predators and denizens in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Tough
Traits: Pack Tactics. The tough has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the tough's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions:
Mace. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Bludgeoning damage.

Heavy Crossbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +3, range 100/400 ft. Hit: 6 (1d10 + 1) Piercing damage.
Description: Toughs are small or medium humanoids that serve as hardy and resilient combatants in the world, known for their pack tactics, their mace and heavy crossbow attacks, and their role as common foes in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Tough Boss
Traits: Pack Tactics. The tough has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the tough's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions:
Multiattack. The tough makes two attacks, using Warhammer or Heavy Crossbow in any combination.

Warhammer. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, the tough pushes the target up to 10 feet straight away from itself.

Heavy Crossbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 100/400 ft. Hit: 13 (2d10 + 2) Piercing damage.
Description: Tough Bosses are small or medium humanoids that serve as formidable and resilient combatants in the world, known for their pack tactics, their warhammer and heavy crossbow attacks, and their role as common leaders of toughs in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Treant
Traits: Siege Monster. The treant deals double damage to objects and structures.
Actions:
Multiattack. The treant makes two Slam attacks.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 5 ft. Hit: 16 (3d6 + 6) Bludgeoning damage.

Hail of Bark. Ranged Attack Roll: +10, range 180 ft. Hit: 28 (4d10 + 6) Piercing damage.

Animate Trees (1/Day). The treant magically awakens up to two trees within 60 feet. Each animated tree uses the Treant stat block with Intelligence and Charisma scores of 1, cannot speak, and lacks this action. The animated trees act immediately after the treant on the same Initiative count and obey its commands. A tree remains animated for 1 day, until it dies, until the treant dies, or until it is more than 120 feet from the treant, after which it becomes inert and takes root if possible.
Description: Treants are ancient guardians of the wilderness, towering plant creatures who defend forests with immense strength and deep-rooted wisdom. Their limbs strike like falling oaks, and they can rouse nearby trees to join them in battle. Revered by druids and feared by those who harm nature, treants embody the living will of the forest.

Name: Tree Blight
Languages: Understands Common and Druidic but can't speak
Actions:
Multiattack. The blight makes two Branch attacks and uses Grasping Root.

Branch. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 15 ft. Hit: 16 (3d6 + 6) Bludgeoning damage.

Grasping Root. Strength Saving Throw: DC 17, one Large or smaller creature the blight can see within 15 feet. Failure: The target is pulled up to 10 feet straight toward the blight and has the Grappled condition (escape DC 16) from one of six roots. Until the grapple ends, the target takes 13 (2d6 + 6) Bludgeoning damage at the start of each of its turns.
Bonus Actions: Gnash. Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 17, one creature Grappled by the blight. Failure: 19 (3d8 + 6) Piercing damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Tree Blights are huge and malevolent plant creatures that serve as twisted and corrupted guardians of the natural world, known for their powerful branch attacks, their grasping root ability, and their role as threats to forests and nature in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Triceratops
Actions:
Multiattack. The triceratops makes two Gore attacks.

Gore. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. Hit: 19 (2d12 + 6) Piercing damage. If the target is Huge or smaller and the triceratops moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 9 (2d8) Piercing damage and has the Prone condition.
Description: Triceratops are huge and powerful beasts that can be found in grassland environments, known for their gore attacks, their ability to charge at high speeds, and their role as both threats and denizens in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Troglodyte
Traits:
Stench. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12, any creature (other than a troglodyte) that starts its turn in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the troglodyte. Failure: The target is Poisoned until the start of its next turn. Success: The target is immune to all troglodyte Stench for 1 hour.

Sunlight Sensitivity. While in sunlight, the troglodyte has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.
Actions: Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Slashing damage.
Description: Troglodytes are medium-sized and foul-smelling monstrosities that can be found in dark and subterranean environments, known for their stench and sunlight sensitivity, their rend attacks, and their role as both threats and denizens in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Troll
Traits:
Loathsome Limbs (4/Day). If the troll ends any turn Bloodied and took 15+ Slashing damage during that turn, one of the troll's limbs is severed, falls into the troll's space, and becomes a Troll Limb. The limb acts immediately after the troll's turn. The troll has 1 Exhaustion level for each missing limb, and it grows replacement limbs the next time it regains Hit Points.

Regeneration. The troll regains 15 Hit Points at the start of each of its turns. If the troll takes Acid or Fire damage, this trait doesn't function on the troll's next turn. The troll dies only if it starts its turn with 0 Hit Points and doesn't regenerate.
Actions:
Multiattack. The troll makes three Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Slashing damage.
Bonus Actions: Charge. The troll moves up to half its Speed straight toward an enemy it can see.
Environment: Arctic, Forest, Hill, Mountain, Swamp, Underdark
Description: Trolls are large and regenerating giants that can be found in a wide range of environments, known for their loathsome limbs and regeneration abilities, their rend attacks, and their role as both threats and denizens in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Troll Limb
Traits:
Regeneration. The limb regains 5 Hit Points at the start of each of its turns. If the limb takes Acid or Fire damage, this trait doesn't function on the limb's next turn. The limb dies only if it starts its turn with 0 Hit Points and doesn't regenerate.

Troll Spawn. The limb uncannily has the same senses as a whole troll. If the limb isn't destroyed within 24 hours, roll 1d12. On a 12, the limb turns into a Troll. Otherwise, the limb withers away.
Actions: Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (2d4 + 4) Slashing damage.
Environment: Arctic, Forest, Hill, Mountain, Swamp, Underdark
Description: Troll Limbs are small and regenerating giant limbs that can be found in a wide range of environments, known for their regeneration and troll spawn abilities, their rend attacks, and their role as both threats and denizens in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Twig Blight
Traits: Pack Tactics. The blight has Advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of its allies is within 5 feet of the target and that ally is not Incapacitated.
Actions: Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Slashing damage.
Description: Twig blights are small, malicious plant creatures formed from twisted roots and brittle branches. They lurk among underbrush and deadwood, striking in swarms to overwhelm intruders. Though individually weak, their coordinated attacks and ability to blend into forest terrain make them dangerous ambushers in the wild.

Name: Tyrannosaurus Rex
Actions:
Multiattack. The tyrannosaurus makes one Bite attack and one Tail attack.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 10 ft. Hit: 33 (4d12 + 7) Piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 17). While Grappled, the target has the Restrained condition and can't be targeted by the tyrannosaurus's Tail.

Tail. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 15 ft. Hit: 25 (4d8 + 7) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.
Description: Tyrannosaurus Rexes are huge and powerful beasts that can be found in grassland environments, known for their bite and tail attacks, their ability to grapple prey, and their role as both threats and denizens in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Ultharoth Fiend
Traits:
Fiendish Restoration. If the ultharoth fiend dies outside Gehenna, its form collapses into corrosive ichor, and it instantly reforms elsewhere in Gehenna with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The ultharoth fiend has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The ultharoth fiend uses Hypnotic Gaze and makes two Mercurial Whip attacks.

Mercurial Whip. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 15 ft. Hit: 25 (6d6 + 4) Force damage, and the ultharoth fiend teleports the target up to 10 feet to an unoccupied space it can see that is not in the air.

Hypnotic Gaze. Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 17, each creature in a 30‑foot Cone. Failure: 10 (3d6) Psychic damage, and the creature is Stunned until the start of the ultharoth fiend’s next turn. Success: The creature becomes immune to this ultharoth fiend’s Hypnotic Gaze for 24 hours.

Spellcasting. The ultharoth fiend casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Intelligence as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17): At will: Alter Self, Clairvoyance, Detect Magic; 1/day each: Dimension Door, Fireball (5th‑level), Wall of Fire
Bonus Actions: Fiendish Guile (Recharge 4–6). The ultharoth fiend casts Dispel Magic, Invisibility (self only), Misty Step, or Suggestion, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
