Description: Ultharoth fiends are elite yugoloth strategists who command battles through manipulation and precision. Their shifting whips distort space, dragging foes into vulnerable positions, while their hypnotic gaze can halt entire squads in an instant. Gifted with potent spellcraft and ruthless cunning, they serve as mercenary generals in planar wars, shaping conflicts with cold, calculated efficiency.

Name: Burrowmaw Ravager
Senses: Darkvision 120 ft., Tremorsense 60 ft., passive Perception 10
Traits: Stone Ripper. The burrowmaw ravager can tunnel through solid rock at half its Burrow Speed, leaving behind a 10-foot-diameter tunnel.
Actions:
Multiattack. The burrowmaw ravager makes three Rending Claw attacks.

Rending Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 10 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Slashing damage.
Bonus Actions:
Disorienting Glare (Recharge 5-6). Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 14, each creature in a 30-foot Cone. Failure: The target can't take Reactions until the start of the ravager's next turn, and the target rolls 1d8 to determine its behavior on its next turn:

1-4. The target takes no actions.

5-6. The target takes no action or Bonus Action and uses all its movement to move in a random direction.

7-8. The target makes a melee attack against a random creature within reach or does nothing if no such creature is present.
Description: Burrowmaw Ravagers are large and subterranean monstrosities that can be found in the underdark, known for their stone ripper ability, their rending claw attacks, and their role as both threats and denizens in the ecosystems they call home.

Name: Undead Spirit
HP: 30 (Ghostly and Putrid only) or 20 (Skeletal only) + 10 for each spell level above 3
CR: None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)
Traits:
Festering Aura (Putrid Only). Constitution Saving Throw: DC equals your spell save DC, any creature (other than you) that starts its turn within a 5-foot Emanation originating from the spirit. Failure: The creature has the Poisoned condition until the start of its next turn.

Incorporeal Passage (Ghostly Only). The spirit can move through other creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain. If it ends its turn inside an object, it is shunted to the nearest unoccupied space and takes 1d10 Force damage for every 5 feet traveled.
Actions:
Multiattack. The spirit makes a number of attacks equal to half this spell's level (round down).

Deathly Touch (Ghostly Only). Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d8 + 3 + the spell's level Necrotic damage, and the target has the Frightened condition until the end of its next turn.

Grave Bolt (Skeletal Only). Ranged Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, range 150 ft. Hit: 2d4 + 3 + the spell's level Necrotic damage.

Rotting Claw (Putrid Only). Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d6 + 3 + the spell's level Slashing damage. If the target has the Poisoned condition, it has the Paralyzed condition until the end of its next turn.
Description: Undead Spirits are medium-sized and spectral undead that can be summoned through necromantic magic, known for their festering aura and incorporeal passage abilities, their deathly touch, grave bolt, and rotting claw attacks, and their role as both threats and tools in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Unicorn
Traits:
Legendary Resistance (3/Day). If the unicorn fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Magic Resistance. The unicorn has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The unicorn makes one Hooves attack and one Radiant Horn attack.

Hooves. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage.

Radiant Horn. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) Radiant damage.

Spellcasting. The unicorn casts one of the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14): At will: Detect Evil and Good, Druidcraft; 1/day each: Calm Emotions, Dispel Evil and Good, Entangle, Pass without Trace, Word of Recall
Bonus Actions: Unicorn's Blessing (3/Day). The unicorn touches another creature with its horn and casts Cure Wounds or Lesser Restoration on that creature, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Legendary Actions:
Charging Horn. The unicorn moves up to half its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks, and it makes one Radiant Horn attack.

Shimmering Shield. The unicorn targets itself or one creature it can see within 60 feet of itself. The target gains 10 (3d6) Temporary Hit Points, and its AC increases by 2 until the end of the unicorn's next turn. The unicorn can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.
Regional Effects:
The region containing a unicorn's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:

Obscuring Foliage. The unicorn and its allies have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks while within 1 mile of the lair.

Positive Energy. Whenever a creature within 1 mile of the lair regains Hit Points from a spell, it regains the maximum number of Hit Points possible. Additionally, the effects of curses are suppressed within 1 mile of the lair.

If the unicorn dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.
Description: Unicorns are large and majestic celestial creatures that can be found in forest and feywild environments, known for their legendary resistance and magic resistance abilities, their hooves and radiant horn attacks, their spellcasting capabilities, and their role as both protectors and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Vampire
Traits:
Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the vampire fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Misty Escape. If the vampire drops to 0 Hit Points outside its resting place, the vampire uses Shape-Shift to become mist (no action required). If it can't use Shape-Shift, it is destroyed.

While it has 0 Hit Points in mist form, it can't return to its vampire form, and it must reach its resting place within 2 hours or be destroyed. Once in its resting place, it returns to its vampire form and has the Paralyzed condition until it regains any Hit Points, and it regains 1 Hit Point after spending 1 hour there.

Spider Climb. The vampire can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

Vampire Weakness. The vampire has these weaknesses:

Forbiddance. The vampire can't enter a residence without an invitation from an occupant.

Running Water. The vampire takes 20 Acid damage if it ends its turn in running water.

Stake to the Heart. If a weapon that deals Piercing damage is driven into the vampire's heart while the vampire has the Incapacitated condition in its resting place, the vampire has the Paralyzed condition until the weapon is removed.

Sunlight. The vampire takes 20 Radiant damage if it starts its turn in sunlight. While in sunlight, it has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.
Actions:
Multiattack (Vampire Form Only). The vampire makes two Grave Strike attacks and uses Bite.

Grave Strike (Vampire Form Only). Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) Necrotic damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 14) from one of two hands.

Bite (Bat or Vampire Form Only). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 17, one creature within 5 feet that is willing or that has the Grappled, Incapacitated, or Restrained condition. Failure: 6 (1d4 + 4) Piercing damage plus 13 (3d8) Necrotic damage. The target's Hit Point maximum decreases by an amount equal to the Necrotic damage taken, and the vampire regains Hit Points equal to that amount. A Humanoid reduced to 0 Hit Points by this damage and then buried rises the following sunset as a Vampire Spawn under the vampire's control.
Bonus Actions:
Charm (Recharge 5-6). The vampire casts Charm Person, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17), and the duration is 24 hours. The Charmed target is a willing recipient of the vampire's Bite, the damage of which doesn't end the spell. When the spell ends, the target is unaware it was Charmed by the vampire.

Shape-Shift. If the vampire isn't in sunlight or running water, it shape-shifts into a Tiny bat (Speed 5 ft., Fly Speed 30 ft.) or a Medium cloud of mist (Speed 5 ft., Fly Speed 20 ft. [hover]), or it returns to its vampire form. Anything it is wearing transforms with it.

While in bat form, the vampire can't speak. Its game statistics, other than its size and Speed, are unchanged.

While in mist form, the vampire can't take any actions, speak, or manipulate objects. It is weightless and can enter an enemy's space and stop there. If air can pass through a space, the mist can do so, but it can't pass through liquid. It has Resistance to all damage, except the damage it takes from sunlight.
Legendary Actions:
Beguile. The vampire casts Command, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17). The vampire can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Deathless Strike. The vampire moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Grave Strike attack.
Regional Effects:
The region containing a vampire's lair is warped by its presence, creating the following effects:

Children of the Night. The vampire exerts influence over the animals in its domain. From dusk until dawn, Medium or smaller Beasts have the Charmed condition while within 1 mile of the lair.

Looming Shadows. Shadows within 1 mile of the lair seem to move as if alive. Any creature (excluding the vampire and its allies) that finishes a Short Rest while within 1 mile of the lair must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or gain no benefit from that rest.

Mists. The area within 1 mile of the lair is Lightly Obscured by a persistent, creeping fog. The vampire and any creatures of its choice are unaffected by the fog.

If the vampire dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.
Description: Vampires are small or medium-sized and charismatic undead that can be found in the underdark and urban environments, known for their legendary resistance, misty escape, spider climb, and vampire weakness abilities, their grave strike and bite attacks, their charm and shape-shift bonus actions, their beguile and deathless strike legendary actions, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Vampire Familiar
Traits: Vampiric Connection. While the familiar and its vampire master are on the same plane of existence, the vampire can communicate with the familiar telepathically, and the vampire can perceive through the familiar's senses.
Actions:
Multiattack. The familiar makes two Umbral Dagger attacks.

Umbral Dagger. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Piercing damage plus 7 (3d4) Necrotic damage. If the target is reduced to 0 Hit Points by this attack, the target becomes Stable but has the Poisoned condition for 1 hour. While it has the Poisoned condition, the target has the Paralyzed condition.
Bonus Actions: Deathless Agility. The familiar takes the Dash or Disengage action.
Description: Vampire Familiars are small or medium-sized and cunning humanoids that can be found in the underdark and urban environments, known for their vampiric connection ability, their umbral dagger attacks, their deathless agility bonus action, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Vampire Nightbringer
Traits: Sunlight Hypersensitivity. The vampire takes 10 Radiant damage if it starts its turn in sunlight. While in sunlight, it has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.
Actions:
Multiattack. The vampire makes one Bite attack and one Shadow Strike attack.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) Piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) Necrotic damage. The target's Hit Point maximum decreases by an amount equal to the Necrotic damage taken, and the vampire regains Hit Points equal to that amount.

Shadow Strike. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) Slashing damage plus 14 (4d6) Cold damage.
Bonus Actions: Shadow Stealth. While in Dim Light or Darkness, the vampire takes the Hide action.
Description: Vampire Nightbringers are small or medium-sized and shadowy undead that can be found in the underdark and urban environments, known for their sunlight hypersensitivity ability, their bite and shadow strike attacks, their shadow stealth bonus action, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Vampire Spawn
Traits:
Spider Climb. The vampire can climb difficult surfaces, including ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

Vampire Weakness. The vampire suffers the following limitations:

Forbiddance. It cannot enter a residence without an invitation from someone inside.

Running Water. It takes 20 Acid damage if it ends its turn in running water.

Stake to the Heart. If a Piercing weapon is driven into its heart while it is Incapacitated, the vampire is destroyed.

Sunlight. The vampire takes 20 Radiant damage at the start of its turn in sunlight and has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks while exposed.
Actions:
Multiattack. The vampire makes two Claw attacks and uses Bite.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) Slashing damage. A Medium or smaller target becomes Grappled (escape DC 13) by one of the vampire’s claws.

Bite. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 14, one willing creature or one that is Grappled, Incapacitated, or Restrained. Failure: 5 (1d4 + 3) Piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) Necrotic damage. The target’s Hit Point maximum is reduced by the Necrotic damage taken, and the vampire regains that many Hit Points.
Bonus Actions: Deathless Agility. The vampire takes the Dash or Disengage action.
Description: Vampire spawn are lesser undead servants bound to their creators, driven by hunger and instinct. They stalk dark alleys and subterranean tunnels, using their climbing prowess and predatory speed to corner victims. Though not as powerful as true vampires, their bite drains life and vitality, making them dangerous foes in close quarters.

Name: Vampire Umbral Lord
Traits:
Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair). If the vampire fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Shadow Escape. When reduced to 0 Hit Points outside its resting place, the vampire teleports to its resting place unless it is in running water or sunlight. If unable to teleport, it is destroyed. Once inside its resting place, it is Paralyzed for 1 hour, then regains 1 Hit Point.

Vampire Weakness. The vampire suffers the following limitations:

Forbiddance. It cannot enter a residence without an invitation.

Running Water. It takes 20 Acid damage if it ends its turn in running water.

Stake to the Heart. If a Piercing weapon is driven into its heart while it is Incapacitated in its resting place, the vampire becomes Paralyzed until the weapon is removed.

Sunlight. The vampire takes 20 Radiant damage at the start of its turn in sunlight and has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks while exposed.
Actions:
Multiattack. The vampire makes two attacks, choosing Grave Strike or Sickening Ray.

Grave Strike. Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (1d8 + 5) Slashing damage plus 13 (3d8) Necrotic damage.

Hunger of Hadar (Recharge 5–6). The vampire casts Hunger of Hadar (5th‑level), requiring no components and using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 18).

Sickening Ray. Ranged Attack Roll: +10, range 120 ft. Hit: 16 (2d10 + 5) Necrotic damage, and the target is Poisoned until the start of the vampire’s next turn.
Bonus Actions: Sanguine Drain. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 18, one creature within 30 feet that is not a Construct or Undead. Failure: 14 (4d6) Necrotic damage. The target’s Hit Point maximum is reduced by the damage taken, and the vampire regains that many Hit Points.
Legendary Actions:
Beguile. The vampire casts Command without components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 18). It cannot use this action again until the start of its next turn.

Umbral Strike. The vampire moves up to half its Speed and makes one Grave Strike or Sickening Ray attack.
Regional Effects:
A vampire’s lair twists the surrounding region:

Children of the Night. From dusk to dawn, Medium or smaller Beasts within 1 mile of the lair are Charmed by the vampire.

Looming Shadows. Shadows behave unnaturally within 1 mile. A creature (other than the vampire or its allies) that finishes a Short Rest in the area must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or gain no benefit from the rest.

Mists. A creeping fog lightly obscures the area within 1 mile. The vampire and creatures it chooses ignore the obscuration.

These effects end immediately if the vampire dies or relocates its lair.
Description: Vampire Umbral Lords are powerful undead sovereigns who command darkness itself. Their mastery of necrotic magic, shadowy movement, and soul‑draining abilities makes them terrifying rulers of hidden domains. They manipulate fear, fog, and living shadows to isolate prey before striking with lethal precision.

Name: Venomous Snake
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) Piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) Poison damage.
Environment: Coastal, Desert, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Swamp
Description: Venomous Snakes are tiny and slithering beasts that can be found in coastal, desert, forest, grassland, hill, and swamp environments, known for their bite attack that deals piercing and poison damage, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Vine Blight
Actions:
Constricting Vine. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 10 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 12). Until the grapple ends, the target takes 4 (1d8) Bludgeoning damage at the start of each of its turns, and the blight can't make Constricting Vine attacks.

Entangling Plants (Recharge 5-6). The blight casts the Entangle spell, using Constitution as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12).
Description: Vine Blights are medium-sized and creeping plants that can be found in forest environments, known for their constricting vine attack that grapples and damages targets, their entangling plants action that casts the entangle spell, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Violet Fungus
Actions:
Multiattack. The fungus makes two Rotting Touch attacks.

Rotting Touch. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 10 ft. Hit: 4 (1d8) Necrotic damage.
Description: Violet Fungi are medium-sized and fungal plants that can be found in underdark environments, known for their rotting touch attack that deals necrotic damage, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Violet Fungus Necrohulk
Actions:
Multiattack. The necrohulk makes two Rotting Slam attacks.

Rotting Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) Necrotic damage.

Spore Bomb (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 15, each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point the necrohulk can see within 60 feet. Failure: 28 (8d6) Necrotic damage, and the target has the Poisoned condition until the start of the necrohulk's next turn. While Poisoned, the target can't regain Hit Points. Success: Half damage only.
Bonus Actions:
Absorb Body. Strength Saving Throw: DC 15, one Medium or Small creature the necrohulk can see within 5 feet. Failure: The target is pulled into the necrohulk's space and becomes grafted to its body. The necrohulk can have only one target grafted at a time.

While grafted, the target has the Restrained condition and Disadvantage on Constitution saving throws. When the necrohulk moves, the grafted target moves with it. If the target dies while grafted, its body is destroyed, and the necrohulk regains 10 Hit Points.

The grafted target or a creature within 5 feet of the necrohulk can take an action to make a DC 15 Strength (Athletics) check. On a successful check, the target is no longer grafted and moves to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the necrohulk.
Description: Violet Fungus Necrohulks are large and hulking plant creatures that can be found in underdark environments, known for their rotting slam and spore bomb attacks, their absorb body bonus action, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Vroc
Traits:
Demonic Restoration. If the Vroc dies outside the Abyss, its form collapses into foul ichor, and it instantly reforms elsewhere in the Abyss with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The Vroc has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
Actions:
Multiattack. The Vroc makes two Shred attacks.

Shred. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) Poison damage.

Spores (Recharge 6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 15, each creature in a 20‑foot Emanation centered on the Vroc. Failure: The creature becomes Poisoned and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. While Poisoned this way, the creature takes 5 (1d10) Poison damage at the start of each of its turns. Pouring a flask of Holy Water on the creature ends the effect early.

Stunning Screech (1/Day). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 15, each creature in a 20‑foot Emanation centered on the Vroc (demons automatically succeed). Failure: 10 (3d6) Thunder damage, and the creature is Stunned until the end of the Vroc’s next turn.
Description: Vrocs are savage, vulture‑like demons that haunt the skies of the Abyss. Their razor talons tear flesh with ease, while clouds of toxic spores choke and weaken their prey. Their piercing screeches can stun entire groups, making them terrifying shock troops in demonic hordes. Driven by hunger and cruelty, they descend upon victims with relentless ferocity.

Name: Vulture
Traits: Pack Tactics. The vulture has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the vulture's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions: Beak. Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 2 (1d4) Piercing damage.
Description: Vultures are medium-sized and scavenging beasts that can be found in desert, grassland, and hill environments, known for their pack tactics trait, their beak attack, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Warhorse
Actions: Hooves. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (2d4 + 4) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature and the horse moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 5 (2d4) Bludgeoning damage and has the Prone condition.
Description: Warhorses are large and powerful beasts that can be found in urban environments, known for their hooves attack that deals bludgeoning damage and can knock targets prone, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Warhorse Skeleton
Actions: Hooves. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature and the skeleton moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target has the Prone condition.
Description: Warhorse Skeletons are large and undead creatures that can be found in the shadowfell, underdark, and urban environments, known for their hooves attack that deals bludgeoning damage and can knock targets prone, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Warrior Commander
Actions:
Multiattack. The warrior makes three attacks, using Greatsword or Longbow in any combination.

Greatsword. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 5 ft. Hit: 19 (4d6 + 5) Slashing damage. The warrior also creates one of the following effects:

Sap. The target has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of the warrior's next turn.

Maneuver. One ally who can see or hear the warrior can take a Reaction to move up to half the ally's Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

Longbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +9, range 150/600 ft. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) Piercing damage, and the target's Speed decreases by 10 feet until the end of the target's next turn.
Bonus Actions: Tactical Charge. The warrior moves up to half its Speed straight toward an enemy it can see without provoking Opportunity Attacks.
Reactions: Counterattack. Trigger: The warrior is hit by an attack roll. Response: The warrior adds 4 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss. On a miss, the warrior can make one Greatsword or Longbow attack against the attacker.
Description: Warrior Commanders are small or medium-sized and commanding humanoids that can be found in any environment, known for their multiattack action that allows them to create sap and maneuver effects, their tactical charge bonus action, their counterattack reaction, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Warrior Infantry
Traits: Pack Tactics. The warrior has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the warrior's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions: Spear. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 4 (1d6 + 1) Piercing damage.
Description: Warrior Infantry are small or medium-sized and disciplined humanoids that can be found in any environment, known for their pack tactics trait, their spear attack that can be used in melee or ranged combat, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Warrior Veteran
Actions:
Multiattack. The warrior makes two Greatsword or Heavy Crossbow attacks.

Greatsword. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Slashing damage.

Heavy Crossbow. Ranged Attack Roll: +3, range 100/400 ft. Hit: 12 (2d10 + 1) Piercing damage.
Reactions: Parry. Trigger: The warrior is hit by a melee attack roll while holding a weapon. Response: The warrior adds 2 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.
Description: Warrior Veterans are small or medium-sized and experienced humanoids that can be found in any environment, known for their multiattack action, their parry reaction, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Water Elemental
Condition Immunities: exhaustion, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, unconscious
Traits:
Freeze. If the elemental takes Cold damage, its Speed decreases by 20 feet until the end of its next turn.

Water Form. The elemental can enter an enemy's space and stop there. It can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.
Actions:
Multiattack. The elemental makes two Slam attacks.

Slam. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

Whelm (Recharge 4-6). Strength Saving Throw: DC 15, each creature in the elemental's space. Failure: 22 (4d8 + 4) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 14). Until the grapple ends, the target has the Restrained condition, is suffocating unless it can breathe water, and takes 9 (2d8) Bludgeoning damage at the start of each of the elemental's turns. The elemental can grapple one Large creature or up to two Medium or smaller creatures at a time with Whelm. As an action, a creature within 5 feet of the elemental can pull a creature out of it by succeeding on a DC 14 Strength (Athletics) check. Success: Half damage only.
Environment: Coastal, Planar (Elemental Plane of Water), Swamp, Underwater
Description: Water Elementals are large and fluid creatures that can be found in coastal, elemental plane of water, swamp, and underwater environments, known for their freeze and water form traits, their slam and whelm attacks, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Water Weird
Condition Immunities: exhaustion, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, unconscious
Traits:
Invisible in Water. The water weird has the Invisible condition while fully immersed in water.

Water Bound. The water weird dies if it leaves the water to which it is bound or if that water is destroyed.
Actions: Surge. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 10 ft. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) Cold damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 13), and it has the Restrained condition until the grapple ends.
Description: Water Weirds are large and elusive elemental creatures that can be found in underdark and urban environments, known for their invisible in water and water bound traits, their surge attack that deals cold damage and can grapple targets, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Weasel
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1 Piercing damage.
Description: Weasels are tiny and agile beasts that can be found in forest, grassland, and hill environments, known for their bite attack, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Werebear
Speed: 30 ft., 40 ft. (bear form only), Climb 30 ft. (bear form only)
Actions:
Multiattack. The werebear makes two attacks, using Handaxe or Rend in any combination. It can replace one attack with a Bite attack.

Bite (Bear or Hybrid Form Only). Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 17 (2d12 + 4) Piercing damage. If the target is a Humanoid, it is subjected to the following effect. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 14. Failure: The target is cursed. If the cursed target drops to 0 Hit Points, it instead becomes a Werebear under the DM's control and has 10 Hit Points. Success: The target is immune to this werebear's curse for 24 hours.

Handaxe (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only). Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft or range 20/60 ft. Hit: 14 (3d6 + 4) Slashing damage.

Rend (Bear or Hybrid Form Only). Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Slashing damage.
Bonus Actions: Shape-Shift. The werebear shape-shifts into a Large bear-humanoid hybrid form or a Large bear, or it returns to its true humanoid form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.
Description: Werebears are small or medium-sized and powerful humanoids that can be found in arctic, forest, and hill environments, known for their shape-shift bonus action that allows them to transform into bear forms, their multiattack action that allows them to use handaxe and rend attacks, their bite attack that can curse targets, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Wereboar
Actions:
Multiattack. The wereboar makes two attacks, using Javelin or Tusk in any combination. It can replace one attack with a Gore attack.

Gore (Boar or Hybrid Form Only). Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is a Humanoid, it is subjected to the following effect. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12. Failure: The target is cursed. If the cursed target drops to 0 Hit Points, it instead becomes a Wereboar under the DM's control and has 10 Hit Points. Success: The target is immune to this wereboar's curse for 24 hours.

Javelin (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only). Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) Piercing damage.

Tusk (Boar or Hybrid Form Only). Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature and the wereboar moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 7 (2d6) Piercing damage and has the Prone condition.
Bonus Actions: Shape-Shift. The wereboar shape-shifts into a Medium boar-humanoid hybrid or a Small boar, or it returns to its true humanoid form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.
Description: Wereboars are small or medium-sized and aggressive humanoids that can be found in forest, grassland, and hill environments, known for their shape-shift bonus action that allows them to transform into boar forms, their multiattack action that allows them to use javelin and tusk attacks, their gore attack that can curse targets, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Wererat
Actions:
Multiattack. The wererat makes two attacks, using Scratch or Hand Crossbow in any combination. It can replace one attack with a Bite attack.

Bite (Rat or Hybrid Form Only). Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is a Humanoid, it is subjected to the following effect. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 11. Failure: The target is cursed. If the cursed target drops to 0 Hit Points, it instead becomes a Wererat under the DM's control and has 10 Hit Points. Success: The target is immune to this wererat's curse for 24 hours.

Scratch. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Slashing damage.

Hand Crossbow (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only). Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 30/120 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Piercing damage.
Bonus Actions: Shape-Shift. The wererat shape-shifts into a Medium rat-humanoid hybrid or a Small rat, or it returns to its true humanoid form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.
Description: Wererats are small or medium-sized and cunning humanoids that can be found in forest and urban environments, known for their shape-shift bonus action that allows them to transform into rat forms, their multiattack action that allows them to use scratch and hand crossbow attacks, their bite attack that can curse targets, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Weretiger
Actions:
Multiattack. The weretiger makes two attacks, using Scratch or Longbow in any combination. It can replace one attack with a Bite attack.

Bite (Tiger or Hybrid Form Only). Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is a Humanoid, it is subjected to the following effect. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 13. Failure: The target is cursed. If the cursed target drops to 0 Hit Points, it instead becomes a Weretiger under the DM's control and has 10 Hit Points. Success: The target is immune to this weretiger's curse for 24 hours.

Scratch. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Slashing damage.

Longbow (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only). Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 150/600 ft. Hit: 11 (2d8 + 2) Piercing damage.
Bonus Actions:
Prowl (Tiger or Hybrid Form Only). The weretiger moves up to its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks. At the end of this movement, the weretiger can take the Hide action.

Shape-Shift. The weretiger shape-shifts into a Large tiger-humanoid hybrid or a Large tiger, or it returns to its true humanoid form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.
Description: Weretigers are small or medium-sized and stealthy humanoids that can be found in desert, forest, and grassland environments, known for their prowl and shape-shift bonus actions that allow them to move stealthily and transform into tiger forms, their multiattack action that allows them to use scratch and longbow attacks, their bite attack that can curse targets, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Werewolf
Traits: Pack Tactics. The werewolf has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the werewolf's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions:
Multiattack. The werewolf makes two attacks, using Scratch or Longbow in any combination. It can replace one attack with a Bite attack.

Bite (Wolf or Hybrid Form Only). Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is a Humanoid, it is subjected to the following effect. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12. Failure: The target is cursed. If the cursed target drops to 0 Hit Points, it instead becomes a Werewolf under the DM's control and has 10 Hit Points. Success: The target is immune to this werewolf's curse for 24 hours.

Scratch. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Slashing damage.

Longbow (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only). Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 150/600 ft. Hit: 11 (2d8 + 2) Piercing damage.
Bonus Actions: Shape-Shift. The werewolf shape-shifts into a Large wolf-humanoid hybrid or a Medium wolf, or it returns to its true humanoid form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.
Description: Werewolves are small or medium-sized and ferocious humanoids that can be found in forest and hill environments, known for their pack tactics trait, their shape-shift bonus action that allows them to transform into wolf forms, their multiattack action that allows them to use scratch and longbow attacks, their bite attack that can curse targets, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: White Dragon Wyrmling
Speed: 30 ft., Burrow 15 ft., Fly 60 ft., Swim 30 ft.
Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 14
Traits: Ice Walk. The dragon can move across and climb icy surfaces without needing to make an ability check. Additionally, Difficult Terrain composed of ice or snow doesn't cost it extra movement.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes two Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Slashing damage plus 2 (1d4) Cold damage.

Cold Breath (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: 22 (5d8) Cold damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: White Dragon Wyrmlings are medium and icy dragons that can be found in arctic environments, known for their ice walk trait, their multiattack action that allows them to use rend attacks, their cold breath attack that can deal cold damage in a cone, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Wight
Traits:
Sunlight Sensitivity. While in sunlight, the wight has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.

Gravebound Will. The wight is animated by a fragment of its former determination, giving it an unnatural resilience and a predatory instinct for living souls.
Actions:
Multiattack. The wight makes two attacks, using Necrotic Sword or Necrotic Bow in any combination. It can replace one attack with Life Drain.

Necrotic Sword. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) Necrotic damage.

Necrotic Bow. Ranged Attack Roll: +4, range 150/600 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) Necrotic damage.

Life Drain. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 13, one creature within 5 feet. Failure: 6 (1d8 + 2) Necrotic damage, and the target’s Hit Point maximum is reduced by the same amount.

A Humanoid slain by this attack rises 24 hours later as a Zombie under the wight’s control unless restored to life or its body is destroyed. The wight can control up to twelve zombies at a time.
Description: Wights are medium and malevolent undead that can be found in desert, shadowfell, swamp, underdark, and urban environments, known for their sunlight sensitivity and gravebound will traits, their multiattack action that allows them to use necrotic sword and necrotic bow attacks, their life drain attack that can reduce a target's hit point maximum and raise slain humanoids as zombies under their control, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Will-o'-Wisp
Damage Resistances: acid, bludgeoning, cold, fire, necrotic, piercing, slashing
Condition Immunities: exhaustion, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, unconscious
Traits:
Ephemeral. The wisp can't wear or carry anything.

Illumination. The wisp sheds Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet.

Incorporeal Movement. The wisp drifts through creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain, taking 5 (1d10) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

Lurelight Essence. The wisp’s glow subtly shifts in color and intensity, instinctively mimicking comforting or familiar lights to draw creatures closer.
Actions: Shock. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d8 + 2) Lightning damage.
Bonus Actions:
Consume Life. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 10, one living creature the wisp can see within 5 feet that has 0 Hit Points. Failure: The target dies, and the wisp regains 10 (3d6) Hit Points.

Vanish. The wisp and its light become Invisible until its Concentration ends. The effect ends immediately after the wisp makes an attack roll or uses Consume Life.
Description: Will-o'-Wisps are tiny and eerie undead that can be found in forest, swamp, and urban environments, known for their ephemeral, illumination, incorporeal movement, and lurelight essence traits, their shock attack that deals lightning damage, their consume life bonus action that can kill a target with 0 hit points and heal the wisp, their vanish bonus action that allows them to become invisible, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Winged Kobold
Traits:
Pack Tactics. The kobold has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the kobold's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

Sunlight Sensitivity. While in sunlight, the kobold has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.
Actions:
Dragon-Tooth Blade. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Piercing damage.

Chromatic Spittle. Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 30 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) damage of a type chosen by the kobold: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison.
Environment: Arctic, Coastal, Desert, Forest, Hill, Mountain, Swamp, Underdark, Urban
Description: Winged Kobolds are small and agile dragonkin that can be found in a wide variety of environments, known for their pack tactics and sunlight sensitivity traits, their dragon-tooth blade melee attack, their chromatic spittle ranged attack that can deal various types of damage, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Winter Wolf
Traits: Pack Tactics. The wolf has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the wolf's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions:
Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

Cold Breath (Recharge 5-6). Constitution Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 15-foot Cone. Failure: 18 (4d8) Cold damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Winter Wolves are large and fierce monstrosities that can be found in arctic environments, known for their pack tactics trait, their bite attack that can knock targets prone, their cold breath attack that can deal cold damage in a cone, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Wolf
Traits: Pack Tactics. The wolf has Advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the wolf's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.
Description: Wolves are medium and wild beasts that can be found in forest, grassland, and hill environments, known for their pack tactics trait, their bite attack that can knock targets prone, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Worg
Actions: Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Piercing damage, and the next attack roll made against the target before the start of the worg's next turn has Advantage.
Description: Worgs are large and cunning fey that can be found in forest, grassland, hill, and feywild environments, known for their bite attack that can grant advantage to the next attack against the target, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Wraith
Damage Resistances: acid, bludgeoning, cold, fire, piercing, slashing
Condition Immunities: charmed, exhaustion, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, unconscious
Traits:
Incorporeal Movement. The wraith drifts through creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain, taking 5 (1d10) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

Sunlight Sensitivity. While in sunlight, the wraith has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.

Echo of Despair. The wraith’s presence subtly chills the air and dims nearby light, a lingering effect of the anguish that birthed it.
Actions:
Life Drain. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 21 (4d8 + 3) Necrotic damage. The target’s Hit Point maximum is reduced by the same amount.

Create Specter. The wraith targets a Humanoid corpse within 10 feet that has been dead for no longer than 1 minute. The spirit rises as a Specter under the wraith’s control. The wraith can control up to seven specters at a time.
Description: Wraiths are small or medium and malevolent undead that can be found in shadowfell and underdark environments, known for their incorporeal movement and sunlight sensitivity traits, their life drain attack that can reduce a target's hit point maximum, their create specter action that can raise humanoid corpses as specters under their control, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Wyvern
Actions:
Multiattack. The wyvern makes one Bite attack and one Sting attack.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) Piercing damage.

Sting. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Piercing damage plus 24 (7d6) Poison damage, and the target has the Poisoned condition until the start of the wyvern's next turn.
Description: Wyverns are large and fierce dragons that can be found in hill and mountain environments, known for their multiattack action that allows them to use bite and sting attacks, their sting attack that can deal poison damage and poison targets, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Stonefeaster Sentinel
Senses: Darkvision 60 ft., Tremorsense 60 ft., passive Perception 16
Traits:
Earth Meld. The stonefeaster sentinel can glide through nonmagical, unworked earth and stone without disturbing the material.

Gem Sense. The sentinel can pinpoint the location of precious metals and gemstones within 60 feet of itself.
Actions:
Multiattack. The sentinel makes one Bite attack and three Claw attacks.

Bite. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 17 (4d6 + 3) Piercing damage.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) Slashing damage.
Bonus Actions: Burrow Charge. The sentinel moves up to its Speed or Burrow Speed in a straight line toward a creature it can sense.
Description: Stonefeaster Sentinels are medium and resilient elementals that can be found in the elemental plane of earth and underdark environments, known for their earth meld trait that allows them to glide through earth and stone, their gem sense trait that allows them to locate precious metals and gemstones, their multiattack action that allows them to use bite and claw attacks, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Yeti
Traits:
Fear of Fire. If the yeti takes Fire damage, it has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the end of its next turn.

Frostborn Predator. The yeti’s body radiates a faint chill, and its fur crackles with ice crystals formed from its own breath.
Actions:
Multiattack. The yeti can use its Chilling Gaze and makes two attacks, using Claw or Ice Throw in any combination.

Claw. Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) Slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) Cold damage.

Ice Throw. Ranged Attack Roll: +6, range 30/120 ft. Hit: 6 (1d4 + 4) Bludgeoning damage plus 2 (1d4) Cold damage.

Chilling Gaze. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 13, one creature the yeti can see within 30 feet. Failure: 5 (2d4) Cold damage, and the target is Paralyzed until the start of the yeti’s next turn unless it has Immunity to Cold. Success: The target is immune to all yeti Chilling Gazes (but not abominable yetis) for 1 hour.
Description: Yetis are large and ferocious monstrosities that can be found in arctic environments, known for their fear of fire and frostborn predator traits, their multiattack action that allows them to use claw and ice throw attacks, their chilling gaze attack that can deal cold damage and paralyze targets, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Yorcholath Demon
Traits:
Demonic Restoration. If the yorcholath demon dies outside the Abyss, its form dissolves into corrosive sludge, and it immediately reforms elsewhere in the Abyss with full Hit Points.

Magic Resistance. The yorcholath demon has Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Spider Climb. The yorcholath demon can climb vertical surfaces and ceilings without making an ability check.

Web Walker. The yorcholath demon ignores movement penalties caused by webs.
Actions:
Multiattack. The yorcholath demon makes two Caustic Lash attacks and may cast Web or Dominate Person if available.

Caustic Lash. Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +8, reach 10 ft. or range 120 ft. Hit: 25 (6d6 + 4) Acid damage.

Spellcasting. The yorcholath demon casts one of the following spells without Material components, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15):At will: Detect Thoughts, Gaseous Form (self only), Web; 1/day: Dominate Person
Bonus Actions: Shape-Shift. The yorcholath demon transforms into a Medium Humanoid or a Medium spider, or returns to its true form. Its statistics remain unchanged. Equipment does not transform.
Reactions: Toxic Escape. Trigger: The yorcholath demon is hit by an attack. Response: It halves the attack’s damage (round down) and teleports to an unoccupied space it can see within 30 feet. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 15, each creature within 5 feet of the destination. Failure: The creature becomes Poisoned until the end of its next turn. While Poisoned this way, it is also Incapacitated.
Description: Yorcholath demons are sinister shapeshifters born from the venomous depths of the Abyss. Their bodies drip with acidic ichor, and their lashes corrode flesh from afar. Masters of deception and web‑woven terrain, they slip between humanoid and spider forms to infiltrate, manipulate, and ambush their prey. Serving powerful demon lords as spies and assassins, they strike with precision and vanish in a cloud of toxic mist.

Name: Young Black Dragon
Senses: Blindsight 30 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 16
Traits: Amphibious. The dragon can breathe air and water.
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes three Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 9 (2d4 + 4) Slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) Acid damage.

Acid Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 14, each creature in a 30-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 49 (14d6) Acid damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Young Black Dragons are large and malevolent chromatic dragons that can be found in swamp environments, known for their amphibious trait that allows them to breathe air and water, their multiattack action that allows them to use rend attacks, their rend attack that can deal slashing and acid damage, their acid breath attack that can deal acid damage in a line, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Young Blue Dragon
Senses: Blindsight 30 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 19
Actions:
Multiattack. The dragon makes three Rend attacks.

Rend. Melee Attack Roll: +9, reach 10 ft. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) Slashing damage plus 5 (1d10) Lightning damage.

Lightning Breath (Recharge 5-6). Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 16, each creature in a 60-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 55 (10d10) Lightning damage. Success: Half damage.
Description: Young Blue Dragons are large and cunning chromatic dragons that can be found in coastal and desert environments, known for their multiattack action that allows them to use rend attacks, their rend attack that can deal slashing and lightning damage, their lightning breath attack that can deal lightning damage in a line, and their role as both threats and denizens in the realms they inhabit.

Name: Young Brass Dragon
Senses: Blindsight 30 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 16
Actions:
